name: Student Center
description: The Student Center mission is to enrich the Rice experience and foster a vibrant community by providing developmental opportunities, delivering excellent service and promoting collaboration through facilities and events, student run businesses and student activities.
summary: The Student Center is comprised of three functional areas: Facilities & Events, Student Activities, and Student Run Businesses.

name: Center for Career Development
description:
The Center for Career Development (CCD) offers programs, services and resources to guide you in pursuing your career goals. We equip you with the skills and confidence needed to discover your interests and navigate your career journey from college to graduation, and throughout your life.

 

SERVICES

Career Exploration: 

The CCD can help you with internships, jobs, career/major/grad school exploration, resume reviews, mock interviews, job-shadowing (externships), and more. We have a team of career counselors that specialize in helping you find and make your place in the world. Schedule an appointment with them in 12twenty! We also conduct workshops all semester on a variety of career development topics. Check out our schedule here.

 

Jobs and Internships (12twenty): 

You already have an account on 12twenty, Rice's career services and campus recruiting platform. Just log in through the SSO to schedule an appointment, register for workshops and employer events, explore career resources, and apply for jobs and internships from employers specifically interested in hiring Rice talent.

 

Employer Connections (and on campus recruiting): 

Employers want to hire Rice talent. The CCD helps you connect with employers in every career cluster, whether you want to explore job fields or network with people in your industry -- on campus or beyond the hedges. This includes a Career and Internship Expo every semester, and on campus recruiting.

 

Resources: 

Resources are available 24/7 at ccd.rice.edu for career prep, interviewing, resumes, professional attire, and more.
summary: The CCD offers programs, services and resources to guide you in pursuing your career goals. We equip you with the skills and confidence needed to discover your interests and navigate your career journey from college to grauation, and throughout your life

name: Student Center Information Desk
description:
Student Center Information Desk Building Managers provide Student Center customers with both Rice and non-Rice information and are always willing to help answer your questions. Various schedules and Rice maps are available at the information desk as well as other services below.

* Magazines and Games check out.

* Mail Services (drop off stamped mail or purchase a stamp)

* Lost & Found -- Lost items may be taken to the Information Desk. After 60 days, items left in the Lost and Found are donated to local Houston charities.

* Postage stamps and Envelopes are available for sale at the desk.

* Poster/Flyer Approvals

* Telephones-- there are phones located throughout the building for patrons' use.

* Taxis - If a car service (shuttle, taxi, etc.) is desired, the Information Desk attendant will call one for you.

* Tickets for Rice Program Council (RPC) and Passport to Houston events are sold at the Information Desk (often at a discount) with a valid student ID.
summary: The Student Center Information Desk disseminates information to all Information Desk Building Managers and Supervisors.

name: Rice Coffeehouse
description: Rice Coffeehouse is one of three student run businesses at Rice University. The other businesses are The Hoot and Rice Bikes. Student Run Businesses are educational opportunities that benefit all members of the Rice community. We are entirely managed and staffed by students, with guidance from our two wonderful advisors Caitlin and Cristy. As a Student Run Business, Rice Coffeehouse managers work together to make sure that the rent is paid, the products are served, the catering orders are filled, health code is followed, and a good time is had by all that come to Rice Coffeehouse.
summary: We are a student-run coffee shop at Rice University serving FTO coffee to the masses at student-friendly prices.

name: Center for Civic Leadership
description: The Center for Civic Leadership (CCL) seeks to foster civic leadership through integrated curricular and experiential learning opportunities that enable students to understand the complexity of social issues, their roles as active members of society, and the mechanisms for achieving equitable and inclusive communities.
summary: The Center for Civic Leadership (CCL) seeks to foster civic leadership through integrated curricular and experiential learning opportunities that enable students to understand the complexity of social issues, their roles as active members of society, and

name: Office of Student Media
description: ABOUT RICE STUDENT MEDIA At Rice University, the Office of Student Media (OSM) oversees the blanket tax student media organizations that are the undergraduate student newspaper, The Rice Thresher; the undergraduate student yearbook, The Campanile; and the undergraduate student radio station KTRU; and their online counter-parts. OSM also manages the new student guide, First Look; the off-campus housing website, Beyond the Hedges; and all other revenue raising functions for the media, such as advertising and underwriting. OSM is part of the Dean of Undergraduates division and our media organizations are part of Rice’s 200 student groups and clubs.
summary: The Office of Student Media facilitates student development within blanket tax media organizations through training, advising and mentoring students in each medium under the auspices of a designated public forum supporting the tenets of the First Amendme

name: Multicultural Affairs
description: OMA helps students understand and appreciate racial, ethnic, gender and other differences, while creating opportunities for students to challenge prejudice and expand their cultural knowledge and appreciation. OMA utilizes its programming and support systems to provide an optimum developmental environment where all members of the University community may reach their potential in an atmosphere free from harassment and bias, thereby ensuring Rice’s standing as an intellectually and culturally-vibrant community.
summary: The Office of Multicultural Affairs (OMA) strives to provide comprehensive educational, cultural and social programs that are designed to emphasize inclusiveness while promoting intercultural dialogue, awareness, and respect for diversity.

name: Office of Undergraduate Research and Inquiry
description: Every day inquiry motivates our faculty to open new frontiers of knowledge, to design novel solutions to complex problems, and to express our humanity through creative works. By providing new resources to support inquiry-based pedagogy at all levels of the curriculum, this initiative promises to more closely integrate the research and teaching missions of the university. Through sustained engagement in the inquiry process, Rice undergraduates develop the skills to ask probing questions, employ appropriate methods to collect evidence, think critically and creatively about their findings, and communicate effectively to a range of audiences.
summary: Office of Undergraduate Research and Inquiry

name: Office of the Dean of Undergraduates
description: The Office of the Dean of Undergraduates brings together all aspects of the undergraduate student experience. At Rice, we feel strongly in educating the whole student. From academics and advising, to mental and physical wellbeing, career services and extracurricular and social activities, the people in our offices are here to support every undergraduate student.
summary: The Office of the Dean of Undergraduates brings together all aspects of the undergraduate student experience.

name: The Hoot
description: As one of Rice's Student Run Businesses, The Hoot receives advising, personal and leadership development from the Student Center's two SRB Advisors, Caitlin Lindsay and Cristy Torres. The day to day functions of the business are completely lead by the management team of 8 undergraduate students. They provide catering, delivery on campus and operate from the Ambassador Kitchen space in the RMC Student Center.
summary: The Hoot is one of Rice University's three Student Run Businesses. They provide late-night hot food, snacks, and drinks to keep you energized through the night!

name: Rice Bikes
description: Rice Bikes is one of three Student Run Businesses at Rice; we are managed completely by students. Our Management Team of 5 undergraduates receives advising, leadership and personal development from our Student Center Advisors, Caitlin Lindsay and Cristy Torres. While they support us, we ensure that the business meets our mission of providing safe and affordable bikes to the Rice and Greater Houston Community. We engage with our community through programs like Bike and Beats, planned Rides, Sustainability Week, Freewheels Houston and support for Beer Bike every year. Stop by our shop, located in the H&D garage on the inner loop road and let us repair your bike, sell you a new or refurbished one or provide you with a rental to get around campus!
summary: Rice Bikes is one of three Student Run Businesses at Rice managed completely by students. The entire Rice Bikes team strive to serve the Rice and Greater Houston Communities by providing affordable repairs, rentals and refurbished bikes. Stop by today!

name: Graduate Student Association
description: The Graduate Student Association (GSA) comprises degree seeking graduate students at Rice University. The GSA’s mission is to enrich the graduate student experience by advocating for the physical and psychological wellbeing of graduate students, promoting graduate career and professional development resources, and empowering graduate student-run social and cultural programming for the Rice community.
summary: The GSA serves as the graduate student connection for advocacy, cultural and community programming, event funding, professional development resources, and communication here at Rice.

name: Rice University Student Association
description:
The Student Association is Rice University's student government with representatives from all 11 residential colleges and one of the oldest student organizations on campus founded in 1918 originally as Rice's Student Council. The Student Association advocates for undergraduate student priorities on the University, local, state, and national level. In addition to having representatives serving on the University's governing committees, the Student Association manages and distributes funding to the 300+ registered student organizations.

Sign up for our public notice emails to be informed of our meetings and keep in touch with everything going on around campus: sa.rice.edu/publicnotice
summary: The Student Association is Rice University’s student government and advocates for students on a University, local, state, and national level. In addition, the Student Association manages and distributes funding to registered student organizations.

name: Impact Rice Retreat
description: The Impact Rice Retreat is a leadership development experience for freshmen and sophomores that aims to foster relationships, facilitate skill-building, instill confidence, and empower students to engage with their passions in a significant way so that they can make a change as leaders, both inside and outside the Hedges. This is accomplished through engaging peer-led workshops that emphasize self-awareness, fostering interpersonal relationships, recognizing personal passions and values, and developing leadership confidence and vision. Through this inspirational retreat, students also build tight-knit friendships through fun-filled activities and events.
summary: The Impact Rice Retreat is a leadership development experience for freshmen and sophomores that aims to foster relationships, facilitate skill-building, instill confidence, and empower students to engage with their passions in a significant way.

name: Will Rice College
description: The college of gods and goddesses. The only college to have ever swept Beer Bike (and we did it 5 times!). Are you looking to get involved with Will Rice or just see what's happening next? We have tons of committees to join and events nearly every week, ranging from food breaks to painting classes to Favorite Professor Dinner.
summary: Will Rice College (est. 1957) is the second oldest college. Represented by the phoenix and built on Myth, Power, and Value.

name: Lovett College
description: Welcome to Edgar Odell Lovett College! Lovett is the 7th residential college in founding order and, without question, the BEST.
summary: EOL RRF

name: Sid Richardson College
description:
Sid Richardson College (also referred to as Sid, SRC, or Sid Rich) opened in 1971 as a men's residential college. Like the other residential colleges on the Rice campus, Sid Richardson College provides undergraduate residential and dining facilities, social organizations and student government, as well as faculty, alumni, and community associates. A $2 million pledge from the Sid Richardson Foundation, which was established by Texas oilman, cattleman, and philanthropist Sid W. Richardson (1891–1959), funded the construction of the college. The college became co-ed in 1987. Members of Sid Richardson College are called Sidizens.

Sid Richardson College has a wealth of traditions. Every Friday afternoon from 3:00-6:00pm, the college's main speakers, affectionately known as the stacks, blast music in what is known as Radio Free Sid (the named derived from Radio Free Europe of the Cold War era) across the campus from the 7th floor balcony. Radio Free Sid begins and ends each roughly 3-hour-long set of music with AC/DC's Back in Black, widely considered to be the college's theme song.[73][74] Other traditions include Night of Innocence (a showing of Disney movies in the commons the same night as NOD), Sid 80s (Sid's fall public party), Sid Hi-Liter (spring public party), vatoring of Sidizens who co-advise, Floor Wars (a series of competitive events between the floors), Squid Richardson Day, and more.
summary: Welcome to Sid Richardson College, the tallest and best college at Rice University.

name: Jones College
description: Jones is the winningest college at Rice! We have a crazy amount of college spirit, and plenty of ways to get involved! JIBA!
summary: Jones Residential College at Rice University

name: McMurtry College
description: McMurtry College is the 10th residential college to grace the Rice University campus. McMurtry first opened its doors in the fall of 2009.
summary: McMurtry College is the 10th residential college to grace the Rice University campus. McMurtry first opened its doors in the fall of 2009.

name: Hanszen College
description: Hanszen events page: https://owlnest.rice.edu/actioncenter/organization/hanszen/events
summary: The official OwlNest page of Hanszen College.

name: Brown College
description: Margaret Root Brown College was established in 1965 and prides itself on its strong community and culture of care. Its members, past and present, rally around Brown's strong traditions while not being afraid to make innovations of their own. One of the things that makes Rice unique is its Residential College system and the opportunities for academic, social, and personal growth it provides. Within Brown, students will make some of the closest relationships they will have during their Rice careers and develop connections with our extensive network of Associates and Alumni. Brown is not only a building, it is a platform that provides every student with opportunities for innovation, leadership, and creativity.
summary: Margaret Root Brown College was established in 1965 and prides itself on its strong community and culture of care. Its members, past and present, rally around Brown's strong traditions while not being afraid to make innovations of their own.

name: Wiess College
description: Wiess is Nice! Welcome to Team Family Wiess, where our focus on family and community shape every interaction. Founded in 1957, Wiess College is a constantly evolving environment, grounded by its history and traditions (which we define as anything that's done twice!). One of the things that makes Rice unique is its Residential College system and the opportunities for academic, social, and personal growth it provides. The relationships created within the W-shaped walls will last lifetimes, which can be seen through our strong Associate and Alumni network. Goldenrod covered Wiessmen(the gender-neutral term for members) help foster growth, friendship, and leadership within the most recognizable college on campus. TEAAAAMMM!! WIEEESSS!!
summary: Wiess Residential College at Rice University

name: Martel College
description: Welcome to the Best College Ever -- Martel College! This is the 9th residential college in the Rice University residential college system. Included here are all events and programs organized by Martel College's student government. These include programs created by the committees, the class representatives, the Executive Council, and more! See our website and Wikipedia page for more info! Wiki: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Residential_colleges_of_Rice_University&stable=0
summary: Welcome to the Best College Ever -- Martel College! This is the 9th residential college in the Rice University residential college system. Included here are all events and programs organized by Martel College.

name: Baker College
description: Welcome to Baker College, named in honor of Captain James A. Baker! We're the first college alphabetically, chronologically and in every other way possible! We take pride in our breakfasts, traditions and the ever-growing community that calls Baker home. And for those from other colleges, we hope you'll make a visit and maybe take part in some of our traditions. Baker, Hell Yeah!
summary: Welcome to the Baker College Page! If you love Veggies, Meat, and celebrating Christmas half the year, you're in the right place!

name: Duncan College
description:
Duncan College, Rice University’s eleventh residential college, is named to commemorate Charles William Duncan, Jr., who was U.S. Secretary of Energy on the Cabinet of President Jimmy Carter and former chairman of Rice Board of Governors, and his wife, Anne. Anne and Charles Duncan have been extraordinarily generous to Rice through helping establish the James A. Baker III Institute of Public Policy and supporting the Shepherd School of Music. In 2007, they donated $30 million for Rice’s Vision for the Second Century, which included establishing new residential colleges to accommodate the need to expand the student body while keeping the characteristics that makes Rice distinctive. The Duncans have a passion for environmental sustainability which was the motivation behind designing Duncan as Rice’s “green” college. As such, Duncan is one of the first buildings in Houston to receive LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) Gold Certification.

Duncan College opened its doors in 2009 to its first matriculation year. However, the official Duncan dedication took place November 19, 2010 in the Duncan quad. November 19th is now regarded as Duncan’s birthday, “Duncan Day” and the week leading up to it “Duncan Week”. Duncan’s first year also inspired the phrase “Somos equipo, somos familia” which is Spanish for “We are a team, we are a family”, the official Duncan motto. 

Duncan Hype Video
summary: Duncan

name: Office of Alumni Relations / Association of Rice Alumni (ARA)
description:
The purpose of the Association of Rice Alumni (ARA) is to provide the means of a continuing relationship between the university and its former students. The association strives to stimulate intellectual and social participation of all members of the ARA and to enable them to contribute to and share in the enhancement of the university. In addition, the association endeavors to foster deeper concern among its members for the welfare of the university, to strengthen the bond of understanding between the university and the community, to encourage gifts, to attract outstanding students and faculty, and to contribute toward maintaining Rice as a university in keeping with the ideals and aims of its founder. The ARA is governed by the ARA Board of Directors in conjunction with the Office of Alumni Relations.

Programs and Resources:

Sallyportal: Sallyportal is Rice’s own professional development platform focused on mentorship and making connections. Sallyportal is a network of Rice community members - representing hundreds of fields and a wide range of professional experiences - who have all raised their hands saying they are willing to help you. Customize your profile to get tailored advice, whether you’re looking to choose a major, discuss career paths, prepare for an internship and more. It’s never too early to get networking!

Life After Rice: Sponsored by the Association of Rice Alumni and in collaboration with campus partners, the Life After Rice program brings together students and alumni for an interactive panel discussion and networking lunch at Cohen House. Alumni panelists provide students with professional advice and insight to help advance their career and life success. Be on the lookout for upcoming events in the Life After Rice Series! For more information, go to https://alumni.rice.edu/LifeAfterRice.

OwlEdge Externship Program: The Office of Alumni Relations helps to support the Center for Career Development (CCD) with the OwlEdge Externship Program, a short-term (half to multi-day) job shadowing experience that provides students with an opportunity to connect with Rice alumni, parents and friends to explore career options and gain a realistic perspective of an industry. Apply in Handshake!
summary: The purpose of the Association of Rice Alumni (ARA) is to provide the means of a continuing relationship between the university and its former students.

name: Liu Idea Lab for Innovation & Entrepreneurship
description:
The Liu Idea Lab for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (Lilie) is the home of experiential learning in entrepreneurship and innovation at Rice University.

Lilie focuses on teaching both entrepreneurial skills and the entrepreneurial mindset to prepare our students to be entrepreneurs and innovators in whatever industry they choose, be it a technology startup, government, civic group, non-profit, small business, or a large corporation. Lilie is comprised of a state-of-the-art, on-campus lab space, experiential courses, co-curricular opportunities, and an array of resources to support student learning.
summary: The Liu Idea Lab for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (Lilie) is the home of everything entrepreneurship and innovation at Rice University.

name: Rice Athletics
description: Welcome to Rice Athletics 🦉👐, home of your Rice Owls. Rice Fight Never Dies. Sammy The Owl. The MOB. Rice Cheer & Dance. The Blue & Gray. We consider all students on campus part of our Rice Athletics family! We could not have any success without YOU! When you attend games and events, you truly make a difference in the arena of competition. It’s an honor to hold up our owl signs and experience so many of the ‘you had to be there’ moments together. Visit Rice Athletics and see what it means to be part of the Rice Family today!
summary: Welcome to Rice Athletics 🦉👐, home of your Rice Owls. Rice Fight Never Dies. Sammy The Owl. The MOB. Rice Cheer & Dance. The Blue & Gray. We consider all students on campus part of our Rice Athletics family! We could not have any success without YOU!

name: Peer Career Advisors
description: Peer Career Advisors (PCAs) are a group of student volunteers who are selected through an application and interview process during each Spring semester. They serve as ambassadors to undergraduates and play a valuable role in supporting the Center for Career Development (CCD). PCAs reinforce the knowledge, skills, and resources offered through the CCD. PCAs are specially trained to assist you with the following topics: Resume and cover letter reviews, how to use 12twenty for job and internship basics, and using our online resources (Focus2, Vault, etc), and CCD services. Each residential college has its own team of PCAs; therefore, they are easily accessible and available to answer your questions in person or through email, even when the CCD is closed for the night. Check out our 2025-2026 PCAs here or on the poster in your college commons!
summary: Peer Career Advisors (PCAs) are a group of student volunteers who serve as ambassadors to Rice students and play a valuable role in supporting the Center for Career Development (CCD).

name: TestC
description: event test group
summary: event test group

name: The Rice Thresher
description:
The Rice Thresher is the official campus newspaper of Rice University, published by undergraduate students. Since its first publication in January 1916, the Thresher has continued to provide the student body with up-to-date campus news and information. The Thresher is published each Wednesday during the school year except during examination periods and holidays.

The Rice Thresher aims to promote the free flow of information on campus by acting as a designated public student forum and publishing articles relevant to the student body and the greater Rice community. To this end, the Thresher publishes a weekly print edition and online content. The Thresher also hosts the Backpage, a weekly satire piece published on the back of the paper.

The Thresher includes the following teams: news, opinion, features, arts & entertainment, sports, business, copy, design, photography, videography, digital content, and web. Students interested in developing skills in these areas are encouraged to join!
summary: The Rice Thresher is the official campus newspaper of Rice University, published by undergraduate students. The Rice Thresher aims to promote the free flow of information on campus by acting as a designated public student forum.

name: Rice University Honor Council
description: The purpose of the Honor System is to allow maximum freedom for students, undergraduate and graduate, in the completion of all academic work and to ensure the integrity of the work. When students accept this freedom and trust, they are placed on their honor by the group neither to cheat on any assignment nor to violate the trust placed in them in any way. Students demonstrate their responsibilities to their fellow students under the Honor System when they can pledge, in good conscience, that their work is their own and that they have neither witnessed nor suspect violations by others.
summary: The Rice Honor Council is a body composed of elected and appointed undergraduate students responsible for adjudicating cases involving the Rice Honor System.

name: Rice Pre-Medical Society
description: The Rice Pre-Medical Society (RPMS) is one of the largest student organizations on campus, bringing together over 700 members united by an interest in medicine. RPMS offers a wide range of resources—including physician networking events, shadowing and research opportunities, and community volunteering—to support students at every stage of their pre-health journey.
summary: The Rice Pre-Medical Society (RPMS) is an undergraduate student-based organization that aims to provide resources, advice, and opportunities to all Rice students interested in the health professions.

name: Black Graduate Student Association
description: The BGSA supports graduate students in their appreciation and understanding of Black graduate students' academic and social pursuits. We provide a safe space for the exchange of intellectual ideas and social issues that impact Black communities, on and off Rice's campus. Our programs and partnerships facilitate cultural understanding, allyship, and appreciation among members and friends of the BGSA.
summary: The BGSA supports graduate students in exploring the academic and social experiences of Black students. We offer a safe space for dialogue on issues impacting Black communities and foster cultural understanding, allyship, and appreciation.

name: The Shepherd School of Music
description: The Shepherd School of Music at Rice University is one of the premier music schools in the world, combining the intensity of a conservatory experience with the educational excellence of a renowned private university. The Shepherd School of Music was launched in 1975, making it one of the youngest major university level music programs in the country. In just over forty years it has risen to become a leading American music school, providing music performance training for an international student body of 285 music majors focused on a professional career. Our world-class faculty of artist-teachers are dedicated to cultivating the talents of each student and equipping them with the skills and education necessary for success in the professional world.
summary: A platform to advertise events for The Shepherd School of Music.

name: Office of Academic Advising
description:
The Office of Academic Advising (OAA) at Rice University strives for an unparalleled environment for undergraduates to explore opportunities, identify goals, and implement plans to reach those goals.

 

The OAA provides academic advising to any undergraduate student at Rice, and we direct, coordinate, and support campus wide advising by providing training and comprehensive resources to all faculty, staff, and student advisors.

 

The OAA also disseminates academic information directly through systematic programming in the residential colleges, namely through our Peer Academic Advisors (PAAs).

 

Individual advising on general topics (such as course scheduling and academic planning) is available to all undergraduate students, and we provide specialized advising to new students, student athletes, pre-med/pre-health professions students, pre-law students, transfer students, readmitted students (or those seeking readmission), students undecided about their major, and students in need of academic support services.
summary: The Office of Academic Advising at Rice University strives for an unparalleled environment for undergraduates to explore opportunities, identify goals, and implement plans to reach those goals. The OAA provides academic advising to any undergraduate!

name: Peer Academic Advisors
description: Peer Academic Advisors (PAAs) are Rice students trained by the Office of Academic Advising (OAA) to provide academic advising and assistance to their peers. There is a team of PAAs at every residential college! PAAs) serve as liaisons to the OAA and support the office's mission to strive for an unparalleled environment for undergraduates to explore opportunities, identify goals, and implement plans to reach those goals. During the academic year, PAAs are a resource to all students at their college for information and guidance on academic issues.
summary: Peer Academic Advisors (PAAs) are Rice students trained by the Office of Academic Advising (OAA) to provide academic advising and assistance to their peers. There is a team of PAAs at every residential college!

name: Office of Access, and Institutional Excellence
description: The office of Access and Institutional Excellence provides high-level strategic leadership for diversity initiatives and coordinates offices across the campus to help create one point of responsibility for all programs and efforts around diversity by purposely and deliberately working to build greater community across, and out of, the very differences of sexuality, gender, class, race and region associated with past and present inequities.
summary: The Rice University Office of Access and Institutional Excellence seeks to support and guide the university community in fulfilling its mission of “cultivating a diverse community of learning and discovery.”

name: Office of Admission
description: Our goal each year is to enroll an impressive class of curious thinkers, passionate dreamers and energetic doers who believe improving the world demands more than bold thought and brave action. It takes unconventional wisdom. Each student brings a set of experiences to the table – different academic backgrounds, thoughts, values, beliefs and cultural perspectives – and that's what makes studying at Rice a unique experience.
summary: The Office of Admission works closely with the Student Admission Council student organization with recruitment and yield admission events.

name: Graduate and Postdoctoral Studies
description: The Office of Graduate and Postdoctoral Studies act as an academic partner to graduate students as they pursue their graduate degree. Our office handles: Thesis submission, candidacy declaration, graduate student career development, payroll and tuition, fellowship and funding guidance, community building, and student support for well-being and academic issues.
summary: The Office of Graduate and Postdoctoral Studies are an academic partner to graduate students as they pursue their graduate degree.

name: Super Smash Bros. Club
description:
The goal of this organization is to create a large network of Rice students who love Smash. While there is an emphasis on the competitive side of Smash, there are also events meant to introduce newer players and to encourage players of any skill-level to bond. Some of these events include teaching sessions, tournament viewings, and off-campus events.

On the competitive side, there will be internal tourneys within Rice and external tourneys at venues around Houston where the competitors can prove their merit. There is encouragement for both Ultimate players and Melee players to attend these events and means for these players to improve. 

Overall, our club aims to create an inclusive atmosphere that both introduces new players to the game and allows skilled players to thrive in the broader Smash community.
summary: A club centered around the video game franchise Super Smash Bros.. Competitive and casual players alike are welcome to bond over the game through various events, creating a close-knit community at Rice.

name: Rice Undergraduate Geoscience Society
description: The mission of this organization is to enrich the experience of undergraduate students at Rice University studying the Earth, Environmental, and Planetary Sciences (EEPS). We believe that by providing a structured organization for engagement with Earth, Environmental, and Planetary Sciences and those interested in the field we can strengthen the EEPS undergraduate community. We also believe that we can improve the educational opportunities of EEPS students at Rice by providing them with a voice in the EEPS department, encouraging their engagement with graduate students, faculty members, alumni, an Houston community members, and providing them with programming to help with post-graduation plans.
summary: The mission of this organization is to enrich the experience of undergraduate students at Rice University studying and/or interested in Earth, Environmental, and Planetary Sciences (EEPS) or Environmental Science.

name: Rice Data Science
description: Rice Data Science is a student-led and student-centered organization that enables students to realize their passions and career aspirations in the field of data science. Our goal is to create a collaborative, enriching, and interdisciplinary Data Science community at Rice. We coordinate career preparation opportunities, technical workshops, and social events to provide Rice students with the ability to develop greater proficiency in data science and engage with peers who share their passion for data science.
summary: Rice Data Science is a student-led and student-centered organization that enables students to realize their passions and career aspirations in the field of data science.

name: Rice Women in Business
description: Rice Women in Business is a professional organization that aims to expand leadership, networking, and learning opportunities for undergraduate students at Rice, with a special focus on supporting women interested in exploring careers in business. The organization’s mission is to provide members with tools to help them advance both professionally and personally by offering career and industry panels, networking opportunities, and speaker events. All are welcome who support this mission.
summary: Rice Women in Business provides all students interested in women pursuing careers in business with tools for professional and personal growth, offering career panels, networking opportunities, and speaker events. All are welcome who support this mission.

name: English Undergraduate Association
description: The EUA provides an infrastructure for students pursuing a major in English, those considering the major, and those interested in English. Our goal is to encourage a more interconnected community, serve as a network between students and the English Department, and emphasize the relevance of the major outside of the classroom. To achieve these goals, we will engage the Rice community through sponsored events, both on and off campus.
summary: Founded in 2016, the EUA seeks to connect English majors and English students across Rice's residential colleges. The EUA hosts social and professional events to foster community among and provide resources to English majors at Rice.

name: Torque Rice Women's Ultimate Frisbee
description: Torque Rice Women's Ultimate Frisbee is a competitive club team composed of a diverse group of girls. We come together from a variety of backgrounds to compete, have fun, and encourage each other on and off the field in the spirit of the game.
summary: Torque Rice Women's Ultimate Frisbee is a competitive club team composed of a diverse group of girls. We come together from a variety of backgrounds to compete, have fun, and encourage each other on and off the field in the spirit of the game.

name: Rice Ballroom Dance Team
description: Rice Ballroom Dance Team promotes partner style dancing by offering classes in competitive ballroom styles and by attending competitions and social dance functions. Fall 2024 Practices: Mondays 7-9pm in the RMC Grand Hall, Wednesdays/Thursdays TBA
summary: Rice Ballroom Dance Team promotes partner style dancing by offering classes in competitive ballroom styles and by attending competitions and social dance functions. All levels of experience welcome!

name: Rice Rally Club
description: Rice Rally Club is a student-run organization taht promotes Rice athletics, helps build school spirit, enhances the student experience at athletic events, and creates an inclusive enviornment between students and the student-athletes on campus.
summary: Rice Rally Club is a student-run organization that promotes Rice athletics, helps build school spirit, enhances the student experience at athletic events, and creates an inclusive environment between students and the student-athletes on campus.

name: Christians on Campus
description:
We are a community of students who gather to enjoy Christ through reading the Bible, singing, fellowship, and mutual support. Our goal is to cultivate a space for spiritual growth and connection with God and one another. Open to all students interested in exploring or deepening their Christian faith.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=umdUXPEd1nU
summary: We’re a student community enjoying Christ through Bible reading, singing, fellowship, and mutual support. We aim to grow spiritually and connect with God and each other. Open to all students exploring or deepening their Christian faith.

name: Rice Wildlife Conservation Corps
description: Rice Wildlife Conservation Corps spreads awareness of endangered/threatened species and works to help support local and global animal conservation programs. Club members would have the opportunity to learn more about the animal species around them and understand their place in our ecosystems. In addition, through events, fundraisers, field trips, and guest appearances; members would have the opportunity to learn about how to promote the sustainability and preservation of animal species on the brink of extinction. The Wildlife Conservation Club allows students to come together supporting their beliefs and passions for the planet's biodiversity. Past events include beach clean-ups, documentary screenings, and the annual spring break volunteer trip to Mission:Wolf, a sustainable wolf sanctuary in Westcliffe, Colorado.
summary: Rice Wildlife Conservation Corps (RWCC) is a club focused on spreading awareness of environmental issues, creating a community of wildlife conservation enthusiasts, and working to help support local and global animal conservation programs.

name: Rice Undergraduate Materials Science and Nanoengineering Society
description: The Rice Materials Science and Nanoengineering Society works to provide all undergraduate Rice students interested in Materials Science and Nanoengineering with academic, professional, and social resources. The Society will fulfill its goals by acting as a liaison between students and administration, organizing and providing information on company or organization tours and professional conferences, and providing a forum where current and potential Materials Science majors can interact and exchange useful information.
summary: The organization acts as an academic, professional, and social resource for students interested in Materials Science and Nanoengineering.

name: Rice Consulting
description:
Rice Consulting is a student-led, undergraduate management consulting group that provides strategy services to local, regional, and national businesses in order to help our members gain real consulting experience while generating tangible value for our clients. Rice Consulting aspires to make consulting both accessible and fun as a profession and service for Rice students and the clients it serves, and to expose interested Rice students to meaningful client-facing engagements.

 

Rice Consulting seeks to prepare motivated and high-performing students for a successful career, develop valuable communication, leadership, and analytic skills in our members, and foster a collaborative and charming consulting community at Rice University.
summary: Rice Consulting is an undergraduate management consulting group that provides strategy services to local, regional, and national businesses in order to help members gain real consulting experience while generating tangible value for our clients.

name: Rice Women's Club Soccer
summary: Rice Women's Club Soccer is an organization for both undergraduate and graduate students to compete in a competitive soccer league at a level between Intramural Sports and Varsity Sports.

name: Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering Graduate Student Association
description:
Our mission is to:

  1. Foster professional and personal relationships amongst graduate students and between the graduate student body and members of the ChBE faculty.
  2. Provide a forum for concerns, both professional and personal, about graduate student life within the Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering department
  3. Encourage professional growth through: (a) peer mentorship, (b) recruitment, and (c) affiliate / internship relationships
summary: The departmental Graduate Student Assocation (dGSA) representing any graduate student at Rice University who has been accepted into, or is advised by a faculty member of, the Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering Department.

name: Moody Center for the Arts
description: The Moody serves as an experimental platform for creating and presenting works in all disciplines. The mission of the Moody is to encourage creative thinking and original expression, enrich curricular innovation, and promote cross-campus and community collaboration through transformative encounters with the arts.
summary: The Moody serves as an experimental platform for creating and presenting works in all disciplines.

name: Psychological Sciences
description: The Department of Psychological Sciences, at Rice University's School of Social Sciences, is a unique blend of basic and applied psychology. Our breadth of research interests creates a unique learning and research environment. The primary emphasis in the department is on research, in both laboratory and applied settings.
summary: Psychological Sciences - Studying the Mind to Better People's Lives

name: Center for Academic and Professional Communication
description:
The Center for Academic and Professional Communication (formerly the Center for Written, Oral, and Visual Communication) provides workshops, online resources, and one-on-one consultations for all Rice students. Our goal, as part of the Program in Writing and Communication, is to help students gain the skills they need to communicate effectively in an academic setting and in the professional world. The CAPC provides resources and support tailored for undergraduate, graduate, and international students' unique needs. We also support faculty as they teach their students to communicate more effectively and hone their own communication skills.

OUR SERVICES

Oral, visual, and written communication take many forms, and it's essential to adhere to best practices and guidelines for each. Our staff offer assistance with various modes of communication, including the following:

Written Communication:

Academic writing

Scientific writing

Graduate school applications

Fellowship applications

Oral Communication:

In-class presentations

Conference presentations

Research or elevator pitches

Visual Communication:

PowerPoint slides

Research posters

Graphs and charts

Data visualization
summary: Our mission is to assist all members of the Rice community in effectively communicating their ideas to a wide range of audiences, using various written, oral, and visual modes of communication. The CAPC offers workshops, resources, and consultations.

name: Spanish, Portuguese, Latin American Studies
description:
The Department of Spanish, Portuguese, and Latin American Studies is dedicated to the research and teaching of the literatures, cultures and languages of the Spanish and Portuguese speaking worlds. We offer a full range of undergraduate courses in two majors: Latin American Studies (LASR) and Spanish and Portuguese (SPPO), leading to the B.A. Please see the Rice University General Announcements for current undergraduate degree requirements and course listings. Of the eight faculty currently in the department, two are originally from the United States. The rest are from Argentina, Germany, Spain, Uruguay, and Venezuela. Our faculty are researchers and teachers with world-wide recognition. Their areas of expertise span the fields of Iberian, Latin American and U.S. Latino literature, visual media, cultural perspectives, performance studies, material culture, language acquisition, language varieties and many other topics. The department hosts talks, special symposia, film screenings, and cultural events within the School of Humanities and across other campus units, as well as in off-campus theaters, libraries, and performance venues. Houston is an important center for cultural production in the Spanish and Portuguese languages. The strength of our program draws on the cultural richness of the surrounding community, which is reflected in our service learning programs, and our many cultural events.

Spanish, Portuguese, and Latin American Studies | Rayzor Hall 307 | spanish@rice.edu | 713/348-5451
summary: The department offers a full range of undergraduate courses in two majors: Spanish and Portuguese, and Latin American Studies.

name: Engineering Professional Master's Program
description:
The Professional Master’s degree in the George R. Brown School of Engineering at Rice University is designed for the student who wishes to expand his or her engineering knowledge. The degree emphasizes the practical aspects of engineering, combined with management and communication coursework.

Graduates of our program have gone on to work for some of the leading engineering companies in the world, including Schlumberger, Google, Amazon, Tesla, Baylor College of Medicine, Microsoft and ExxonMobil. This degree prepares you to be a leader in the engineering profession and provides you with both academic coursework and networking opportunities to expand your career.
summary: The Professional Master’s degree in the George R. Brown School of Engineering at Rice University is designed for the student who wishes to expand his or her engineering knowledge.

name: Kinesiology
description:
As an academic unit of the Wiess School of Natural Sciences, the Kinesiology Department is committed to health promotion, disease prevention, and training in the sciences of health and human movement. We offer two undergraduate degrees: Bachelor of Arts in Kinesiology with Concentration in Health Sciences or Concentration in Sports Medicine.

Mission Statement:

As an academic unit of the Wiess School of Natural Sciences, the Kinesiology Department is committed to health promotion, disease prevention, and training in the sciences of health and human movement. Our faculty strive for excellence by providing relevant research in the sports medicine and health sciences areas, professional education in the classroom, industry opportunities in the form of internships with cutting-edge organizations, & opportunities to pursue graduate professional degrees.  We accomplish this through appropriate knowledge concerning the practice and application of the health science and sports medicine curriculums.
summary: As an academic unit of the Wiess School of Natural Sciences, the Kinesiology Department is committed to health promotion, disease prevention, and training in the sciences of health and human movement.

name: Campus Post Office/Delivery Services
description: Delivery Services operates a United States Postal Service contract station. We offer all the services that a regular USPS does with the exception of money orders. Delivery Services handles the mail for the departments. The USPS delivers the mail addressed to the residential colleges. Delivery Services is the central drop-off and pick-up for FedEx Express, Ground and Home.
summary: Delivery Services handles the mail for the departments. The USPS delivers the mail addressed to the residential colleges. Delivery Services is the central drop-off and pick-up for FedEx Express, Ground and Home.

name: Religion and Public Life Program
description: The Religion and Public Life Program (RPLP) is an academic program at Rice University devoted to using research on religion to build common ground for the common good. Since its founding in 2010, the RPLP has fulfilled its vision through research, training, and outreach. The RPLP has achieved research success through studying nine countries, surveying over 45,000 individuals, and publishing 11 books and 106 articles in academic journals. The program has also been successful in providing transformative education by training 75 undergraduate students to do research, and in engaging Houston and empowering its success by hosting over 80 public events and reaching thousands more through the media. The RPLP team brings together a diverse set of researchers at different stages of their academic careers to foster cross-disciplinary collaboration on religion in the public sphere. Undergraduate student fellows participate in data collection, complete semester-long analysis projects, help develop and support our programming, and host our scholar and civic leader guests. Learn more about ongoing research projects, current RPLP fellows, and upcoming events at rplp.rice.edu.
summary: The Religion and Public Life Program is an academic program at Rice University devoted to using research on religion to build common ground for the common good.

name: Latin American Graduate Student Association
description: The Latin American Graduate Student Association provides a professional, informative, and social network for graduate students at Rice University to understand and appreciate Latin American culture.
summary: The Latin American Graduate Student Association provides a professional, informative, and social network for graduate students at Rice University to understand and appreciate Latin American culture.

name: Rice Office of STEM Engagement
description: The Rice Office of STEM Engagement (R-STEM) serves as the single point of contact for all science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) related outreach and education efforts at Rice University. We promote science awareness by supporting faculty and students at Rice University and K-12 educators in developing STEM-related educational outreach programs and initiatives. We outline this in our Vision & Mission. In collaboration with the Office of the Vice Provost for Research at Rice University, R-STEM facilitates interdisciplinary collaborations, professional development programs and workshops for K-12 teachers, and collaborations between scientific and educational communities that integrate authentic research and practices. If you have questions regarding any of our teacher or student programs, outreach events, or want to find out how you can get involved in supporting STEM in Houston, feel free to email us any time at stem@rice.edu.
summary: The Rice Office of STEM Engagement (R-STEM) supports and promotes Rice University's wide-ranging efforts to improve K-12 science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) education in all Greater Houston Area school districts. We are proud to serve

name: Cornerstone Christian Fellowship
description:
Open to all students interested in exploring faith, our purpose is to make God-loving and compassionate disciples of Jesus Christ among all nations through two primary goals: to bring college students at both Rice and UH through a process of discipleship that will enable them to live in passionate pursuit of Jesus Christ with their lives long after they leave college, and as college students deepen in their relationships of Christ, we hope to see them reach out as a community to others by building relationships with other Christians as well as non-Christians.

Check out more at ccf.rice.edu, or on Instagram @ccf.hcc!
summary: Cornerstone Christian Fellowship is the college ministry of Houston Chinese Church.  Open to all students, this organization exists to make God-loving and compassionate disciples of Jesus Christ among all nations.

name: Rice Linguistics Student Association
description: The Rice Linguistics Student Association (RLSA) is an undergraduate academic and social club for anyone interested in the languages of the world and the study of language. We host peer academic advising in linguistics, movie viewings, game nights, and community outreach events (e.g. International Mother Language Day) on campus throughout the year.
summary: An undergraduate social and academic club for all students (majors and non-majors) interested in language and linguistics.

name: Rice University Young Democrats
description: Welcome to the Rice Young Democrats! We are a group of passionate students who work with a network of faculty, political officials, and community members across the Houston area to fight for democracy and human rights. We host politicians on campus, have letter-writing events, and collaborate with other student clubs on campus, and aim to give students a safe space to advocate for immigration rights, women's rights, the environment, LGBTQ+ rights, and most importantly, MOVEMENT and PROGRESS in each of these issue areas on a community and party-wide level.
summary: As a club for Democrats and progressives, we register voters, connect students with quality internships, promote progressive activism, and coordinate with political campaigns to increase turnout. We are chartered under the College Democrats of America.

name: Mariachi Luna Llena
description: Mariachi Luna Llena is Rice University's student-run professional mariachi group. The mariachi is dedicated to building cultural bridges both at Rice University and in the Greater Houston community. Through public and private performances, the group aims to keep the rich tradition of mariachi music alive and to increase awareness of Texas’s Mexican cultural heritage. In the past, the mariachi has performed at Miller Outdoor Theater, the Houston Rodeo, at Rockets and Astros games, and even in international music festivals abroad! The ensemble seeks to build family and community across the Greater Houston area. Mariachi Luna Llena truly believes that musical performance brings people together.
summary: Mariachi Luna Llena is Rice's student-led mariachi band! We do on-campus performances and events around Houston. Some of our past gigs include the Houston Rodeo, Rockets and Astros games, Miller Outdoor Theater, and even international music festivals!

name: Rice Escape
description: Rice Escape is Rice's on campus student made and student run escape room.
summary: Rice's first student-run escape room.

name: Rice Eclipse Rocketry Team
description:
We are Rice University’s student rocketry team. Our mission is to enrich the undergraduate and graduate education of our members with project-based and hands on learning to develop cutting edge aerospace and engineering technologies. We develop launch vehicles for intercollegiate rocketry competitions, rocket engines, and avionics hardware and software. And importantly, no experience is required to join us!

Rice Eclipse is a diverse and constantly growing club. Since its founding in 2014, it has expanded from 5 to over 70 active members working on 19 different technical subteams spread between 3 major technical divisions. Majors represented include chemistry, computer science, mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, chemical engineering, material science, economics, linguistics and many more! Many members participate in multiple subteams across multiple disciplines, and others focus their time and energy into one team.

Our ranks include undergraduate and graduate students. Our members learn practical skills like design and fabrication from their peers long before they encounter these topics in their courses, in addition to invaluable interpersonal skills. We are brought together by a passion for learning and innovation. Every member is dedicated to helping his or her teammates reach their full potential as future engineers, scientists, and people, no matter their skill level or academic level. 

If you are a Rice student interested in joining our endeavor, passionate about engineering, aerospace, or rocketry, or want to be a part of a large design team please reach out to us! An again, no prior experience is needed or expected.
summary: The Rice Eclipse Rocketry Team aims to enhance the engineering education of our members by designing, developing, and testing rocket engines and launch vehicles. Eclipse is open to any student regardless of major, interest, or experience level.

name: Japanese Student Association
description: The Japanese Student Association is a community of students with a shared interest in the Japanese culture and language. Through hosting fun activities such as game nights, movie nights, and food-making workshops, we hope to connect students across campus. We are excited to provide fully in-person events this year!
summary: We are a cultural club that welcomes everyone and anyone with an interest in Japanese culture to join.

name: Rice Salseros
description: Rice Salseros is an organization that teaches latin dancing to the Rice community. Every Monday at 7 pm we teach a beginner and intermediate dance class for an hour in the Grand Hall or Grad Bubble. We usually teach salsa, though we also teach bachata and Cuban salsa rueda. We also have a choreography team! Our goal is to spread the skills and love of dance through the Rice community!
summary:
a. Provide a network of salsa dancers at Rice.
b. Organize social and educational events in which dancers can share and develop their skills.
c. Serve as a liaison between the Houston salsa community and Rice students and community members.

name: Department of Bioengineering
description: The Rice University Department of Bioengineering is located in the Texas Medical Center, the largest in the world. Our undergraduate and doctoral program are consistently ranked among the top 10 bioengineering and biomedical engineering degree programs in the U.S. The doctoral program draws on interdisciplinary skills that reach from the biological sciences to modern materials science, computer science and bioprocess design. Our comprehensive curriculum provides a fundamental understanding of basic life and medical sciences, as well as advanced analytical expertise complemented by hands-on experience in translational research that transfers innovations from bench to bedside.
summary: Advancing knowledge in the bioengineering field at the Rice University George R. Brown School of Engineering. Through innovative research & learning environments, we empower students to collaborate & invent.

name: School of Social Sciences Gateway Program
description:
The Gateway Program provides experiential and co-curricular opportunities for Social Sciences Undergraduates to apply what they learn in the classroom to real life. Our internships and practica, research, international programs, and events are designed to lead our majors to a thoughtful path to career development and self-discovery. Thus, prior to graduation, social sciences students have an idea what it takes to build networks and become leaders in academia and the workforce.

 

PROFESSIONAL PRACTICA

Professional practica provide students with real-world experience in consulting and finance. Students participate in faculty-led, upper-level courses and on-site experiential learning at local firms, which help them produce a final portfolio based on their professional experience.

OWLCONNECT

OwlConnect events bring Rice alumni and career leaders to campus to provide current students with experienced perspectives on career paths, job progression, and other tips on how to market their degree in social sciences to a particular field. The goal is for students to leave with a better understanding of how to translate their academic studies into challenging and rewarding careers.

TRAVEL GRANTS

Gateway awards travel grants to undergraduate social sciences majors who are presenting their research at a conference outside of Houston. Proposals are accepted on a rolling basis. Each student may receive this award a maximum of two times during their undergraduate career at Rice.
summary: The Moody Gateway Program offers students majoring in the social sciences a way to answer the frequent question, "Where can I go with my social sciences degree?"

name: Center for Languages and Intercultural Communication
description: The Center for Languages & Intercultural Communication focuses on developing Rice students’ communicative, interactional, and intercultural abilities and knowledge in non-native and heritage languages. Thus, CLIC seeks to provide a broad and critical educational experience that promotes multilingualism and helps students become culturally competent and interact successfully, appropriately, and respectfully with people from different countries and sociocultural backgrounds.
summary: The Center for Languages & Intercultural Communication teaches 10 languages. Our courses focus on the development of interactional competence in the target language. Courses are based on a student-centered, critical thinking approach to language analysis

name: Visual and Dramatic Arts
description:
Creative Scholars, Engaged Artists

The Department of Art welcomes the full spectrum of Rice University students. Scientists, architects, historians, engineers and economists, among many others, augment our core of arts majors to create a diverse, lively forum of artists and thinkers. We believe this composite community is a vital asset to majors and non-majors alike: art thrives in contact with new and varied perspectives, and the risk-taking and critical thinking necessary to making art are crucial in many other fields. Beyond a dynamic artistic practice, we aim to cultivate an artistic frame of mind. Arts@rice.edu
summary: ART.RICE.EDU arts@rice.edu

name: The Doerr Institute for New Leaders
description:
Every Rice student has the capacity to lead. We help you unlock it.
Because leaders are not born; they are grown. 

We offer free leader development programs to all Rice students that expand upon your diverse qualities and increase your capacity to make a difference in the world.

The following Doerr Institute programs are offered each semester:

> Activation One-on-One Leadership Coaching

> Catalyst Leadership Workshops

> Synthesis Leader Groups

> Leadership Cohorts 

> Off-campus Excursions


Get Matched! 
Take our Match Survey to find the perfect program to start your Rice leadership journey. 

_______________________________________________

Recognitions

Most Outstanding Program 2019, Association of Leadership Educators (ALE)
summary:
The Doerr Institute for New Leaders offers free leader development programs to all Rice students that expand upon your diverse qualities and increase your capacity to make a difference in the world.
Find out more at RiceLeader.org

name: Fondren Library
description:
Welcome to Rice University’s Fondren Library!

Fondren Library, located at the center of the academic quad, is a key center of learning in our educational community, providing access to scholarly information, research support, library instruction, and computing and study facilities. At Fondren Library and through our extensive web sites, you'll find important services and resources to help you meet your academic and research goals.

The library is the home of books and print materials, as well as a wide range of audio-visual media, sound recordings, and rare and unique collections of books, photographs, manuscripts, and maps. Throughout the Library, renovations are underway that will further facilitate collaboration and quiet study. The library also links users to a world of scholarly information available through licensed online databases and journals and a vast network of reciprocal borrowing agreements with libraries around the world. Just as importantly, the Libraries’ expert librarians and professional staff provide unparalleled research assistance and library services in person, over the phone, or online.

We hope you'll consider Fondren Library to be your educational partner and source of quality scholarship and learning during your time at Rice University. We welcome your thoughts and suggestions about your experience in the library and with the libraries’ online services. I wish you every success in your inquiries and pursuit of learning. -Sara Lowman, Vice Provost & University Librarian

library.rice.edu
summary: Fondren Library supports Rice University’s research and teaching by providing innovative research and information services, access to scholarly resources, and inviting physical and virtual spaces.

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of the Mech GSA is to create a strong community between graduate students in the department, increase visibility of research fields and encourage collaborations, and lastly, contribute thoughts and ideas to the School of Engineering and GSA as a whole.
summary: Mech GSA's purpose is to create a strong community between graduate students in the department, increase visibility of research fields and encourage collaborations, and lastly, contribute thoughts and ideas to the School of Engineering and GSA as a whole

name: Rice Chinese Christian Fellowship
description: As part of the campus ministry of the Houston Chinese Church at Rice University (or as the Rice Chinese Christian Fellowship associated with the Houston Chinese Church), Rice Chinese Christian Fellowship aspires to build up a community of Rice Chinese seeking to follow the example and commands of Jesus Christ and grow in knowledge of Him and love for Him through prayer, small group Bible Study, worship, service and daily application. It is our ultimate goal to nurture future Chinese Christian leaders who are willing to pursue the Great Commission set by Jesus Christ at Matthew 28:19-20.  While this organization has specific interest in international students of Chinese origin, our meetings and membership are open to everyone.
summary: Rice Chinese Christian Fellowship aspires to build up a community of Rice Chinese seeking to follow the example and commands of Jesus Christ and grow in knowledge of Him and love for Him through prayer, small group Bible Study, worship, and service.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Association
description: The CGSA embodies the social fabric of the Chemistry graduate department. We host many events each month to encourage students to take a break from lab work and socialize. Further, we provide for career development opportunities whether that is inviting faculty from other schools, bringing in industry speakers, or offering resources for practice talks.
summary: Departmental GSA serving the graduate chemistry student association at Rice University.

name: BASYK Dance Club
description: BASYK is a K-pop/hip hop dance club that welcomes dancers of all experience levels to participate in the fun of dance!  Through free workshops and various campus wide performances, such as our annual CYPHER showcase, BASYK provides undergraduate and graduate students a way to socialize and dance together.
summary: BASYK is a K-pop/hip hop dance club that welcomes dancers of all experience levels to participate in the fun of dance! We perform at cultural festivals at Rice and host a showcase for the dance community at the end of the year.

name: Baker Institute Student Forum
description: The Baker Institute Student Forum promotes policy engagement and discussion on campus. By hosting informal, student-led events, BISF enables policy-minded students to grapple with contemporary issues and make interdisciplinary connections. Through formal events with Baker Institute fellows, BISF links undergraduates with scholars in a wide range of policy areas. BISF also sponsors an undergraduate policy competition and publishes an undergraduate public policy journal.
summary: The Baker Institute Student Forum promotes policy engagement and discussion on campus through formal and informal events. BISF also sponsors an undergraduate policy competition and publishes an undergraduate public policy journal.

name: Rice Architecture Society
description:
Rice Architecture Society is the voice of the Rice Architecture student body. With representatives from every studio, this group helps to shape the culture of the school while also providing a liaison between the students and the school’s faculty and administration. Society’s main social and educational initiatives include:


- Open studios: late-night social gatherings for the exchange of snacks and ideas
- Mini-charrettes: short-term design competitions
- Architectronica (fall) and ArchiArts (spring): university-wide parties
- Materials Reuse: free digital and physical community-sourced resources
- Peer Mentorship: all freshmen are paired with an upperclassman to assist them throughout the year


Society also helps organize and run the community Mini-Kitchen, wellbeing events, Ask the Dean panels, DEI lectures, student directory, and study breaks. Its hardworking, focused members collaborate closely with faculty, staff, Society-adjacent student organizations at Anderson (ARC, AGSA, NOMAS), and the Student Association at Rice University to build a culture of care and participation at Rice Architecture.
summary: The purpose of Rice Architecture Society is to serve as the student governing body for the Rice Architecture School. The club holds regular meetings and serve as the point of contact between administration and student body.

name: Rice Badminton Club
description:
Welcome to the Rice Badminton Club (RBC)!

We are a competitive sports club open to the whole Rice community (undergrads, grads, faculty, staff, etc.). There are no skill level requirements for playing; anyone who loves badminton is welcome to join! Nevertheless, we also participate in national & state-wide collegiate tournaments every year, so we strongly welcome players with competition experience to join us.

We offer practice/playing for all registered members during the semester.

Tentative details for Fall 2025:

1. Member Registration: A Google form will be later posted that must be submitted alongside the club dues and signed waiver to be added as an registered member on OwlNest.

2. Practice: There will be courts for practice in Tudor East Gym once the Fall semester starts (time TBD once the semester starts). Official communication on practice times will be sent to official registered members once times and location are finalized. There may or may not be a training class offered this semester (more details are being worked out by the officers).

3. Tournaments: We will selecting a team and participating in the South Central Conference Team Tournament (qualifier for Collegiate Nationals) hosted in Houston Badminton Center this year representing Rice University in the Fall semester. More details on competition and team selection will be sent to registered members. 

If there are any questions, feel free to email us.
summary: We are a competitive sports club, and anyone who loves badminton is welcome!

name: Rice University Chess Club
description: We aim to provide all Rice students, those interested as well as those not well-informed, with a non-competitive setting for learning and practicing chess, as well as socializing with equally-interested members in the Rice and Houston community.
summary: We aim to provide all Rice students, those interested as well as those not well-informed, with a non-competitive setting for learning and practicing chess, as well as socializing with equally-interested members in the Rice and Houston community.

name: Rice Climbing Club
description:
We are a group of Rice Students of all experience levels that are passionate about rock climbing. We hold two weekly practices at Momentum Silver Street and Momentum Katy, as well as non-practice meet-ups at the Momentum Silver Street and Momentum Katy climbing gyms, and welcome climbers of all ability levels, from beginner to nationally-ranked athlete! Practices are designed with all ability levels in mind so that everyone can participate in improving their climbing game and experience! We organize outdoor climbing trips at least once per semester to areas nearby, and any club member is able to join us on these trips (even if you've never climbed outside before!). We also offer opportunities to compete in local competitions and the USA Climbing Collegiate circuit. We primarily communicate through our Groupme, but if you have any questions, or are interested in joining us, feel free to reach out via email to cap15@rice.edu!

Momentum Waiver found here

Groupme Linked Here for climbing club
summary: We are a group that is enthusiastic about rock climbing! We hold practices twice a week for those interested, and have other climbing meetups as well! We also have opportunities to compete in climbing and participate in outdoor climbing trips!

name: Rice Club Sailing Team
description: The Rice Club Sailing Team is a competitive sailing team that also seeks to inform members of the Rice community about sailing. We operate out of Lakewood Yacht Club in Seabrook, practicing primarily on a fleet of CFJs and the occasional keelboat or singlehanded boat. We have competitive practices almost every weekend to train for regional and national regattas and learn-to-sail classes several times per semester!
summary: We are a casual sailing team for both undergraduate and graduate students. No experience required.

name: Rice Fencing Club
description: The Rice Fencing Club seeks to promote the sport of fencing within the greater Rice University community. We strive to develop current members' abilities, as well as to provide the equipment and lessons to help new fencers enter the sport. We hold regular practice twice a week with coaches who have fenced and coached at the international level. Our practices focus on developing both the athleticism and tactical thinking that the sport requires.
summary: The Rice Fencing Club seeks to promote the sport of fencing within the greater Rice University community. We strive to develop current members' abilities, as well as to provide the equipment and lessons necessary to help new fencers enter the sport.

name: Rice Habitat for Humanity
description: The purpose of Rice Habitat is: (a) to educate the Rice University community about Habitat for Humanity International’s mission, the need for low-cost housing, and the issue of homelessness; (b) to assist Houston Habitat for Humanity in the construction of homes by supplying volunteers from the Rice University community, as well as to assist other similar service-minded organizations in the greater Houston area; and, (c) to raise money for Habitat for Humanity International, Houston Habitat for Humanity, and for Rice Habitat.
summary:
The Rice University Habitat for Humanity chapter aims to productively and responsibly
involve Rice undergraduates in the work of Habitat for Humanity. The club provides
opportunities to volunteer on build days alongside Houston Habitat for Humanity.

name: Rice Rasikas
description: As Rice University's premier South Asian classical dance team, Rice Rasikas is committed to cultivating the art form and spreading our dance to the Rice community! We use footwork, expressions, body language, and complex formations to tell stories to our audiences, and we compete nationally at 2-3 venues every year. We also participate in on-campus performances like Dhamaka, Cypher, and our team spring showcase so that we can show our friends at Rice what we do. Each of our pieces is original choreography developed by our dancers, and our music is also mixed by members of our team! Through dance, Rasikas find community, foster creative development, and develop lasting friendships with each other while impacting the Rice community as a whole. We hold auditions in the beginning of the fall semester of every year, and we are always looking for strong dancers of all backgrounds to audition to help us win competitions and be part of our Rasikas family! #RR4L
summary: Rice University's ~premier~ South Asian classical dance team

name: Bedside Narratives
description:
Join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/103818799/klGoba7t. 

The Rice University Chapter of Bedside Narratives is a student-run outreach project. This project is dedicated to preserving and sharing the life-legacies of vulnerable patients and elders through the power of storytelling. We aim to give patients and elders the therapeutic benefit of being able to "tell their story" as well as having an undergraduate student-companion visit them on a weekly basis. Members will interview patients and elders about their lives and prepare creative writing, podcasts, and other works of art to capture narratives.
