With that, we also seek to build a relationship between Rice students and the underrepresented portion of the Houston population that fosters an overall more empathetic and connected community.
summary: We pair undergraduate volunteers with residents at nursing homes and hospice care facilities for a semester-long oral history project. Volunteers will visit resident weekly and interview them about their lives and prepare written narratives and podcasts.

name: Society of Women Engineers
description: SWE at Rice is the C057 chapter of the National Society of Women Engineers. With a special focus on supporting women, we foster student’s growth in engineering by connecting students to internships, jobs, and research opportunities, bond members through internal social activities, and expanding the impact beyond. The community through multiple outreach activities. Our goal is to empower students, especially women, to achieve their full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expand the image of the engineering profession as a positive force in improving quality of life, and demonstrate the value of diversity. The organization is open to all who support its mission.
summary: We empower students, with a focus on women supporting each other, to reach their potential as engineers and leaders, expand engineering’s image as a positive force for quality of life and to show the value of diversity. Open to all who share this mission

name: South Asian Society
description: We are a cultural club dedicated to the representation of South Asian traditions and cross-cultural exchange at Rice University and hosting unique cultural events to serve as a platform to create an inclusive environment for all Rice students.
summary: We are a cultural club dedicated to the representation of South Asian traditions and cross-cultural exchange at Rice University and hosting unique cultural events to serve as a platform to create an inclusive environment for all Rice students.

name: The Rice University Alzheimer's Buddies
description:
Rice Alzheimer’s Buddies began in August 2017, with our first memory care visits occurring in January 2018. Our mission is to: (i) engage college volunteers and buddies in meaningful intergenerational relationships; (ii) reduce the stigma and silence around neurodegenerative diseases; and (iii) raise awareness about elder care issues in the Houston community.

We currently have 60+ volunteers in our Buddy Program who visit five memory care facilities every week. We also host an annual Rice Alzheimer's Symposium and welcome students to our committee to help plan this event.
summary: Our mission is to: (i) engage college volunteers and buddies in meaningful intergenerational relationships; (ii) reduce the stigma and silence around neurodegenerative diseases; and (iii) raise awareness about elder care issues in the Houston community.

name: Rice Equestrian Team
description: As an Intercollegiate Horse Show Association team, we compete with other colleges and universities in our region. Riders of all experience levels are welcome, even no experience with horses at all! Lessons are tailored to meet the individual needs of each rider, whether you are just starting out or have been riding for years. Feel free to get into contact with us if you have any questions, and fill out this form if you're interested in joining!
summary: As an Intercollegiate Horse Show Association team, we compete with other colleges and universities in our region. Riders of all experience levels are welcome, even no experience with horses at all!

name: Rice Medical Design
description: Rice Medical Design is dedicated to students passionate about medical technology. We spearhead student-driven engineering projects, collaborating with faculty and clinicians. Beyond projects, we organize industry presentations, competitions, and other events to further our members' professional growth.
summary: RiceMD's mission is to foster interest in medical technology. Through speaker events and hands-on engineering projects, we not only support our members' professional growth but also strive for tangible impact within our community.

name: Rice Alliance for Mental Health Awareness
description: The Rice Alliance for Mental Health Awareness (RAMHA) aims to reduce the stigma surrounding mental health disorders by promoting an open and positive discussion of mental health and creating a welcoming environment in which students can seek appropriate help, feel supported by their community, and lead a better life. We strive to capitalize on the energy and dedication of college students at Rice University and those beyond the hedges in the fight against the stigma and oppression surrounding mental health and to educate and empower all Rice students, as well as faculty and staff, to protect their own mental health.
summary: RAMHA is a student organization dedicated to reducing the stigma surrounding mental health disorders and promoting positive conversations about mental health on campus by hosting discussions and events throughout the year.

name: Science Olympiad Alumni Association
description:
The Rice Science Olympiad Alumni Association is a student-run organization built around the idea of providing high school students a venue to explore various STEM-related fields that they may never have heard of, much less learned about. We do this by hosting a science competition called Science Olympiad, where students compete in 23 different STEM events ranging from thermodynamics to fossils (see https://www.soinc.org/ for more information on the competition). 

This will be our sixth year hosting the competition, and we hope to continue our success forward as, in our in-person format, we have had more than 500 students from 30 teams competing, and virtually, we hosted 80 teams from around the country with more than 1000 students competing. More importantly, this is an enormously useful way to get students active and interested in STEM (as many of the alumni can attest to), and we think that this is a great way to give back to our scientific community. For more information on our invitational/regional competition, please see our website scioly.rice.edu.
summary: Student-run organization that primarily deals with hosting and running an annual Science Olympiad STEM competition for over 500 high schools students from 30 high schools across the nation.

name: Rice Program Council
description:
For over 50 years, Rice Program Council has provided students with opportunities for campus-wide bonding and entertainment.

RPC’s goal is to provide the Rice undergraduate community with a diverse array of fun and memorable campus-wide social events throughout the year. While RPC is most known for Esperanza, our fall semester formal dance, our most popular event by far is Beer Bike.

Traditionally, our major fall events include: Screw-Yer-Roommate, Homecoming events, Esperanza, Gingerbread House Building competition, and the Fall Study Break. Our spring events include: Crush Party, Rondelet, Finals Study Break, and Beer Bike.

RPC also provides opportunities to go beyond the hedges and explore Houston through subsidized tickets provided by the Passport to Houston Committee. These include “Rice Nights” at the Houston Ballet, Symphony, Theatre, and Opera. Tickets are provided to the Houston Zoo Lights in the winter and Houston Rodeo in the spring.
summary: RPC is a student run, campus-wide organization involved in hosting diverse campus-wide social events throughout the year. These events aim to build community and enhance the quality of life for all Rice students.

name: Rice Vegan Society
summary: RVS strives to share information on a vegan diet and lifestyle and its impact on health, environmental sustainability, animal rights etc. with the Rice community. RVS represents vegans at Rice and seeks to encourage a more vegan-friendly campus.

name: Rice University Muslim Student Association
description: The Rice University Muslim Student Association is an open organization welcoming individuals from all backgrounds. We attempt to foster awareness about Islam in our campus, as well as beyond the hedges. We are open to any suggestions and feedback that may better carry out our mission statement. The Rice MSA holds a variety of events throughout the year that cater to both members and the general Rice community. These events include Ramadan iftars, Eid banquets, guest lecturers, Charity Week, and Islam Immersion Week, as well as community service events.
summary: The Rice University Muslim Student Association is an open organization welcoming individuals from all backgrounds. We foster unity among students at Rice, spread awareness about Islam, and engage in community service.

name: Rice American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: The goals of Rice ASME are to increase communication and develop relationships between students, faculty, and professional engineers in industry, work with industry partners and academic institutions to help identify career opportunities, and promote interest in engineering in both the Rice and local Houston communities. We host information and recruiting sessions, invite guest speakers, and promote attendance in the ASME national chapter. In addition, Rice ASME hosts mentorship and social opportunities to serve as a resource for all Rice mechanical engineering students.
summary: The Rice American Society of Mechanical Engineers serves as the local student chapter of the national ASME professional society. We seek to develop and enhance the education of members through professional development and social events.

name: Rice Women's Resource Center
description: The mission of the Rice University's Women's Resource Center is to increase awareness of and sensitivity to gender issues in order to build a more supportive, dynamic, and conscious atmosphere on campus.
summary: The mission of the Rice University's Women's Resource Center is to increase awareness of and sensitivity to gender issues in order to build a more supportive, dynamic, and conscious atmosphere on campus.

name: R2: The Rice Review
description: R2 is an anthology of original literature (poetry, fiction, creative non-fiction), that is published at least once a year. The club also promotes the literary arts around campus by hosting literary events such Open Mic Night.
summary: R2 is an anthology of original literature (poetry, fiction, creative non-fiction), that is published at least once a year. The club also promotes the literary arts around campus by hosting literary events such Open Mic Night.

name: Sport Management
description: The Department of Sport Management explores business concepts as related to the sport industry. The department offers an undergraduate degree in Sport Management with the following areas of concentration: Sport Analytics, Sport Law, and Sport Leadership.
summary: Sport Management is an undergraduate major within the School of Social Sciences, and is much like a business administration degree. We will provide you the curriculum and experience needed to get hired in your field of choice in the sport industry.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
description:
AIAA aims to foster interest in the many industries that involve flight, aeronautics, and space travel and exploration. AIAA plans to do this by hosting professional speakers working in different relevant fields, providing networking opportunities for its members, and creating an inclusive platform for all those who have interest in aerospace.

Past speakers have included John Scott, who works in NASA's Propulsion and Power Division, Justin Figueroa, an Airplane Configuration Design Engineer working at Boeing, and many others.
summary: AIAA aims to foster interest in the many industries that involve flight, aeronautics, and space travel and exploration.

name: Rice Bhakti Yoga Club
description:
Would you say you’re a deep thinker? Do you love vegetarian food, music, yoga, meditation, and enlightening discussions? Rice Bhakti Yoga offers a platform to have the complete yoga experience - body, mind, & soul - and connect with like-minded people.

Through a dynamic philosophy of self-realization, acquiring skills like meditation and developing cutting-edge lifestyle tools for the modern day, this club is a sacred space for attaining wisdom to lead a successful and more importantly, happy lifestyle.

Join us for weekly sessions (mantra meditation, wisdom talks, dinner) as well as field trips, creative sessions, picnics, and community activities. Join us for a chance to unwind and relax. This club is about you and your wellness. Rice Bhakti Yoga, where spirituality becomes practical.
summary: Would you say you’re a deep thinker? Do you love vegetarian food, music, yoga, meditation, and enlightening discussions? Rice Bhakti Yoga offers a platform to have the complete yoga experience - body, mind, & soul - and connect with like-minded people.

name: Center for Engaged Research and Collaborative Learning
description: Formerly known as Houston Enriches Rice Education, the HERE Project has become the Center for Engaged Research and Collaborative Learning (CERCL) and is affiliated with Rice’s Kinder Institute for Urban Research. Mission Through innovative research, engaged pedagogy, and creative approaches to application, The Center for Engaged Research and Collaborative Learning seeks to promote and advance models and practices of an expansive approach to leadership both on and off the Rice campus. Objectives While the actives of the Center will grow and shift over time, it functions based on an understanding that fulfillment of its mission involves careful attention to some of the basic building blocks of leadership: (1) creative knowledge production; (2) critical thinking skills; (3) effective communication strategies; and (4); application through engagement with a wide-range of perspectives, persons, and interests, both locally and globally. Initiatives CERCL’s mission and objectives are enacted through innovative programming meant to enhance attention and approaches to addressing the socio-cultural, historical and political dimensions of life in Houston.
summary: Through innovative research, engaged pedagogy, and creative approaches to application, The Center for Engaged Research and Collaborative Learning (CERCL) seeks to promote and advance models and practices of an expansive approach to leadership.

name: Rice Taiwanese Association
description: RTA promotes Taiwanese cultural awareness throughout the Rice campus. In order to offer our members the opportunity to meet other students interested in the Taiwanese culture, RTA sponsors study breaks, night market, and other programs.
summary: RTA promotes Taiwanese cultural awareness at Rice. RTA sponsors study breaks, night market, and other programs for our members to meet other students interested in the Taiwanese culture. Follow @ricetaiwanese on Instagram for more event information!

name: Best Buddies
description: The mission of Best Buddies is to establish a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment, leadership development, and inclusive living for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD). We do this at the college level by matching mutually enriching friendships between young adults with and without IDD and by promoting inclusion throughout the community.
summary: We are dedicated to our part in ending the isolation of people with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD). At Rice, we match one-on-one friendships between young adults with and without IDD and promote a more inclusive community for all.

name: Rice MusicMDs
description:
Rice MusicMDs is a music therapy-inspired outreach organization of students with musical experience
who volunteer to promote patient healing in healthcare institutions via music-assisted patient
communication. MusicMDs members are trained to actively assist in the healing process by providing live, one-on-one musical performances to individual patients, families, and staff at Houston Methodist.
summary:
Rice MusicMDs is a music therapy-inspired outreach organization of students with musical experience
who volunteer to promote patient healing in healthcare institutions via music-assisted patient
communication, by providing live, one-on-one performances.

name: Rice Dance Theatre
description: Rice Dance Theatre offers classes twice per week in styles such as contemporary, jazz, hip hop, ballet, and more, all taught by professional dancers from the Houston community. We hold auditions each semester open to all Rice students, and accepted members are cast in student-choreographed dances, which are performed to over 200 audience members in our semester showcase.
summary: Established in the '70s, RDT is the only pre-professional contemporary dance company on Rice's campus. We provide a space to take weekly classes in various styles and perform both student and professional choreography.

name: American Medical Women's Association Rice Branch
description:
AMWA Rice aims to foster an enriching environment for students pursuing the medical profession, with a special focus on supporting women, and to engage the Rice and Houston communities on key issues concerning women’s health and rights.
Our projects, such as the AMWA Rice-Baylor Mentorship Program, medical panels, and medical immersion weekends, seek to create a network of support for Rice students pursuing medicine, with an emphasis on supporting women. Additionally, we encourage our members to pursue leadership roles in developing projects that advocate for women’s health and rights issues that they are passionate about. All are welcome who support this mission.
summary: AMWA Rice aims to foster an enriching environment for students pursuing the medical profession, with a special focus on supporting women, and to engage the Rice and Houston communities on key issues concerning women’s health and rights.

name: Humanities Graduate Student Association
description: Our club's purpose is to facilitate open and honest communication between the Humanities graduate students and the staff, faculty, and Dean of Humanities. We advocate for Humanities graduate student interest and wellbeing, fostering inclusive engagement among the graduate student population of the Humanities.
summary: Our club facilitates communication between Humanities graduate students and the staff, faculty, and Dean of Humanities, as well as advocating for graduate students' interests and wellbeing.

name: Rice Electric Vehicle Team
description:
Rice Electric Vehicle represents all academic years and a diverse set of majors across Rice University. This organization allows Rice undergraduates to participate in a large-scale, multidisciplinary design project, providing an opportunity to develop project management, leadership, collaboration, and professional skills used in industry. 

After previous success in the Shell Eco Marathon, the club is now also pivoting to the American Solar Challenge and Formula Sun Grand Prix. The team participated in its first competition in Spring 2012 at the Shell Eco-Marathon Americas (SEMA), an international competition which scores vehicles based on their energy efficiency. The team placed at an impressive second in the Solar Prototype category.

In the 2019 Shell Eco-Marathon, the team produced exciting results with their vehicle completing all 7 qualifying laps and yielding a calculated efficiency of around 980 MGe. And most recently, the team attended the 2022 Shell Eco-Marathon, outperforming all other university teams in its division.

Now being revived for dedicated solar car endurance races with more and tighter competition, the Rice Electric Vehicle team hopes to serve as a tangible application and example of the engineering excellence at Rice University as we undertake a more ambitious, road-legal, carbon-fiber solar car designed for both real roads and racetracks.
summary: Rice Electric Vehicle (REV) designs and constructs electric vehicles to race in local, national, or international car competitions.

name: Rice Taiwanese Graduate Student Association
description: Rice Taiwanese Student Association is a group of master, doctor, and post-doc Rice students focused on Taiwanese culture and tradiations. We strive to help all members blend into Rice community, getting prepared for job recruiting and connecting to local Taiwanese groups.
summary: Rice Taiwanese Student Association brings together graduate and postdoctoral students to celebrate Taiwanese culture and traditions. We help members integrate into the Rice community, prepare for job recruiting, and connect with local Taiwanese groups.

name: Rice University Mock Trial
description: Rice University Mock Trial is a nationally competitive team. Each year we compete in simulated trials as attorneys and witnesses, developing skills in argumentation, writing, public speaking, and acting. Visit our website at mocktrial.rice.edu to learn more!
summary: Rice University Mock Trial is a nationally competitive team. Each year we compete in simulated trials as attorneys and witnesses, developing skills in argumentation, writing, public speaking, and acting. Email riceumocktrial@gmail.com to learn more!

name: Nocturnal
description: Nocturnal is an all-gender 6-part a cappella group that performs contemporary music at Rice University and across Texas. Nocturnal is known for its eclectic, bold, and authentic experimentation across indie pop, alternative rock, R&B, folk, and more. Some of our activities include collaborating with Houston social justice organizations, singing with choreography at an annual Texas invite-only showcase, and hosting the only late-night raunchy After Dark show. Every semester, we host 2-3 concerts, each with unique song selections. We welcome diverse musicians from all walks of life.
summary: Nocturnal is an all-gender a cappella group at Rice University that performs at on-campus and off-campus events. Our members seek to improve their musical abilities and share their joy of singing with the Rice community and beyond.

name: Rice Women's Club Water Polo
description: CWPA-registered women's club water polo team. We compete in the Texas division. Undergrad and graduate students welcome. We usually compete in three tournaments per season but practice together year round.
summary: CWPA-registered women's club water polo team. We compete in the Texas division. No experience necessary, please reach out if you have any interest!

name: Rice Lions
description:
BOOKINGS: riceulions@gmail.com OR @ricelions on Instagram

Rice Lions is Rice University's premiere and student-run lion dancing team. We come together to showcase the traditional art of lion dancing with vibrant lion costumes, energetic choreography, and powerful drumming. The team is dedicated to preserving and promoting the cultural heritage of lion dancing, while fostering community and camaraderie among its members. The team aims to integrate itself into and learn from the greater lion dancing community worldwide. Join the crowd and experience the excitement and energy of a Rice Lions performance today!
summary: A student-run lion dance team. Our mission is to promote and preserve the art of lion dancing at Rice University, Houston, and beyond. We aim to connect Rice Lions with the greater lion dancing community and foster a friendly social environment.

name: Rice Robotics Club
description:
Register to join our club here: https://forms.gle/CLmVti3XTgQxwDUb6
Club Calendar: HERE
 
The Rice Robotics Club (RRC) is focused on providing members the resources to explore their interests in engineering and design. Our goal is to encourage students to collaborate on projects to develop their skills in engineering for industry development and research. Our club focuses on nurturing the development of engineering based skills including expertise in engineering design, manufacturing, computation, and electronics. We also encourage members to establish leadership skills by developing solutions to real-world problems as a team and leading innovative projects.

                   RRC utilizes the facilities found at Rice University to guarantee members with an environment containing all of the necessary resources to pursue their interests. The Oshman Engineering Design Kitchen, where RRC is based, is equipped with a variety of heavy machinery and 3D printers allowing members to prototype and manufacture any and all of their designs. For those inexperienced with the machinery, RRC is staffed with knowledgeable members ready to teach and assist newcomers in operating the equipment. Our club also encourages members to learn 3D modeling and offers seminars in Computer Aided Design (CAD) for both SOLIDWORKS and OnShape.
summary: Rice Robotics Club’s mission is to overcome challenging robotics problems and prepare students for their careers by providing practical learning experiences. We foster collaboration across disciplines & equip our members with necessary robotics skills.

name: Rice Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students
description:
We aim to:
provide a community where students on pre-health tracks, especially those from underrepresented backgrounds, can receive resources and advice to strengthen applications
provide mentorship to pre-health students, especially those from underrepresented backgrounds, thereby increasing exposure to health-related professions
increase knowledge of the application process for various health-related graduate schools (dental, medical, masters in public health, etc…)
make partnerships with various organizations and health professionals in the Houston community to better the health of the larger Houston community through volunteership

All are welcome who support this mission
summary: We exist to promote both the professional and academic advancement of pre-health students at Rice University, with a focus on increasing representation of minority populations within health care professions. All are welcome who support this mission.

name: Rice Pakistani Student Association
description:
The purpose of the Rice Pakistani Students Association is to create a vibrant and collaborative community welcoming all who are interested in celebrating Pakistani culture and traditions within Rice and to provide opportunities for members to interact within the greater Pakistani leadership in Houston, Texas and the United States.

Additional objectives include -
To create a vibrant and collaborative community at Rice for RPSA members
To provide a mutual support system for RPSA membersat Rice
To voice the concerns of RPSA members at Rice to the administration
To share Pakistani values, culture and heritage with the greater Rice community
To provide avenues for networking with the greater Pakistani community in Houston and more broadly, within the US.
To develop and maintain a strong alumni network
summary: The Rice Pakistani Student Association (RPSA) creates a vibrant, collaborative community welcoming all who are interested in Pakistani culture and traditions. We provide opportunities to connect with Pakistani leadership in Houston and across the U.S.

name: Rice Table Tennis Club
description:
The Rice Table Tennis club (RTTC) is an organization for table tennis players, recreational or competitive, to meet up and compete. RTTC also helps form connections with Houston's competitive table tennis community and the National College Table Tennis Association (NCTTA).

Before joining Rice Table Tennis, you need to join Rice University Club Sports on Owlnest and complete the Club Sports Waiver. 

If you are interested in joining us for the 2025-2026 academic year you can fill put this form -- so we will contact you :)

Follow us on Instagram: @ricetabletennis
summary: The Rice Table Tennis Club helps develop players' skills and love of table tennis in a community in which they can express their interest and form relationships.

name: Rice University Student Chapter of the Association for Women in Mathematics
description: Our goal is to provide a network with a special focus on supporting women and other underrepresented groups who are undergraduates, graduate students, and faculty members in mathematics, applied mathematics, statistics, and other disciplines who do computational research. We cater events to promote interest in mathematics among women and other underrepresented groups, and to provide leadership, career development, and outreach opportunities for young mathematicians. All are welcome who support this mission.
summary: A network offering leadership, career development, and outreach for young mathematicians, with a focus on supporting women and other underrepresented groups across all academic levels in math and related fields. Open to all who share this mission.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description:
VSA is a group of mainly Rice undergraduate students dedicated to the promotion and teaching of Vietnamese culture. Throughout the year, we are involved in several community projects, such as the annual College Leadership Workshop (CLW) & Southeast Asian Pacific Islander (SEAPI) Night in the fall and the Cultural Showcase + Maid Cafe in the spring, and we love creating opportunities for students to lead and engage.

Beyond cultural programming and volunteer opportunities, we host casual socials like food outings and study breaks to foster friendships and deepen our connections with fellow students, faculty, and staff. Whether you're interested in Vietnamese history, language, music, or cuisine—or simply looking for a welcoming space—VSA invites you to be part of our growing family. Vietnamese or not, all are welcome!

Check out one of our most popular dances!
summary: VSA is a group of mainly Rice undergraduate students dedicated to the promotion and teaching of Vietnamese culture. In addition, we host an annual College Leadership Workshop in the fall and are involved in the Cultural Showcase in the spring!

name: Rice Coalition on Hunger and Homelessness
description:
The mission of Coalition Rice is to develop service and advocacy programs for the homeless and economically disadvantaged of Houston through a combination of direct relief efforts, long-term reincorporation projects, and on-campus educational events.

 

We host several regular events that encourage our members to think critically about poverty and homelessness.

  • Sandwich Pack Preparation: We collaborate with local shelters (ex. Emergency Aid Coalition) each semester to prepare hundreds of sack lunches and snack packs. 
  • Off-Site Events: Once a semester, we partner with a local organization to plan an off-site service event during which volunteers provide direct relief to the surrounding Houston community.
  • Panel Events: We host panel events that feature professors, social workers, and public figures who are knowledgeable about homelessness and poverty in Houston. These events provide Rice students with valuable insight into how the Houston community is currently developing and implementing long-term solutions to homelessness.
  • Insurance Initiatives: We partner with Montrose Grace Place to assist members of Houston's homeless community with applications to government sponsored programs such as Medicaid.
summary: The mission of Coalition Rice is to develop service and advocacy programs for the homeless and economically disadvantaged of Houston through a combination of direct relief efforts, long-term reincorporation projects, and on-campus educational events.

name: Rice Campanile
description: The Rice Campanile is dedicated to producing an annual and historical student-run publication, which accurately and fairly represents undergraduate student life, and provides a designated public forum. The Campanile strives to present an accurate and fair representation of a full academic year at Rice University, and provides an opportunity for students to develop journalistic knowledge and skill. We hope to promote constant improvement in the diversity and depth of coverage while also providing education and cultivating skills. Ultimately, the yearbook is a space to elevate the voices of Rice and capture the not-so-conventional moments of Rice through design, photo, and story-telling.
summary: The undergraduate yearbook of Rice University

name: Rice Baptist Student Ministry
description:
A Christian organization that fosters community and creates a safe environment for those interested in Christianity.

We host small and large events and offer many community service opportunities.
summary:
A Christian organization that fosters community and creates a safe environment for those interested in Christianity. 

We host small and large events and offer many community service opportunities.

name: Queer Graduate Student Association
description:
The Queer Graduate Student Association seeks to provide a supportive community for graduate students and postdoctoral researchers who identify as a gender or sexual minority or variant (GSM) and their allies who do not identify as such. The goal of the group is to foster an inclusive community for all graduate students and postdocs.

We accomplish these goals by:

1. Providing social activities to encourage and nurture a supportive community
2. Working with other GSM focused organizations on campus and within Houston to extend this sense of community beyond graduate school
3. Collaborating with the Rice Wellness Center and Houston Montrose Center to provide Ally Training. Aimed at providing Rice students, faculty and staff with the knowledge and tools with which they can help maintain an open and accepting community on campus.
4. Planning campus-wide events in which the Rice community is invited to learn about and celebrate its GSM diversity
summary: The Queer Graduate Student Association (QGSA) fosters a community for graduate students and postdocs who identify as GSM and allies. We host social events, collaborate and plan campus programs including ally training. Open to all who share this mission.

name: Rice University Korean Graduate Student Association
description: Rice University Korean Graduate Student Association (KGSA) is organized to build a community and enhance a sense of community among graduate and post-graduate students on campus who wish to celebrate Korean culture and traditions. The goal of KGSA is to let KGSA members communicate, socialize and support each other.
summary: Rice University Korean Graduate Student Association (KGSA) is organized to build a community and enhance a sense of community among graduate and post-graduate students on campus focused on celebrating Korean culture and traditions.

name: ComSciClub
description: ComSciClub provides a community to foster the development of students' science communication skills, encourages students to present their research both on campus and in the community, and creates a scientific communication network in Houston to provide long-term professional development opportunities for students. ComSciClub also hosts ComSciCon Houston, a yearly scientific communication workshop hosted in partnership with neighboring institutions.
summary: ComSciClub fosters students' science communication skills, encourages students to present their research, and creates a scientific communication network in Houston. ComSciClub also hosts ComSciCon Houston, an annual science communication workshop.

name: Applied Physics Graduate Student Association
description: The APGSA seeks to provide community and support for graduate students in Applied Physics. We sponsor various research talks and social events and discuss concerns and ideas which may, in turn, benefit the Applied Physics program.
summary: The APGSA seeks to provide community and support for graduate students in Applied Physics. We sponsor various research talks and social events and discuss concerns and ideas which may, in turn, benefit the Applied Physics program.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
SHPE changes lives by empowering students interested in the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development. Open to all who support this mission, SHPE’s vision is a world where students are highly valued and influential as the leading innovators, scientists, mathematicians, and engineers.

At SHPE Rice, our mission is to create a welcoming familia that offers career exposure and professional opportunities to those interested in the Hispanic STEM community at Rice University. We host various professional development workshops, sponsor students to attend the annual SHPE National Convention, and hold biweekly general body meetings (GBMs). Our community outreach extends to local high schools, and we also organize fun social events to foster a sense of familia!

Visit shpe.rice.edu for more information. Be sure to subscribe to our email list and join our GroupMe to stay updated on all our events!
summary: SHPE Rice creates a welcoming familia providing career exposure & professional opportunities to those interested in the Hispanic STEM community. Open to all who share this mission, we foster an inclusive, supportive environment for our familia to thrive.

name: Rice Hillel
description: As the only inclusive, egalitarian and pluralistic Jewish campus organization, Rice Hillel welcomes LGBTQI students, those from interfaith backgrounds, and everyone's Jew-adjacent friends.
summary: Open for all students interested in Jewish life, learning, and community, Rice Hillel provides religious, cultural, and social activities for the Rice community.

name: Rice Undergraduate Finance Club
description:
Rice Finance is an undergraduate organization that helps students develop fundamental skills for the finance industry. In addition to hosting recruiting, networking, and informational events, Rice Finance also provides a semester-long training program to teach students basic finance and investment skills. Upon completion of the training program, students are encouraged to apply to the Rice Undergraduate Investment Fund (RUIF), the part of Rice Finance that manages an investment portfolio held by the Rice Management Company. RUIF contains 9 sectors that each cover a specific industry. Each sector pitches a stock every semester, and the Rice Finance Board and the Portfolio Review division make investment decisions.

 

You can find some of our previous work & more information about the club here:
Rice Finance - Club Overview
 
Link to sign up for this listserv for anyone not subscribed yet: Rice Finance Listserv
summary: Rice Finance is an undergraduate organization that helps students develop fundamental skills for the finance industry. The club collectively manages an investment portfolio held by the Rice Management Company

name: Rice University Pre-Law Society
description: Established in 1995 as the Legalese, Rice University Pre-Law Society is Rice University’s official and only Pre-Law focused group. We unite students interested in going to law school as well provide information concerning law school admissions, law school itself, and the legal profession. This is done through the organization of various events throughout the year.
summary: Rice University Pre-Law Society's mission is to educate our members about law school admission and the legal profession.

name: Rice Women in STEM
description: We aim to support fellow undergraduate students at Rice in their pursuit of higher education and future careers in the STEM fields, as well as encourage younger girls in the local community. Our club hosts esteemed female leaders, including Rice faculty, to come talk to us during lunch meetings. Another main focus of our club is to do outreach and help inspire interest in STEM fields in younger students. The organization is open to all who support its mission, with a special focus on fostering community for women in STEM.
summary: Rice Women in STEM supports students pursuing majors and careers in science, technology, engineering, and math, with a focus on women. Open to all who share this mission, we provide peer support, mentorship, career guidance, and academic resources.

name: Baylor College of Medicine - Patient Discharge Initiative
description: The Patient Discharge Initiative is a group of ~90 Rice University undergraduates that aims to understand and address health disparities in Harris County. Through volunteering at Ben Taub Hospital, we support patients through navigating our healthcare system. Often, our patients identify as low-income, uninsured, or non-English speaking; we strive to connect our patients to medical and social community resources and to improve their post-discharge quality of life. To supplement our work at the hospital, we have seven committees pursuing diverse projects, such as researching the impact of educational interventions on patient understanding of HPV and mammography, connecting with community organizations to better understand their services and foster improved referrals, and developing and updating the resources we provide to patients. We are constantly working with our physician mentors from the Baylor College of Medicine and administrators in the Population Health Department to improve our services and would love to have you join us in our mission to address social determinants of health and improve health equity in Houston!
summary: The Patient Discharge Initiative is a group of students passionate about addressing social determinants of health in Harris County. We support patients through community resources and pursue diverse projects related to health equity in our community.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers Rice Chapter
description:
The club’s mission is the following:

  • Promote excellence in chemical engineering education
  • Enhance the lifelong career development of chemical engineers through services and networking. 
  • Stimulate collaborative efforts among industry professionals, current students, alumni and professional societies.
  • Encourage other engineering and scientific students to participate in AIChE activities.
summary: The Rice Chapter of the American Institute of Chemical Engineers (AIChE) is an academic and professional club which focuses on providing a community in which both current chemical engineering students and alumni can interact socially and professionally.

name: Rice Bhangra
description: Bhangra is a traditional Indian folk dance originating in Punjab. It is characterized by high energy music and choreography. As a club, we aim to celebrate Bhangra and produce a competitive set that incorporates a modern twist to the dance as well! We compete against other Bhangra teams at national competitions and preform for various events in the greater Houston area.
summary: Bhangra is a traditional Indian folk dance originating in Punjab. It is characterized by high energy music and choreography. As a club, we aim to celebrate Bhangra and produce a competitive set that incorporates a modern twist to the dance!

name: The Rice Apollos
description: The Apollos are Rice's premier all-male A Cappella group! We love to sing and perform and are always open to new opportunities to do so. We sing all genres, including but not limited to pop, jazz, R&B, and barbershop. We want to give members a chance to be a part of a unique community and promote both individual and group improvement in all aspects of vocal technique. We also love to have fun and we love to entertain! Check out our Spotify and follow us on Instagram to learn about some of our performances, music, and members!
summary: Rice University's all-male A Cappella ensemble. We sing all genres of music, including whatever our members want to sing, and perform throughout the year on and off campus!

name: Rice Sustainability Coalition
description: RSC is an organization formed with the intent of unifying Rice environmental organizations. Each individual org will still operate independently, but membership with RSC will allow orgs to communicate with one another, collaborate on events, or simply learn about campus initiatives. RSC meets around every 2 weeks and most club leaders/representatives attend every meeting. Clubs, organizations, and individuals can join RSC. Rice Sustainability Coalition was formerly known as Rice Environmental Society.
summary: We are the umbrella organization for environmental and sustainability organizations on campus. Our membership includes org leaders from over 15 Rice environmental clubs. Students interested in getting involved should email riceenvironsociety@gmail.com.

name: Rice International Student Association
description:
The Rice International Student Association unites and empowers the international student body by
building an inclusive community rooted in connection, advocacy, and cultural celebration. Through
unique events and cross-cultural collaboration, RISA is a space where international voices are
heard, valued, and elevated. We aim to support a globally minded Rice that fosters lifelong friendships
and a network that extends far beyond campus.

  • Join our WhatsApp group — stay in the loop and meet other international students (link).

  • Sign up for our listserv — get updates straight to your inbox (link, use Incognito mode if it doesn't open).

  • Follow us on our socials — click the icons below to connect with us online.

 

To Connect. To Advocate. To Celebrate.
summary: RISA brings together Rice’s international community through connection, advocacy, and cultural celebration — creating a space where every voice is valued and friendships that last beyond campus time.

name: FastWarp: Board and Card Game Club
description: FastWarp is a student-run club at Rice dedicated to tabletop games and gaming culture. At regular meetings, we play a variety of board games and help plan for the board game convention that we host, OwlCon. Our mission is to make sure everyone interested in tabletop games feels welcome and has their interests represented by our offerings, as well as try to promote the club and tabletop gaming in general throughout campus.
summary: We promote board games and tabletop games on campus! Our club is a welcoming environment for anyone to come, play games, and socialize with other students. We also host OwlCon, an annual gaming convention at Rice.

name: Civic Duty Rice
description: Civic Duty Rice strives to engage Rice students and Houston area youth in civic education and community outreach initiatives. We aim to teach young people the importance of civic engagement and to empower them to create change in their communities through increased service activity, political activism and efficacy, and voter participation. We are Rice's foremost nonpartisan voter engagement organization, and we partner with a wide array of Houston-area organizations to help bridge the divide between youth and government. Aside from hosting Volunteer Deputy Voter Registrar trainings, candidate forums, and voter registration drives, our flagship initiative is the Houston Youth Voters Conference, which brings together students from Houston high schools and universities to discuss the importance of youth voting and civic engagement. Our vision for Rice and Houston is to create a community of empowered and interconnected youth who work together to build a more resilient, inclusive city.
summary: Civic Duty Rice strives to build and empower a diverse community of Houston youth around civic participation and action. We accomplish our goals through service activities, political activism, voter registration and participation, and civic education.

name: Bioengineering Graduate Student Association
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be:

  1. To foster better professional and personal relationships among students and between students and faculty members.
  2. To provide a forum for concerns, both professional and personal, about graduate student life, and serve as means of advocacy through interfacing with faculty committees.
  3. To foster personal and professional growth through the following:
  • Social events to build community
  • Efforts towards diversity, equity, and inclusion
  • Opportunities to receive mentorship
  • Outreach and mentorship to our communities 
  • Election of student representatives to the recruitment committee 
  • Networking opportunities
summary: The BIOE GSA represents the interests and activities of Bioengineering graduate students, with the primary goals being to promote the social engagement and professional development of graduate students and to provide an outlet for voicing concerns.

name: Rice University Quiz Bowl
summary: Rice Quizbowl is Rice's academic trivia team. We play and host tournaments throughout the school year, and practice twice a week. We're very low commitment - anyone can come to as many practices as they want, and quizbowl is a lot of fun!

name: Rice Medical Humanities
description: The Rice Medical Humanities club bridges the gap between the Humanities and Social Sciences and Medicine through a variety of educational and community events that actively engage both students and professionals at Rice and beyond. The organization also conducts an annual Medical Humanities Symposium that fosters interest in interdisciplinary connections between medicine and the humanities, as well as promote the medical humanities in undergraduate education.
summary: The Rice Medical Humanities Club is an undergraduate organization that focuses on medicine as an interdisciplinary field. All of our events explore a different connection between the humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and medicine.

name: Rice Omega Psi
description: Rice Omega Psi is a chapter of Omega Psi Inc., a national cognitive science honor society. The organization aims to increase awareness of and involvement in Cognitive Science at Rice while recognizing undergraduate excellence in scholarship. This goal is accomplished by encouraging research, networking, and community outreach among students, faculty, and professionals in cognitive science and related fields.
summary: Rice Omega Psi is a chapter of Omega Psi Inc., a national cognitive science honor society. The organization aims to increase awareness of and involvement in Cognitive Science at Rice while recognizing undergraduate excellence in scholarship.

name: Rice University Human Factors and Ergonomics Society Student Chapter
description:
Welcome to Rice University's Human Factors and Ergonomics Society (HFES) Student Chapter! 

The Human Factors and Ergonomics Society (est. 1957) is the world's largest scientific association for human factors/ergonomics professionals. Members come from various interdisciplinary backgrounds, including students, researchers, academics, and industry practitioners. Its mission is to advance the science and practice of designing for people in systems through knowledge exchange, collaboration, and advocacy. The Rice University HFES Chapter is an established affiliate of the national HFES organization.

Our goal is to promote and advance the understanding of human factors involved in the design, manufacture, and use of machines, systems, environments, and devices of all kinds. We do this via the exchange of of knowledge/methods in the behavioral, biological, and physical sciences, as well as the industrial, computer science, and engineering disciplines.
summary: The Rice HFES Student Chapter exists to serve the needs of individuals interested in the topic of human factors. As a chapter, we seek to create networking, professional, and educational opportunities to promote human factors at Rice University.

name: Academic and Cultural Empowerment at Rice
description: ACE@Rice is a collective of graduate and undergraduate students that seeks to strengthen academic skills, reduce the achievement gap, and motivate H.I.S.D Title I high school students to pursue further education after they graduate.
summary: ACE@Rice is a collective of graduate and undergraduate students that seeks to strengthen academic skills, reduce the achievement gap, and motivate H.I.S.D Title I high school students to pursue further education after they graduate.

name: Rice University Club Baseball
description:
The Rice Club Baseball organization is a group of Rice students who love the game of baseball enough to continue their competitive career through their college years.  The team is composed of players of all skill levels, but has become more competitive this decade. Founded in the Fall of 2004, the team has been competing in the National Club Baseball Association for more than five years.  Since 2004, the team has played more than 100 games against other colleges and universities from around the country.  In 2010, the club team won District VII and earned a berth in the NCBA Division II World Series. The past several years have seen the emergence of a New Core group of talented players, who look to make their mark on the team lore.

Practices include 2 hour long field practices, in addition to 2 batting cage and bullpen sessions. We ask that players attend at least 1 practice every week. Our fall schedule includes about 5 games, and our Spring schedule includes about 18 games. We do not have a home field, and will travel about 20 minutes for Houston games or several hours for overnight away series or tournaments. We welcome players of all ages and skill levels, although playing time in the spring will be given to those who demonstrate the most at practice, as well as satisfy the NCBA requirements (24 or younger, unless delayed by military service). Please reach out if you are interested!
summary: We are an organization dedicated to maintaining a team atmostphere that provides a way for members of the Rice community to continue to play baseball during college at a fun but competitive level.

name: Rice Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Student Chapter
description: The Rice Chapter of IEEE is a student organization dedicated to connecting ECE students to exciting opportunities and resources at Rice and beyond. Our goal is to help prepare ECE majors for life “beyond the hedges.” In addition, Rice IEEE encourages freshmen and sophomores to major in Electrical Engineering and works to foster a strong community of students, faculty, and professionals.
summary: The Rice Chapter of IEEE is a student organization dedicated to connecting ECE students to exciting opportunities and resources at Rice and beyond. In addition, Rice IEEE works to foster a strong community of students, faculty, and professionals.

name: Office of Study Abroad
description:
The world is your campus! Explore beyond the hedges to learn in ways you never imagined.

 

The Rice University Office of Study Abroad strives to provide substantial, transformational experiences within each undergraduate’s personal curriculum through intellectually rigorous and culturally enriching international opportunities. 

 

Our Study Abroad Advisors provide individualized advising to help you with your study abroad process before you leave, while abroad, and after you return to campus.

 

We conduct workshops and other events all semester on a variety of topics, all of which will be posted here on OwlNest!

 

You can review our online database for Rice-approved programs keeping your academic and personal needs in mind. Rice-approved programs are distinguished by their academic focus contributing to the curricular needs of Rice University as well as integration with host communities.
summary: The Office of Study Abroad at Rice University strives to provide substantial, transformational experiences within each undergraduate’s personal curriculum through intellectually rigorous and culturally enriching international opportunities.

name: GradGames
description: All grad students are welcome! We do board games, video games, card games, and everything between. Contact at graduatestudentgaming@gmail.com. Discord: https://discord.gg/h3dQgje Mailing list: https://mailman.rice.edu/mailman/listinfo/gradgames
summary:
All grad students are welcome! We do board games, video games, card games, and everything between. Contact at graduatestudentgaming@gmail.com.

Discord:  https://discord.gg/h3dQgje
Mailing list: https://mailman.rice.edu/mailman/listinfo/gradgames

name: Rice Online Learning
description: As a leader in global online education, Rice Online Learning furthers the University’s commitment to teaching and research excellence by delivering quality education in and beyond the classroom. We work hand-in-hand with faculty to drive the latest innovations in digital education, ensuring unsurpassed teaching and learning in a digital environment.
summary: Immerse yourself in an engaging digital learning environment. Whether you’re earning credits, a degree, or discovering a new subject, never miss a minute of your course as you learn on your own schedule.

name: Spontaneous Combustion
description: Spontaneous Combustion is the gold standard of Rice University on-campus improvisational comedy. We've got tomfoolery, shenanigans, with a healthy dose of malarkey. We play games. We act out scenes. All unscripted. All the time.
summary: Rice's One and Only Premier Improvisational Comedy troupe. We are here to entertain you. Come check us out!

name: Students Transforming Rice Into a Violence-Free Environment
description:
STRIVE Coalition is a dedicated group of students who have come together to address sexual and domestic violence on campus. The Coalition is made up of an executive board, STRIVE liaisons to colleges, STRIVE liaisons to graduate programs, STRIVE representatives in a variety of student groups, and volunteers. The Coalition is advised by The SAFE Office.    

Vision: We envision a world that is free from inequality and violence, where every person experiences sexuality and relationships in healthy and positive ways.

Mission: STRIVE is a student-driven coalition that empowers all members of the Rice community to play an active role in eliminating gender-based inequality and sexual violence. 

We do this by:
- Engaging in positive, open dialogue 
- Pooling on and off-campus resources 
- Providing the tools and skills necessary to create cultural change 

STRIVE Liaisons: Liaisons both to the colleges and the graduate programs are specially trained students who host prevention activities and seek to support students by providing information about resources available on and off campus.
summary: Rice STRIVE is a student organization that works to provide survivors of interpersonal violence and others affected by it with resources both on campus and around Houston while also raising awareness of interpersonal violence on campus.

name: The Rice Philharmonics
description:
We are the Rice Philharmonics—Rice University’s premier, all-gender a cappella team. We represent diverse identities and hold diverse academic interests, but we come together over our shared passion for music!

The Rice Philharmonics have competed at the ICCAs (most recently, as national quarter-finalists), toured the United States, and recorded numerous studio albums (stream still phil. and Philos on Spotify and Apple Music)! We also organize many concerts around Rice and within the Houston community.

Members have varying degrees of prior musical experience, but through their time in the Phils, are able to improve in all aspects of vocal technique. The Phils are also unique in that we arrange our own music! Each of our songs—whether it’s Best Part, Standing Next to You, Somewhere Over the Rainbow, Erase Me, Too Sweet—has been arranged by a different Phil. 

If you have questions about joining the phamily or booking a gig, please reach out to Dhilani Premaratne (ddp7@rice.edu)!

 

YouTube · Rice Phils

Spotify
summary: Founded in 1994, The Rice Philharmonics is Rice's premier a cappella singing ensemble. The Phils entertain audiences on campus and beyond, and tour and compete nationally. Recently, the Phils released their third studio album, "Still Phil."

name: University Court
summary: UCourt is a representative body of undergraduate students responsible for administering the Rice Code of Conduct and Rice Alcohol Policy. Additionally, UCourt serves as the judicial branch of the Rice Student Association (SA).

name: Rice Men's Club Volleyball
description: Howdy! We are the Rice Men's Club Volleyball team! We are a group of dedicated Rice students extending our college experience to the sport we love through practice, competition, and fun. Our team practices three times a week and competes across Texas in fall and spring tournaments, taking us to the countrywide National Tournament every year. We're looking to grow as a team in terms of numbers and skill, so if you're interested in being part of the team, we hope to see you at our tryouts in the fall!
summary: Howdy! We are a group of dedicated Rice students extending our college experience to the sport we love through practice, competition, and fun. We're looking to grow as a team in both numbers and skill, so we hope to see you at tryouts in the fall!

name: Rice University National Professional Science Master's Association
description: The Rice University National Professional Science Master's Association is a student-led organization committed to fostering community within the PSM program. We organize social events, volunteer and community outreach, professional development workshops, and various other activities to support student engagement and growth.
summary: The Rice University National Professional Science Master's Association is a student-led organization committed to fostering community within the PSM program.

name: 180 Degrees Consulting - Rice University Branch
description: Our mission at the Rice University chapter of 180 Degrees Consulting is to help local Houston non-profits and NGOs solve their business challenges while offering Rice students the opportunity to develop their consulting skills with real-world experience.
summary: Our mission at the Rice University chapter of 180 Degrees Consulting is to help local Houston non-profits and NGOs solve their business challenges while offering Rice students the opportunity to develop their consulting skills with real-world experience.

name: Rice University Cycling and Triathlon
description:
Rice University Cycling and Triathlon (RUCT) provides an opportunity for the Rice community (undergraduates, graduate students, faculty, staff, and alumni) to pursue both competitive and recreational cycling and triathlon events.

RUCT provides a supportive and constructive environment for novice members, and offers valuable benefits to members of all levels of fitness and experience. The team also provides a social means of uniting members of the Rice community who enjoy cycling and triathlon events for the purposes of training, racing, improving fitness and enjoying the sports together.

This organization represents Rice University in the South Central Collegiate Cycling Conference (SCCCC) of the National Collegiate Cycling Association and the College Triathlon Conference. We also race in a variety of competitive and endurance races around Houston.

We encourage everyone to come try out our practices and get involved! However, you have to pay membership dues in order to officially join the team on OwlNest, receive race reimbursements, and benefit from sponsorships. See our website https://cycling.rice.edu/home for more information.
summary: Rice University Cycling and Triathlon provides an opportunity for the Rice community (undergraduates, graduate students, faculty, staff, and alumni) to pursue both competitive and recreational cycling and triathlon events.

name: Global Medical Missions Alliance
description: GMMA (Global Medical Missions Alliance) is an organization dedicated to equipping students interested in Christian-centered health care and pre-health fields to become medical missionaries. Our campus chapter is dedicated to providing a space for those interested in Christian pre-health to encourage and network with each other through fellowship and prayer. We also invite healthcare professionals from various fields to share their experiences in the missions field and how their faith impacts their careers.
summary: Rice GMMA provides community for all students interested in exploring Christian faith and health professions. We offer opportunities to serve through biannual medical missions and local outreach in the Houston community.

name: Systems, Synthetic, & Physical Biology Graduate Student Association
description: We put the math, physics, and engineering into biology. We are the Systems, Synthetic, & Physical Biology Graduate Student Association (SSPB GSA), the departmental GSA of students of the SSPB Graduate Program and affiliated labs, bringing together and representing all students of the systems, synthetic, and physical biology disciplines – an interdisciplinary group equipped to solve complex biological problems and engineer transformative biotechnologies that revolutionize agriculture, electronics, energy, the environment, information storage, medicine, and manufacturing.
summary: The departmental GSA of students of the SSPB Graduate Program and affiliated labs, bringing together and representing all students of the systems, synthetic, and physical biology disciplines.

name: Rice Red Cross
description: The American Red Cross Club at Rice University is an official chapter of the American Red Cross and serves their mission to, "prevent and alleviate human suffering in the face of emergencies by mobilizing the power of volunteers and the generosity of donors." We are a volunteer-based organization that works with the Greater Houston Area Chapter to host local Red Cross events. Our goal is to provide Rice undergraduate students with meaningful opportunities for education, training, and volunteer/community service so that they remain a part of the Red Cross family throughout their lives.
summary: The mission of the American Red Cross Club at Rice University is to prevent and alleviate human suffering in the face of emergencies by mobilizing the power of volunteers and the generosity of donors.

name: Black Student Association
description: Established in the early 1970s, BSA fosters community, advocacy, and cultural enrichment at Rice University. We provide a safe space for the exchange of intellectual ideas and social issues that impact Black communities, on and off Rice's campus. Through service, educational programming, and events like Soul Night and Black History Month celebrations, BSA promotes awareness of issues affecting Black communities and creates opportunities for connection and engagement. Open to all students who support this mission, BSA supports dialogue, leadership, and cultural appreciation across campus and the greater Houston area.
summary: The Black Student Association fosters cultural awareness, community engagement for those interested in the experiences of Black students at Rice. Open to all who share this mission, BSA enriches campus life through programs, support, and cultural events.

name: Chabad Jewish Student Life
description:
Motivated by a profound love and spurred by boundless optimism, Chabad at Rice University sets into motion a dazzling array of programs and services to educate, inspire, and uplift Jewish students from all walks of life with unconditional love and acceptance.


At Chabad, we are all family.

Welcome to your home away from home!

For the most up to date info follow us on IG @chabadatrice
summary:
Chabad is the "Home Away From Home" for every Jewish student.  We welcome Jews from all backgrounds with our signature love of "Ahavat Yisroel".

There are programs, events, and resources for any and every type of student. Connect to be in the know:)

name: Chi Epsilon
description: Chi Epsilon is the civil engineering undergraduate honor society. Chi Epsilon contributes to the improvement of the profession of civil engineering.
summary: Civil engineering undergraduate honor society for professional development.

name: CSters
description:
CSters is dedicated to building a community for students in computing at Rice, with a special focus on supporting aspiring young women. We have over 150 members, ranging from freshmen to graduate students, representing multiple majors — namely Computer Science, but also Electrical Engineering, Statistics, and Computational and Applied Mathematics.
We seek to empower students in technical fields, with an emphasis on supporting women, by providing education, mentorship, outreach, and opportunities. We strive to raise awareness of gender disparities in college and the tech industry by making a positive social impact in our community and by encouraging young students, especially girls, to pursue studies in science and technology. All are welcome who support this mission.
summary: CSters serves as a network and community for students in computing at Rice, with a special focus on supporting women. We empower our members by hosting social events, providing mentoring opportunities, and connecting with corporate partners.

name: Cloud9 Men's Ultimate Frisbee
description:
Cloud 9 is the Rice Men’s Club Ultimate Frisbee team. We practice twice a week and visit several tournaments across the south central region both in the fall and the spring. During the fall season, new players are encouraged to come to practice and tournaments, regardless of experience level, in order to learn to play and bond with the team. Our spring season is more competitive, and culminates with the college postseason (which includes sectionals, regionals, and nationals).

Beyond just practicing together as a team, we form a bond of friendship between the members of the group. If you spend your time at Rice practicing and competing with this team, you'll find yourself a group of lifelong friends.
summary: Cloud9 is the official men's ultimate frisbee club at Rice! We work to create an environment inclusive to everyone, regardless of experience. We hold practices and compete in tournaments to help everyone have a good time playing this sport!

name: Earthquake Engineering Research Institute Rice University Student Chapter
description: It is the objective of this student chapter to encourage, facilitate and promote learning and interest among students in the field of earthquake engineering through interaction with professionals and experts and through interdisciplinary involvement. This chapter will adopt practices that broaden and deepen the knowledge of the chapter members and community on earthquakes, their effects on society and infrastructure, and means of mitigation. It is of special interest for students in the field of civil engineering, earth sciences, architecture, social sciences, etc.
summary: It is the objective of this student chapter to encourage, facilitate and promote learning and interest among students in the field of earthquake engineering through interaction with professionals and experts and through interdisciplinary involvement.

name: Episcopal Church at Rice - Autry House
description:
Episcopal Church at Rice is an organization focused on finding community and love through Christ. We are open to anyone and everyone, no matter your beliefs. You do not have to be Episcopalian or even Christian to come and explore with us. We are 100% LGBTQIA+ affirming and want to create a community where everyone feels welcome and comfortable being themselves. We believe that the most important message to be found in the Bible is one of love for all. 

Join us Mondays at 7pm at Palmer Church for a church service and dinner. Additionally, Wednesdays at noon, we have a catered lunch on campus to catch up with each other and check in. All are welcome!
summary: Autry is here to love and serve alongside the Rice community. We believe that the Gospel of Grace means the love and mercy of Jesus is for everybody. We are an LGBTQIA+ affirming community. We celebrate all of the ways God has made us

name: Hispanic Association for Cultural Enrichment at Rice
description:
Established in 1972, the Hispanic Association for Cultural Enrichment at Rice (HACER) is one of the most visible organizations on campus. We offer many opportunities for all members of the Rice community to enjoy such as cultural, educational, political, service, outreach, and social activities.

HACER is the verb “to do” in Spanish, and that is exactly what we are known for. HACER strives to be an inclusive space for people of Latine descent and for people who want to learn about the beautiful intricacies of Latin American culture, connect with others of Latine descent, and help move the Latine community forward.

Throughout the year, we host a variety of events in an effort to share elements of our culture with the Rice community, support the greater Latine community beyond the hedges, further conversations on Latine and Latine-American issues, and create a cohesive HACER family. We look forward to getting to know you and we hope you join us as we endeavor to make this year one of the most active yet!
summary: HACER is the Rice Latine student association, offering cultural, educational, service, outreach, and social activities for all of the Rice community interested in promoting community and culture.

name: KTRU Rice Radio
description: KTRU is Rice's student-run radio station bringing eclectic programming to the Houston area (96.1 FM) and streaming online at ktru.org. KTRU does concerts every semester including the largest campus music festival, Outdoor Show.
summary: KTRU is Rice's student-run radio station bringing eclectic programming to the Houston area (96.1 FM) and streaming online at ktru.org. KTRU does concerts every semester including the largest campus music festival, Outdoor Show.

name: Marching Owl Band
description:
We are the Rice University Marching Owl Band (aka The MOB, see here for some history), a suit-wearing, fedora-flashing non-marching "marching" band here to have fun. Used to strict, no-nonsense marching bands? Well, forget about that. Completely. We're not like other (girls) bands.

Here's a few important things to know about us:

  • We never march (no band camp!) or memorize our music!
  • We write, produce, and rehearse a new show for every football game and make fun of our opponents with clever, witty jokes!
  • We accommodate all instruments (e.g. accordions, violins, kazoos, etc.) and all skill levels.
  • There are no auditions!
  • We get the job done – Students are behind the scenes writing scripts, announcing, making props, conducting shows, and managing equipment and uniforms! Script and prop sessions are open to everyone!
  • NOTHING IS MANDATORY! We understand and respect that MOBsters can have busy schedules as students, so we’ve got lots of flexibility to help them achieve maximum ‘academic weapon’ potential.

For a given week with a football game on the weekend, we hold four rehearsals: 

  • two indoor music rehearsals on Monday and Tuesday @ the band hall – you can choose one or both!
  • one field rehearsal on Thursday @ the stadium
  • one dress rehearsal-y rehearsal on game day (usually Saturday) @ the stadium

Be involved as much as you want or as little as you need because, as Rice students too, we understand how important your studies are, and we never want to compete with that. Have an Ochem test coming up? Go study, and we'll see you again when you're not fighting for your life in the trenches. Of course, if academics (or life) are stressing you out, we hope the MOB can be a place for you to destress, take your mind off things, and just vibe with us cool people. 😎

Our motto is: Music is too important (and too much fun) to leave it only to professionals and other serious people. It's so important to us it's even a bumper sticker you can get at our band hall. We take being unserious seriously, so come check us out!
summary: The MOB is Rice's scatter band consisting of any and all instruments. We perform at home sporting events and venues across the Houston area!  We're shameless, we're outrageous, we're irreplaceable.

name: Rice University Psi Chi Chapter
description: Psi Chi is meant to provide resources and create a community for students interested in psychology at Rice University.
summary: Psi Chi is meant to provide resources and create a community for students interested in psychology at Rice University.

name: Korean Student Association
description: The Rice University Korean Student Association is dedicated to celebrating and sharing Korean culture, traditions, and language with the Rice community. All students, regardless of background, who want to learn more about Korean culture, enjoy our events, and find community are welcome!
summary: The goal of the Rice University Korean Student Association is to engage all Rice students, regardless of background, who want to learn more about Korean culture by hosting fun events throughout the school year.

name: Rice Biomedical Engineering Society
description:
The Rice Biomedical Engineering Society (BMES) is a professional, academic, and social club that is made up of students interested in biomedical engineering. We seek to provide guidance and information to incoming bioengineering students and build the biomedical engineering community here at Rice University. The club attempts to connect members to bioengineers across the nation working in industry, in medicine, and in academia.

The mission of the Biomedical Engineering Society is to build and support the biomedical engineering community at Rice with activities designed to promote professional development by showcasing perspectives in medicine, industry, and academia. Furthermore, we seek to provide students with research and industry opportunities in bioengineering by organizing events integrating students with professors and recruiters. Through the mentorship program, we provide prospective bioengineering students with resources and advice to make informed academic decisions. Finally, through student, faculty, and alumni bonding events, we further strengthen the connections within our community. The Biomedical Engineering Society chapter at Rice is dedicated to serving the broad interests of bioengineers and promoting their success at Rice.
summary: Rice Biomedical Engineering Society is a professional, academic, and social club. We focus on giving members opportunities in industry, medicine, and academia. We host mentorship, networking, and fun social events. We're open to any major interested!

name: Rice Catalyst
description:
Catalyst is Rice's undergraduate science journal and media organization. We are committed to showcasing student perspectives on popular science topics and undergraduate research, as well as helping students develop scientific communication skills toward a variety of audiences. Catalyst is divided into several sections:

  • Attractions, which focuses on short-form expository writing meant to help the general public engage in scientific discourse.
  • Breakthroughs, which performs interviews with researchers at Rice and the TMC to bring cutting-edge research topics into the public spotlight.
  • Connections, which leads aspiring scientific writers through the peer-reviewed publication process, thus creating full-length journal articles for audiences with scientific backgrounds.
  • Discoveries, which exposes the general public to intriguing scientific topics by publishing brief blog posts on the official Catalyst website.
