  • Design, which works together in a team environment to assemble the final publication in an organized and professional format, which will be published both in print and online.

Rice Catalyst’s mission is to provide our students with a platform to effectively share their interest in science both within and beyond the hedges. Our student staff heavily invests in our writers to provide constructive criticism and share experiences in scientific communication, which fosters an environment in which all students can thrive regardless of their prior writing or research experience. As a member of Catalyst, you will join one or more of our sections to write/edit articles for our annual publication. For more information, please stay on the lookout to attend our annual retreat, or reach out to ricecatalyst@gmail.com.
summary: Catalyst is Rice's undergraduate science journal and media organization. We showcase student perspectives on popular science topics and undergraduate research.

name: Rice Catholic Student Association
description:
The Rice Catholic Student Association equips students to boldly live out their Catholic faith during and beyond college by facilitating encounters with the person of Christ through the sacraments, community, and a personal prayer life.

 

 

Sacraments

The Rice Catholic Student Association and St. Mary's Chapel provide students and the surrounding community with opportunities for many of the key sacraments that make up our faith. Sunday Mass is held at 10 AM, 12 PM, and 5:30 PM at St. Mary's Chapel. All students are welcome to join us for Mass, whether you were raised Catholic, are a recent convert, or are interested in learning more about Catholicism! We also provide regular opportunities for Confession, as well as Daily Mass and Adoration. Finally, students interested in joining the faith are encouraged to reach out about our RCIA program!

 

Community

The Rice CSA provides many opportunities to encounter the person of Christ through community events. First and foremost, we have weekly groups such as Men's Group, Women's Group, Freshmen Fellowship, and Bible Study. These groups encourage students to form strong connections through prayer, reading, and discussion of our faith. Additionally, we host regular fellowship events throughout the year, such as dinners, game nights, and study breaks. Finally, we host Sunday Supper at 6:30 PM after the student Mass, with great food supplied by members of the nearby community! All are welcome to join us as we build a vibrant Catholic community here at Rice!

 

Personal Prayer Life

The Rice Catholic Student Association is also dedicated to helping students find God through their own prayer lives. Our weekly groups, mentioned above, guide students as they consider how faith impacts their own lives. Similarly, our Speaker Series encourages reflection and discussion on important facets of Catholic theology. The Rice CSA also provides monthly service opportunities so that students can embrace Jesus' call to help those in need. We also hold retreats to give students an opportunity to rest, meditate, and pray. Finally, St. Mary's Chapel and the Catholic Student Center are great places to come during the week for a moment alone with God.

 

We encourage all students who are Catholic or interested in the faith to join us for Mass or one of our events! God bless you.
summary: The Rice Catholic Student Association equips students to body live out their Catholic faith during and beyond college by facilitating encounters with the person of Christ through the sacraments, community, and a personal prayer life. All are welcome!

name: Rice Chinese Students and Scholars Association
description: RCSSA is a student organization with over a decade of history, dedicated to promoting Chinese culture and fostering community at Rice University. Through academic, social, and cultural programming—including mixers, retreats, galas, sports, and career development—RCSSA enriches the campus experience for all students interested in Chinese culture. In addition to strengthening connections among Chinese students and scholars, RCSSA collaborates with university offices and other organizations to encourage cultural exchange and mutual understanding across campus
summary: Rice Chinese Students & Scholars Association (RCSSA) is a non-political, non-religious, nonprofit group that enriches student life, promotes cultural exchange, and fosters communication for those interested in Chinese culture and community.

name: Rice Chinese Theater Club
description: As members of the Rice community interested in Chinese Theater, we want to create and build a space for students who share this interest in Chinese culture or/and theater performing arts. Students in this space can experiment with their ideas, learn and develop their skills, make long-term friendships, and share Chinese Theater with the broader community. We produce one performance per semester and welcome all Rice students and people from the Houston area to join us.
summary: We aim to provide students who have an interest in Chinese culture or/and Theater performing arts a space to share, learn and develop their passions and skills, by producing theatre performances once a semester.

name: Rice Chinese Student Association
description: The Rice Chinese Student Association (CSA) seeks to promote social, cultural, and political awareness of Chinese heritage, traditions, and issues in the Rice and Houston communities. Focusing on Chinese traditions and customs and open to all, we provide within the bounds of Rice University a venue for students interested in the Chinese culture to bond and interact. As the oldest and largest Asian cultural organization on campus, the CSA exists to unite all other Asian organizations together to achieve our common goals.
summary: CSA is a cultural, non-partisan, social organization devoted to promoting Chinese culture and awareness of Asian Pacific Islander American (APIA) issues on campus and within the Houston community.

name: Rice Men's Club Lacrosse
description: The Rice Men's Club Lacrosse team will strive for excellence both on and off the field. We aim to be a supportive community to all members, encouraging each player to grow as an athlete individually as well as achieve our highest level of performance in competition as a team.
summary: The Rice Club Lacrosse team strives for excellence both on and off the field. We aim to be a supportive community to all members, encouraging each player to grow as an athlete individually as well as achieve our highest level of performance as a team.

name: Rice Club Tennis
summary: Rice Club Tennis is a student run club where Rice undergraduate and graduate students can compete and improve their tennis skills while developing a community of people who share similar interests and passions in tennis.

name: Rice Men's Club soccer
description: Premier men's soccer team on campus that focuses on hard physical training that translates into solid performances against all of our area's top teams, with goals of competing at national levels through NIRSA.
summary: Rice Men's Club soccer team.

name: Rice Model United Nations
description: A resurging organization on campus, Rice Model United Nations wishes to leverage Rice's vast international relations resources to provide students with a first-hand exploration of their academic/career interests through the United Nations system. Its two defining characteristics are the hosting of a high school-level conference here at Rice and the development of a traveling, competing MUN team. In order to achieve these goals, RiceMUN strives to engage its members, refining their diplomatic speaking and writing skills and employing their leadership capabilities to ensure continuous growth and development.
summary: RiceMUN aims to provide students with an outlet for diplomatic expression through the merger between international relations and student interests. A new organization, RiceMUN strives to provide Rice students with delegate and secretariat opportunities.

name: Rice PRIDE
description: Rice PRIDE seeks to offer support and promote education, awareness and advocacy of LGBTQ+ and Gender, Sexual orientation, and Romantic orientation Minorities (GSRM) issues. We support these individuals by providing a welcoming space for mental and physical well-being. Working in collaboration with the Office of Access and Institutional Excellence (AIE) under the Office of the Provost and other departments and organizations that facilitate access and well-being, we will supply resources and create programming to further campus-wide understanding, harmony, and community. In order to increase the visibility and promote the intersectionality of Rice’s LGBTQ+ and GSRM community, we will reach out to and involve ourselves with local, national, and international social and advocacy organizations. The club welcomes allies and all members of the Rice community who support this mission.
summary: The undergraduate LGBTQ+ organization at Rice University provides students with social, educational, and advocacy resources, and welcomes allies and all members of the Rice community who support this mission.

name: Rice Undergraduate Biosciences Society
description: We are a group of students passionate about anything related to the life sciences (including but not limited to: biochemistry, bioengineering, ecology, earth science, biophysics, chemistry, neuroscience, synthetic biology, medicine, etc). Our goal is to bring together curious scientists at any stage of their journey, from Rice undergraduates (regardless of major), faculty members, graduate schools, Rice alumni, and researchers from Texas Medical Center to engage in events and activities centered around a shared interest in the natural sciences that will broaden our perspectives and further the knowledge of our community.
summary: We hope to foster a community of people with interests related to the natural sciences, all coming from various backgrounds and majors, creating an environment in which people can openly talk about their interests and share their perspectives.

name: Rice University GSA Soccer Club
description:
Contact Information:

If you are interested in joining our Club, please subscribe to our mailing list here (link works ONLY on Microsoft edge and Firefox) to be kept updated about the club's events and info. If you have a specific request, please contact Bjorn Shockey (bs128@rice.edu), Sara Vanaki (sv67@rice.edu), or Jishnu Murukeshan (jm235@rice.edu). 

 

Membership:

The GSA Soccer Club's membership is open to both male and female members of the Rice University community.  Rice University Alumni and members of the community may be allowed to participate at the discretion of the GSA Soccer Club officers and following the club's bylaws.  

 

 

Summer 2025 Schedule:

    1. Wednesdays & Fridays 6 - 8pm from 9/7 to now, Field 3 at Rice University: need to fill in signup sheet.

Summer 2025 Schedule:

    1. Tuesdays & Thursdays 6:30 - 8:30pm from 5/1 to 9/6, Field 6 at Rice University: need to fill in signup sheet.

 

Spring 2024 Schedule:

  1. Tuesday 7pm to 10:30pm from 1/23 to 3/12, Houston Sports Park: Sportskind game (40 mins). FULL
  2. Thursday 6pm to 7pm, Field 3 (Half field): Los GalacticOwls training. FULL
  3. Thursday 6pm to 7pm, Field 3 (Half field): Open pick up game. 
  4. Thursday 7pm to 8:30pm, Field 3: GSA Competitive game. FULL
  5. Friday 6pm to 8:30pm, Field 3: Open pick up game.

 

Past League Participation (Los GalacticOwls):

The Rice GSA Soccer Team (Los GalacticOwls) is participating in the 8v8 outdoor Sportskind league. This season consists of approximately 10 games. Liability waiver form submission is required to play in this team. This team is currently full. 

 

Competitive Game:

The club has started as of last year a competitive team meeting weekly to play an 11v11 game. Commitment is required for these games, to ensure good quality games during the whole semester. Attendance is taken and players who do not show up unannounced will be removed from the roster to make ways to players on the waiting list.

 

Open pick up games:

For some casual pick-up soccer on campus, come to fields outside the Rice gymnasium (Field 3) on Thursdays from 6.00 pm to 7 pm, and on Fridays from 6 pm to 8:30 pm. 

 

2023/2024 club dues:

  • For players playing Sportsking league: $15
  • For players playing within the competitive team: $8 full year or $7 per semester.
  • No fees to participate in pickup games.

 

Equipment:

The GSA Soccer Club will provide equipment for practice and league participation depending on its current budget.  For 2023-2024, the Club's budget will handle the following:

  • League fees
  • Soccer balls, pinnies, cones, hurdles, posts, goals.

For the 2023/2024 season, players/members are responsible for the following:

  • Individual registration fees 
  • Signing the Club Sports Liability Waiver Form if the wish to play a league with the club.
  • Shinguards
  • Soccer shoes
  • Soccer shirt
  • Socks and Shorts

 

Submit Liability Waiver Form

Request to join the OwlNest Club Sports club here. Once you have been accepted to the club, in the "Forms" section, submit the liability waiver. Once done, confirm by email to Romain at rpa4@rice.edu. Any member who hasn't submitted the waiver will not be allowed to play with the team.

 

Next year league participation: 

Starting in the Fall 2024, the club will participate in HFA tournament (division to be defined still). Each player has to be registered with the club and submit the liability waiver in order to participate. Fees to participate have not been settled on yet. We will be recruiting for this league, if you are interested you can contact us.
summary: The purpose of the Graduate Student Association (GSA) Soccer Club is to promote the game of soccer among graduate and undergraduate students as well as faculty and staff members of Rice University.

name: Rice University Rowing Club
summary: The purpose of this organization is to compete in the sport of rowing as a representative of Rice University in the state of Texas and to provide an opportunity that both new and experienced rowers can enjoy.

name: Rice Urban Agriculture
description: We, Rice Urban Agriculture, aim to foster an environment for people to learn how to garden through hands on experience. Rice Urban Agriculture members will learn how to market and manage produce, develop skills to live a healthy lifestyle, and interact with and support the greater Houston community. The purpose of this organization is to teach Rice students about sustainable agriculture and healthy cooking, to provide the Rice Housing and Dining with convenient organic produce, and to make partnerships with various other student-run organizations in order to promote healthy and environmentally sound food on campus.
summary: Rice Urban Agriculture aims to foster an environment for people to learn how to garden through hands on experience. Members will learn to market and manage produce, develop skills to live a healthy lifestyle, and interact with the Houston community.

name: Rice Women's Club Volleyball
description: We are a group of undergraduate and graduate Rice girls who want to play volleyball at a competitive level, compete against other universities, make lasting friendships, and have a great time doing it.
summary: We are a group of undergraduate and graduate Rice girls who want to play volleyball at a competitive level, compete against other universities, make lasting friendships, and have a great time doing it.

name: STEM Students on the Rise
description: STEM Students on the Rise is a dynamic student club focused on nurturing the growth and empowerment of aspiring scholars in the fields of Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM). We are deeply committed to fostering talent and providing vital support and opportunities to students as they navigate the complexities of STEM education. Through the organization of informative seminars tailored for both high school and college participants, we offer guidance in academic and professional development. Additionally, our dedicated efforts in curating diverse internship opportunities within STEM domains underscore our mission to propel young minds toward success. Notably, we organized a convention last year that brought together college students from across the nation, aiming to redefine traditional notions of success, with a pronounced focus on mental health and holistic well-being. These various initiatives collectively establish STEM Students on the Rise as an inspirational force, guiding the next generation of innovative STEM leaders. By forging new partnerships, developing innovative programs, and staying attuned to the evolving needs of our members, we are dedicated to empowering aspiring scholars on their path to becoming the pioneering leaders of tomorrow's STEM landscape.
summary: STEM Students on the Rise is a student club dedicated to providing support and opportunities for students studying in the STEM fields.

name: Rice Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics
description: Rice SIAM is the Rice chapter of the Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics. Rice SIAM serves as a professional and social organization for students in the CMOR department as well as students with interests in industrial and applied mathematics.
summary: Rice SIAM is the Rice chapter of the Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics. Rice SIAM serves as a professional and social organization for students in the CMOR department as well as students with interests in industrial and applied mathematics.

name: Graduate Students Transforming Rice Into a Violence-free Environment
description:
Vision
We envision a world that is free from inequality and violence, where every person experiences sexuality and relationships in healthy and positive ways.
 
Mission
Engaging in positive open dialogue about gender-based inequality and interpersonal violence while providing tools and skills necessary to create cultural change for graduate students. 
 
Graduate STRIVE liaisons can support you with issues and concerns about:

  • Off/On campus sexual harassment
  • Concerns about Title IX policies
  • Interpersonal Violence
  • Stalking
  • Discrimination based on gender, sex, or pregnancy
  • Dynamics of Healthy Relationships
summary: Grad STRIVE is a dedicated group of grad students who have come together to address sexual and domestic violence on campus. The Coalition is advised by The SAFE Office.

name: Physics and Astronomy Graduate Student Association
description: PAGSA is a Physics and Astronomy graduate student-led organization that strives to provide an inclusive community in the Physics and Astronomy department and provide support to students and early career scientists. To fulfill this goal, PAGSA organizes events including holiday parties, casual social events, journal clubs, mentoring events, professional development panels, outreach, and more. PAGSA leaders serve as representatives for the student body to ensure effective communication with the rest of the department.
summary: The mission of PAGSA is to provide support and community to graduate students in the Physics and Astronomy department at Rice University.

name: Design for America-Rice University
description: Design for America (DFA) is a nationwide network of interdisciplinary student teams and community members using design to create local and social impact. We teach human- centered design to young adults and collaborating community partners through extra- curricular, university-based, student-led design studios tackling national challenges in the areas of education, health, economy and the environment. DFAers represent diverse academic fields such as engineering, design, psychology, and economics. DFA believes that all students have the capacity to use design to innovate the world around them and DFA is a platform to do so.
summary: Design for America is a social innovation club that aims to create a local and social impact. We teach human-centered design to our members and collaborate with organization in Houston.

name: Korean International Student Association
description: Korean International Student Association (KISA) strives to enhance the overall well-being for students interested in Korean culture and issues by facilitating improved career prospects and fostering valuable connections through various social events including Welcome back Party, Thanksgiving Potluck Party, Field Day, Seniors Farewell Party, and most importantly Korean Culture Night. At its core, KISA serves as a cohesive community wherein members find support during challenging times. There are more events to come so stay tuned!
summary: The Korean International Student Association (KISA) fosters community and supports students interested in Korean culture. We promote well-being, career growth, and connections through social events that celebrate community and strengthen relationships.

name: Rice Graduate Christian Fellowship
description:
In response to God's love, grace and truth:

Open to all, RGCF establishes and advances at Rice University a witnessing community of students and faculty who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord: growing in love for God, God's Word, God's people of every ethnicity and culture and God's purposes in the world.
summary: In response to God's love, grace and truth: the purpose of RGCF is to establish & advance at Rice University a witnessing community of students and faculty who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord: growing in love for God, God's Word, & all of God's people.

name: TEDxRiceU
description: TEDxRiceU is an independently organized TEDx club at Rice University that organizes salons where students and faculty at Rice have opportunities to speak about their experiences and ideas. In addition, we organize a large-scale TED-style event every year in the spring semester. We recruit speakers and performers from the Houston area to bring their innovation, ideas, and perspectives from various disciplines together in a celebration of continued education. Our mission is to foster communication and expose the audience to the amazing ideas coming from Rice University and the greater Houston communities.
summary: TEDxRiceU is an independently organized TEDx club at Rice University that organizes TED-style events throughout the year with a large-scale event in the spring semester. We recruit speakers and performers from the Houston area to foster creativity.

name: Rice HOSA Future Health Professionals
description:
HOSA is an international student organization recognized by the U.S. Department of Education and the Health Science Education (HSE) Division of ACTE. HOSA's two-fold mission is to promote career opportunities in the health care industry and to enhance the delivery of quality health care to all people. HOSA provides a unique program of leadership development, motivation, and recognition for secondary, postsecondary, adult, and collegiate students enrolled in health science education and biomedical science programs or have interests in pursuing careers in health professions. 

 

hosa.org
summary: Rice HOSA enables aspiring pre-health students to explore their interests in healthcare through academic competition in medical knowledge events, professional and leadership development, volunteering opportunities, and collaboration with other students.

name: Rice Iranian Society
description: A cultural GSA dedicated to creating a warm and welcoming environment for all students on Rice campus interestd in Iran, showcasing Iranian culture to the student body, and fostering social ties with the Iranian-American community in the Greater Houston area.
summary: A cultural GSA dedicated to creating a warm and welcoming environment for all students on Rice campus interested in Iran, showcasing Iranian culture to the student body, and fostering social ties with the Iranian-American community in Greater Houston.

name: Indian Students at Rice
description: Rice University's ISAR (Indian Students at Rice) is a secular, non-partisan graduate student organization dedicated to creating a warm and welcoming environment for Indian students on Rice campus, showcasing Indian culture to the student body, and fostering social ties with the Indian-American community in the Greater Houston area.
summary: A cultural GSA dedicated to creating a warm and welcoming environment for Indian students on Rice campus, showcasing Indian culture to the student body, and fostering social ties with the Indian-American community in the Greater Houston area.

name: Rice Health Advisors
description: Rice Health Advisors share a mission of peer mentorship and education about mental health and well-being. As members of this advising team, RHAs serve as liaisons between the Wellbeing and Counseling Center and Rice students searching for mental health support by encouraging conversations about mental health and helping students navigate the available care and support our team and the Wellbeing and Counseling Center can offer. As active members of the Rice community, we aim to dispel any stigma associated with mental health issues by facilitating open, constructive, and ongoing discussion about mental health, and being confidants for those who simply seek guidance.
summary: Rice Health Advisors share a mission of peer mentorship and education about mental health and well-being. We serve Rice University students at each college as a resource for physical supplies and overall wellbeing.

name: Rice African Student Association
description: The mission of the Rice African Student Association is to encourage a spirit of unity, curiosity, and respect amongst its members and the campus community. We will do so by actively promoting the diverse cultures, histories, and nuances found on the African continent through events and collaborations with like-minded groups and individuals. We will also work to serve members of our immediate Houston community and, similarly, our extended community on the African continent. We will strive to provide a network of support for all members, linking them to other students, alumni, faculty, and leaders in the community as needed. Finally, we will support each other in academics, athletics, and all social aspects of life, truly exuding the essence of the RASA family.
summary: Rice African Student Association's goal is to promote awareness of the African continent and culture, as well as provide a support network of alumni, faculty, and community leaders for RASA members.

name: Basmati Beats
description: Basmati Beats is Rice University's premier South Asian fusion A Cappella group. We perform mashups of South Asian and Western music in an A Cappella style, sharing our passion for musical innovation through campus performances and national competitions. Open to all students passionate about music, performance, and cross-cultural expression.
summary: Basmati Beats is Rice's premier South Asian fusion A Cappella group. We blend South Asian & Western music in creative mashups, performing on campus and at national competitions. Open to all students passionate about music & cross-cultural expression.

name: Caribbean Student Society
description: The Caribbean Student Association builds community among students interested in Caribbean cultures. Through programs, celebrations, and events, we share the richness of the Caribbean with Rice and Houston. Open to all students who support this mission.
summary: The Caribbean Student Association builds community among students interested in Caribbean cultures. Through programs, celebrations, and events, we share the richness of the Caribbean with Rice and Houston. Open to all students who support this mission.

name: Low Keys
summary: The Low Keys is Rice University's only SSAA a cappella group on campus. The group consists of 12-16 members with Soprano or Alto range voices and rehearses 3 hours a week. The Low Keys perform at concerts, charity events, and other gigs year round.

name: Rice Pre-Dental Society
description: The Rice Pre-Dental Society is an undergraduate student-based organization aiming to educate all Rice students about the profession of dentistry and provide resources in support for those interested in pursuing dentistry.
summary: The Rice Pre-Dental Society is an undergraduate student-based organization aiming to educate all Rice students about the profession of dentistry and provide resources in support for those interested in pursuing dentistry.

name: Rice All-Stars
description: Rice All-Stars is a student organization dedicated to increasing awareness and decreasing stigma around disability related issues and to foster an overall environment of inclusion at Rice University. We do this by playing basketball with individuals with disabilities in the Houston area. Our mission is to provide individuals with special needs in the Houston area with opportunity to play team sports in order to bolster their self confidence, social skills, and overall well-being.
summary: Rice All-Stars is a student organization dedicated to increasing awareness and decreasing stigma surrounding disabilities. We do this by playing basketball with individuals with disabilities in the Houston area.

name: Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints Student Association
description:
LDSSA hosts meetings to discuss the doctrines of the Gospel of Jesus Christ. It is our hope that we can create a space for students at Rice to grow together socially and spiritually. Students of all faiths are welcome to our weekly meetings! 

For further questions, contact LDSSA President Lucy Rixner at lr63@rice.edu
summary: LDSSA hosts meetings to discuss the doctrines of the Gospel of Jesus Christ. It is our hope that we can create a space for students at Rice to grow together socially and spiritually. All students are welcome to our weekly meetings!

name: Rice Business Society
description: To connect and develop Rice undergraduate business students as future leaders in business by creating professional relationships with recruiting companies, hosting events to connect a community of like-minded students interested in business, and providing technical workshops for interview preparation.
summary:
Educate undergraduate students on business careers and opportunities 
Connect Rice undergraduates to internships and job opportunities
Unite the undergraduate business community
Develop successful future business leaders

name: Rice Computer Science Club
description: An organization dedicated to providing Rice students with a tight-knit community of friends and mentors to promote and advocate computer science and working with tech. Community events include study breaks, social outings, and meet and greets between different classes. We also provide many professional resources for members including company-sponsored informational and recruiting events, resume review and interview prep sessions, panels, and tech workshops. One of our most popular initiatives is HackRice, Rice's annual hackathon, and we also host CS Beer Debates in the spring. There are no membership fees or annual dues.
summary: A club that serves to foster, build up, and be a resource for the computer science community at Rice.

name: Owlchemy: Rice Undergraduate Chemistry Society
description: Owlchemy: The Rice Undergraduate Chemistry Society functions as a means to connect students studying chemistry and other adjacent fields. Our club seeks to engage and facilitate student to student and student to mentor interactions. Our main mission as a club is to improve the overall academic and social interaction of both incoming freshmen and upperclassmen while promoting the study of chemistry.
summary: Owlchemy: RUCS is the undergraduate chemistry organization on campus. We seek to promote the study of chemistry in the community through outreach and foster student-student interaction and student-faculty interaction on campus.

name: Rice Neuroscience Society
description: Rice Neuroscience Society (RNS) is for anyone interested in neuroscience! We host a variety of events on the interdisciplinary field of neuroscience, provide advising and networking resources for NEUR majors/minors, and organize outreach events to engage with the greater Houston community. You do not need to be a NEUR major/minor to join!
summary: Rice Neuroscience Society serves as the voice of the neuroscience major and helps increases awareness for neuroscience opportunities both at Rice and in the greater Houston area.

name: Brazilian Student Association at Rice University
description: BRASA is a student-led organization that promotes Brazilian culture, fosters community, and builds connections across campus and beyond. Through cultural events, professional partnerships, and outreach, BRASA provides a welcoming space for students interested in Brazil’s social, artistic, and academic contributions. Open to all, the organization encourages cross-cultural dialogue and collaboration with universities and sectors connected to Brazilian society.
summary:
The Brazilian Student Association - BRASA - is an organization created by the students of Rice University with the purpose of spreading Brazilian culture to the university
community and also providing academic and professional enhancement to its members

name: American Association of Petroleum Geologists
description: To facilitate interactions between professional geologists and students, the Rice AAPG student chapter facilitates a number of events each year. For example, we host seminars, social hours, and recruitment events, and organize field trips to create an active dialogue between oil and gas professionals and the students within the Department of Earth, Environmental, and Planetary Sciences.
summary: The primary role of the American Association of Petroleum Geologists student chapter at Rice University is to facilitate interactions between students and oil and gas professionals.This occurs through networking events, lectures and field trips.

name: PERIOD@Rice
description: PERIOD@Rice is a menstrual equity club that works to serve and advocate for menstruators on Rice's campus and beyond. The club hosts menstrual product donation drives, panels, letter writing to representatives, and engaging campus activities. All are welcome who support this mission.
summary: PERIOD@Rice is a menstrual equity club advocating for menstruators on campus and beyond. We host product drives, panels, letter-writing campaigns, and engaging activities to promote equity and awareness. Open to all who support this mission.

name: Society of Petroleum Engineers
description: SPE's mission is to collect, disseminate, and exchange technical knowledge concerning the exploration, development, and production of oil and gas resources and related technologies for the public benefit; and to provide opportunities for professionals to enhance their technical and professional competence. SPE accomplishes this mission through a schedule of international conferences, exhibitions, workshops, and short courses; publication of journals, books, and electronic media; and section programs.
summary: SPE's mission is to collect, disseminate, and exchange technical knowledge concerning the exploration, development, and production of oil and gas resources and related technologies for the public benefit; and to provide opportunities for professionals.

name: Ecology and Evolutionary Biology GSA
description: Departmental GSA for Ecology and Ecolutionary Biology.
summary: Departmental GSA for Ecology and Ecolutionary Biology.

name: Jewish Studies Student Association
description:
Mission Statement. The Jewish Studies Student Association (JSSA) is a student-led organization. The JSSA strives to serve as a bridge between professors and students by facilitating communication between the Jewish Studies Program and students who are interested in the program. By organizing events such as museum tours, food socials, and cultural events, the JSSA hopes to enlighten Rice students about Judaism, Jewish culture, and other related topics. We hope to expand our student board and develop our presence at Rice in the years to come!

 

Purpose: The purpose of this organization shall be:

 

To bridge the gap between the professors of the Jewish Studies Program and the students of Rice University; and

 

To provide students with the opportunity to discuss questions regarding Judaism, Jewish culture and other related topics of interest pertaining to Jewish Studies as an academic discipline.
summary: The JSSA strives to serve as a bridge between professors and students by facilitating communication between the Jewish Studies Program and students who are interested in the program.

name: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship
description:
In response to God’s love, grace, and truth:

The purpose of InterVarsity Christian Fellowship/USA
is to establish and advance
at colleges and universities
witnessing communities of students and faculty
who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord:
growing in love for God,
God’s Word,
God’s people of every ethnicity and culture,
and God’s purposes in the world.
summary:
InterVarsity is an inter-denominational Christian fellowship seeking to grow in love for God, God’s Word,
God’s people of every ethnicity and culture, and God’s purposes in the world.

name: Rice Esports
description:
To get discord invite, sign in and find the Membership Process & Requirements section

The purpose of this organization shall include, but is not limited to:

    1. provide a forum for members of the Rice community to discuss, practice, and compete in various esports.
    2. creating opportunities for people to meet others with a similar interest in Esports.
    3. acting as a gateway for competitive players to participate in collegiate tournaments.
    4. providing a gaming atmosphere for events such as viewing parties or tournaments.
    5. promoting socialization and communication as a fun aspect of playing esports.
    6. creating a source for players to get help from club members if they want to improve.
summary: Objective is to create opportunities for people with similar interests to meet. Provide gaming atmosphere for events such as viewing parties/tournaments. Creating a source of communication and support for those looking to improve.

name: Moody Student Collaborative
description:
The Moody Student Collaborative seeks to bring arts into the everyday lives of Rice students and to ensure the Moody Center for the Arts is responsive to and inclusive of Rice's diverse student body.

The Collaborative organizes interdisciplinary events such as workshops, art openings, studio visits, and study breaks to make the art community of Houston and beyond more accessible on campus.

The purpose of the Collaborative is to organize events and experiences where Rice’s undergraduate population can gain hands-on experience in the arts, help the Moody foster student engagement through outreach, and shape student-focused events.
summary: This organization acts as a bridge between the Moody Center and students by organizing different events where students can take advantage of all of the different resources the Moody has to offer.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description: The Rice University chapter of ASCE (American Society of Civil Engineers) provides students who are interested in civil engineering as a career with a multitude of opportunities to enrich themselves and learn about the industry. Competitions, social events, and professional presentations are all held within the club to allow students to grow and reach out to their peer students, professors, and professionals in the world of civil engineering.
summary: The Rice University chapter of ASCE (American Society of Civil Engineers) provides students who are interested in civil engineering as a career with a multitude of opportunities to enrich themselves and learn about the industry.

name: French Club
description: French Club hopes to connect Rice Owls through French language, culture, and food! We serve to provide a support network for Francophone and Francophile students at Rice through cultural immersion events, weekly language table, career networking events, cooking lessons, and more. We hope that our activities help Francophones of all skill levels to develop their language abilities and join a network of students, alumni, and faculty across campus!
summary: French Club is a place to celebrate French language, culture, and food! We connect Francophone students at Rice through cultural excursions in Houston, weekly language table, TV and film screenings, cooking events, and more. Bienvenue, nos amis!

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description: Rice University's chapter of National Society of Black Engineers works to provide academic and professional development for black engineers on campus. We partner with companies and various departments on-campus to ensure students feel supported in all ways throughout their undergraduate career. We sponsor students to attend conferences where they can attend career fairs and professional development workshops. We also host events on campus with other engineering clubs and different companies in Houston and beyond.
summary: To increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

name: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics
description:
oSTEM is an undergraduate organization focusing on the professional development and community of LGBTQ students in STEM and STEM adjacent fields. All are welcome who support this mission.

Club listserv: TBD
summary: oSTEM is an undergraduate organization focusing on the professional development and community of LGBTQ students in STEM and STEM adjacent fields. All are welcome who support this mission.

name: Rice Pancakes for Parkinson's
description:
Rice Pancakes for Parkinson's (P4P) hosts an annual pancake breakfast fundraiser in the Grand Hall/Ray Courtyard to raise money to support the Houston Area Parkinson Society. The annual pancake breakfast involves breakfast food, student performances, speeches from Parkinson's patients, professors and the Houston Area Parkinson Society. We also host a medical professionals panel, an educational conference, and a cross-organizational cultural event called Pancakes around the World. The events are planned by a board of Rice undergraduate students chosen by the chair and vice-chairs from applications submitted at the beginning of the year. We also have an initiative called the P4P Volunteers Program through which we engage Rice undergraduate in raising awareness about Parkinson’s disease through monthly interactions with Parkinson’s patients and local community organizations in Houston.

Visit our website for more info! http://ricep4p.blogs.rice.edu/
summary: Pancakes for Parkinson's is an outreach organization that hosts an annual pancake breakfast fundraiser to raise money to support the Houston Area Parkinson Society. We also volunteer and host other events to promote awareness on campus.

name: Rice Partnership for the Advancement and Immersion of Refugees
description:
The Partnership for the Advancement and Immersion of Refugees, otherwise known as PAIR, is a Houston non-profit supporting refugees through their K-12 education. Originally beginning as an organization at Rice, we have now expanded to become a registered 501c3, including multiple other collegiate chapters. 

By bridging the hurdles displacement brings for 400 students annually through mentoring, tutoring, college access, and leadership development programs, we hope to offer our students safety and inclusion. We would always love further volunteers, so if you are interested please click the following link to apply: https://airtable.com/appVYpmk9wm0JmE6K/shrlvw0vYED3IAfpk.

We look forward to working with you all to support our refugee youth!
summary: We aim to bring greater awareness to and serve the refugee population of the Houston community. If you are interested in learning about different cultures or teaching/leading in a classroom setting, we highly encourage you to join us as a volunteer!

name: Office of International Students and Scholars
description: Rice University’s Office of International Students & Scholars' (OISS) mission is to enhance the experience of the international population (which includes all non-immigrant visa students, researchers and faculty) through serving as the chief liaison office for: immigration advising, cultural adaptation, international compliance, networking / public connections, and academic support, with university departments, government agencies, and community advocates.
summary: Rice University’s Office of International Students & Scholars' (OISS) mission is to enhance the experience of the international population (which includes all non-immigrant visa students, researchers and faculty).

name: Rice Outdoor Programs & Education
description: ROPE provides the entire university with opportunities to get outside, explore, and learn. ROPE adventure trips include climbing, canoeing, backpacking and more. Our trips are student led and range from beginner friendly to challenging. Each trip is designed to provide participants with the primary equipment and instruction to help them learn and be successful on on the trail. Expedition style trips take place during recesses and often travel across or outside of Texas. The Outdoor Adventure Center also provides an outfitting service to equip for your own adventure trips. We carry everything necessary for a canoe, kayak or backpacking expedition as well as options for a traditional car camping trip.
summary: ROPE provides Rice with opportunities to get outside, explore, and learn. Our adventure trips are student led and run on weekends and recesses throughout semester. The outdoor adventure center offers rental services to outfit your personal trips.

name: Department of Art History
description: The Department of Art History at Rice University is a place where images, architecture, and works of art are examined with open minds and attentive eyes. Our core faculty includes experts in the art and architecture of the Americas, Asia, and Europe, spanning from antiquity through contemporary times. Across these diverse fields, we look beyond categories of period and style to ask essential questions about what images, built structures, and works of art do, and what we do with them in turn.
summary: The Department of Art History at Rice University is a place where images, architecture, and works of art are examined with open minds and attentive eyes.

name: Professional Science Master's Programs
description: Complete a bachelor's and master's degree back-to-back in 5 years. The Professional Science Master's program deepens a student's knowledge beyond a bachelor's level and integrates study in the sciences, engineering, and mathematics with knowledge and training in management, communication, leadership, policy and ethics, rounding out the program with a required practice work experience. MS degrees offered in: -Bioscience & Health Policy -Environmental Analysis -Space Studies -Subsurface Geoscience -Applied Chemical Sciences (available Fall 2020) Benefits: -only 1 year of graduate school tuition -GRE scores waived -Application fee waived -Only 1 letter of recommendation is required
summary: 4+1 MS degree in Bioscience & Health Policy, Environmental Analysis, Subsurface Geoscience, and Space Studies

name: Camp Kesem Rice
description:
Kesem is the leading national nonprofit supporting children facing a parent’s cancer. Founded in 2000, Kesem’s community consists of more than 5,000 volunteer college student leaders at over 130 college chapters across the country, providing year-round programs and services for children ages 6-18 at no cost to families. Its flagship program, Camp Kesem, is a week-long, sleep-away summer camp that aims to ensure that every child impacted by a parent's cancer is never alone.

Kesem at Rice was founded in 2014 and supports children in the greater Houston community by providing a free week-long summer camp experience and year-round peer support. Kesem at Rice is entirely operated by over 100 passionate college students and serves more than 100 children ages 6-18 per year. For more information about Kesem at Rice please visit kesem.org/chapters/texas or instagram.com/kesemrice.
summary: Kesem at Rice is the Houston chapter of the leading national nonprofit that provides free summer camps and year-round support for children impacted by a parent's cancer.

name: OwlNest Student Engagement Consultants
description:
OwlNest Student Engagement Consultants help students find clubs or events that match their interests and engage on campus and serve as a resource for clubs, colleges, and departments to enhance their OwlNest pages.

 

Learn more by watching out OwlNest 101 video! (Closed captions available)

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XvDe0Dzr-BU

 

Want a personalized consultation or group training session? Fill out the form below!

https://owlnest.rice.edu/submitter/form/start/400610
summary: OwlNest Student Engagement Consultants help students find clubs or events that match their interests and engage on campus, and serve as a resource for clubs, colleges and departments to enhance their OwlNest pages.

name: The Rice University Transfer Student Association
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be:

III.A      To foster personal growth and adaptation of transfer students to the social and academic environment at Rice;

III.B      To provide academic and social support during Orientation Week in partnership with the Office of Academic Advising and First Year Programs respectively;

III.C      To provide opportunities for Rice students to address problems of transfer students within the school;

III.D     To be a unifying voice on campus for transfer students and those who are concerned about issues they face in order to be a stronger advocate for them on campus;

 

Check us out on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/riceclassoftransfers
summary: As new members of the Rice community, transfer students come in with unique perspectives and needs. Our goal is to foster integration into the community by hosting social events, addressing student needs, and representing special interests.

name: Office of Information Technology
description:
Service Information Only (not a joinable OwlNest group)

The Office of Information Technology (OIT) is the university’s central technology department for providing research, academic, and administrative systems. If you have computing questions or need advice, reach out to the OIT Help Desk.

 

Services:

https://oit.rice.edu

Getting Started in IT for Undergraduate Students: https://kb.rice.edu/103785

OIT KnowledgeBase:

https://kb.rice.edu

The OIT Help Desk is the centralized contact for information technology support services including account management, connectivity to Rice network, password resets, general troubleshooting, mobile device support, VPN configuration, email setup, and more. We offer phone and online support.

 

Phone: 713-348-HELP (4357)

Online Requests:

Form: https://oit.rice.edu/request-help

Email: helpdesk@rice.edu

 

In-person Tech Support for Students by Students

Employed by OIT to offer in-person technical support to undergraduates, Student Computing Consultants can address general inquiries and issues pertaining to software applications in PC and Mac computing environments. Locations and hours can be accessed via SCC Student Technical Support.
summary: The Office of Information Technology (OIT) provides computing services and resources to support the educational needs of students including purchasing advice or technical troubleshooting of personal computers.

name: Rice D2K | Data to Knowledge Lab
description: The Rice D2K Lab is a hub for data science education. We provide students with engagement, enrichment, and experiential learning opportunities by connecting students with real-world data science challenges from companies, community organizations and researchers.
summary: The D2K Lab provides students with immersive, experiential learning opportunities in data science while enhancing data-intensive research at Rice and building partnerships with companies, institutions, and community organizations.

name: Rice University Bands
description: The Rice University Band Department includes the Rice Symphonic Band, the Rice Jazz Ensembles, and The MOB (Marching Owl Band). All Rice students are admitted to all organizations in the Band Department regardless of major, and Rice Bands include both graduate students and undergraduates. The Symphonic Band and Jazz Ensembles can be taken for credit. The Rice University Bands' mission is to provide an instrumental music program of the highest quality for the benefit of the entire Rice Community.
summary: Performing ensembles for students of every major. Rice Symphonic Band, Rice Jazz Ensembles, and the home of The MOB.

name: Student Activities/President’s Programming (SAPP) Fund
description:
The Student Activities/President’s Programming (SAPP) Fund provides financial support for registered student club events on campus. All registered student clubs must adhere to the policies and procedures of Rice University and the Office of Student Activities when using SAPP funds. Funding is not guaranteed, so applications should be submitted early and applications should be thoughtfully completed to increase the likelihood of approval.  For more information, please see the SAPP Rules and Guidelines.

 

Application Deadlines:

  • Fall Semester
      • Sunday, September 14, 2025
      • Sunday, October 5, 2025
      • Sunday, November 2, 2025
  • Spring Semester
    • Sunday, February 1, 2026
    • Sunday, March 1, 2026
    • Sunday, March 29, 2026

To apply for SAPP funding, scroll down to the Forms section on this OwlNest page and select the SAPP Round application that corresponds with your event date. Each SAPP Round has its own application, and you’ll be able to see the deadline in the application title. Remember, it's never too early to apply!
summary: The Student Activities President’s Programming Fund (SAPP) supports registered student clubs in hosting engaging and impactful on-campus events, fostering community and enhancing the student experience.

name: Susanne M. Glasscock School of Continuing Studies
description: The Susanne M. Glasscock School of Continuing Studies extends Rice University’s commitment to educational equity and access by offering lifelong and life-changing personal and professional development programs to the Houston community and beyond. As the knowledge economy evolves, we use the degree, non-degree, and certificate space as ground for innovating higher education and creating more access to relevant, personalized and professional courses. Our dynamic, 21st-century learning environment extends far outside the classroom and gives every individual—from pre-school to post-career and all points between—the opportunity to benefit from the educational excellence of Rice University.
summary: The Susanne M. Glasscock School of Continuing Studies furthers Rice University’s commitment to educational outreach by providing lifelong personal and professional development opportunities to the greater Houston community and beyond.

name: Student Wellbeing Office
description: Here in the Student Wellbeing Office, we connect with students in the residential colleges, graduate apartments, and other student spaces around campus to give advice and practical support to help students resolve personal challenges, such as conflicts with friends, difficulty making decisions, struggling with your identity, and academic concerns or problems that are more serious in nature. We also consult with students, faculty, and staff who have concerns about a particular student in distress.
summary: The Student Wellbeing Office supports student development and success by providing a good first point of contact for students who want to talk to someone about solutions to their wellbeing and mental health concerns.

name: Center for Teaching Excellence
description:
The CTE has its roots in the priorities for Rice University outlined in President David Leebron’s “Vision for the Second Century” (V2C), where he emphasized the centrality of outstanding teaching to our mission as an institution of higher learning.

The idea for establishing a CTE then gained some traction in January of 2012 at a campus-wide discussion on teaching that was held in conjunction with Rice 2032, a series of conversations focused on different aspects of the V2C. Following this event, John Hutchinson, the Dean of Undergraduates and an award-winning teacher of Chemistry, convened Rice’s winners of the Piper Professorship to discuss what a CTE at Rice might look like. Building off of these discussions, Hutchinson garnered support from across the campus and, with a group of Faculty Fellows who had a demonstrated history of exceptional teaching, officially launched the CTE in the fall of 2012.
summary: The Center for Teaching Excellence at Rice University fosters outstanding teaching through evidence-based practices, strong community building, and research in pedagogical sciences.

name: George R. Brown School of Engineering and Computing
description: The George R. Brown School of Engineering at Rice University has earned a place among the top schools in the country by partnering with the sciences, humanities, social sciences, architecture and business to ensure that our solutions are developed within the context of an increasingly complex world. Today the school is comprised of nine academic departments, with research that's enriched by 22 engineering-related institutes and centers. About 33 percent of Rice undergraduates are engineering majors and approximately 34 percent of graduate students are in the School of Engineering. Among the more than 140 engineering faculty are numerous members of the National Academies, including eight in engineering, three in science and two in medicine. Three emeritus faculty are also members. Our approximately 1,500 undergraduate and 1,000 graduate students have easy access to professors in the classroom and also work closely with them in their labs. Several departments have active industrial affiliates programs, and many research projects are undertaken with local companies. Students benefit from these relationships through collaborative design and research projects, summer internships, and securing post-graduation jobs.
summary: Rice Engineering's nine academic departments and more than 20 institutes and centers provide a vibrant environment for research and education to help develop the next generation of leaders in engineering and beyond.

name: Caregiving Program
description: The Caregiving program is a peer lead, volunteer organization whose goal is to promote safety and accountability at registered public parties. Much like designated drivers choose to refrain from drinking to make sure their friends get home safe, caregivers volunteer to be sober and respond if someone at a party is in need and get them connected with the appropriate resource. Caregivers are NOT a substitute for REMS or RUPD. Caregivers receive training where they learn the basic signs of intoxication, warning signs of an alcohol emergency and how to seek help. Trainings are held and organized by Head Caregivers within each college. If you would like additional information regarding the Caregiving program, please email wellbeing@rice.edu or contact your college’s head caregiver.
summary: The Caregiving program is a peer lead, volunteer organization whose goal is to promote safety and accountability at registered public parties. Caregivers volunteer to be sober and respond if someone at a party is in need.

name: Rice Business Events
description: Events at Rice University's Jones Graduate School of Business
summary: Events at Rice University's Jones Graduate School of Business

name: Center for the Study of Women, Gender, and Sexuality
description: Established in 2006 as an outgrowth of the Program for the Study of Women, Gender, and Sexuality, the Center for the Study of Women, Gender and Sexuality at Rice encompasses interdisciplinary academic programs and research intensive opportunities.  To learn more, visit our website.
summary: Our Center supports the study of women, gender, and sexuality through hosting public conversations, academic programs, and research opportunities.

name: Rice University Club Sports
description:
Club sports are special interest groups organized to engage in and promote interest in a recreational physical activity. These groups are formed primarily to increase individual and team skills and to augment knowledge through a continuing instructional and competitive program. All students who are enrolled at Rice University are eligible to participate in the program. Faculty and staff members are also eligible to participate in the club sports program unless the league to which a particular club belongs limits involvement to students. Under the supervision of the Recreation Center, the University makes its facilities and services available to participating clubs.

 

To view the current Club Sports Point System, click here:

Club Sports Point System (2024-2025)
