name: Rice Design
description: Rice Design is a student organization dedicated to connecting, educating, and celebrating digital designers.
summary: Rice Design is a student organization dedicated to connecting, educating, and celebrating digital designers.

name: Students at Rice Committed to Optimizing Medical, Exercise, and Rehabilitation Education
summary: Rice University's premier club for Kinesiology-Sports Medicine majors dedicated to the education of medical, exercise and rehabilitation education. Members have the opportunity to learn, network and even compete along with the club.

name: Rice Women in Entrepreneurship
description: Rice Women in Entrepreneurship is a professional organization that aims to expand leadership, networking, and learning opportunities, with a particular focus on opportunities for undergraduate women at Rice. Our mission is to create a collaborative, tight-knit community of entrepreneurial women by equipping our members with the resources to advance professionally and personally. We host a variety of career/industry panels, networking opportunities, and community events throughout the year. Open to all undergraduates at Rice interested in entrepreneurship who support this mission.
summary: Rice Women in Entrepreneurship is a professional organization that aims to expand leadership, networking, and learning opportunities, focusing on undergraduate women. Open to all Rice undergraduates interested in entrepreneurship who share this mission.

name: Across All Borders
description: Our organization aims to minimize the language barriers that exist within the Rice community, especially with Rice staff. We hope to bring a community of different language speakers together, and help each other strengthen their English while building a sense of connection and belonging.
summary: Our organization aims to minimize the language barriers that exist within the Rice community, especially with Rice staff. We hope to bring a community of different language speakers together and help strengthen their English while building connections.

name: National Organization of Minority Architecture Students Rice Chapter
description: NOMAS Rice aims to empower students to foster justice and equity in communities of color through architecture, outreach, community advocacy, professional development, and design excellence. NOMAS Rice is a student chapter of the professional organization that is the National Organization of Minority Architects (NOMA).
summary: The purpose of NOMAS is to connect Rice students to a larger network of representation in architecture; all those interested in this mission are welcome.

name: RAW.photography
description: RAW.photography is a community for photographers to connect, collaborate, learn, and have fun. We host many events, workshops, and photography trips throughout the semester. Photographers at all levels are welcomed. Visit: https://linktr.ee/Rice.RAW
summary: RAW.photography is a community for photographers to connect, collaborate, learn, and have fun. We host many events, workshops, and photography trips throughout the semester. Photographers at all levels are welcomed. Visit: https://linktr.ee/Rice.RAW

name: Rice Black Women's Association
description: Formerly known as Black Girl Magic, the Rice Black Women's Association (RBWA) is an organization focused on the empowerment of Black women. All are welcome who support this mission. RBWA facilitates connections and relationships between current and past members and Rice alumni., while promoting opportunities to focus on self care, and also discuss many of the topics relevant to their collegiate and post-collegiate lives. However, we encourage engagement from all members of the community, including non-binary and gender non-conforming persons, and all other queer students, in our activities and events. We aim to create comfort zones where members of our community can connect, find inspiration, strength and the motivation to not only be their true selves, but also learn to truly value and appreciate their uniqueness.
summary: Formerly Black Girl Magic, the Rice Black Women’s Association empowers Black women. RBWA builds connections among current members, alumni, and friends through relationships that foster community and growth. All are welcome who support this mission.

name: The Crochet Club
description: The Crochet Club offers a collaborative space for students of all skill levels to learn about the art of crochet, and to create exciting craft projects. Whether you have no experience with crochet, or you are a seasoned professional looking to refine your skills, we welcome you to join. In addition to smaller craft projects, another major value of our organization is service. Each semester, we pursue a large service project enabling us to think of creative ways of using the craft to benefit the community.
summary: The Crochet Club offers a collaborative space for students of all skill levels to learn about the art of crochet, and engaging exciting craft projects that bring awareness or support to meaningful causes.

name: Rice Students for Sensible Drug Policy
description: Rice Students for Sensible Drug Policy is the Rice University chapter of Students for Sensible Drug Policy, an international grassroots network of students who are concerned about the impact substance use disorders have on our local community, but who also know that the draconian policies from the War on Drugs era are not the answer. Rice SSDP does not condemn or encourage drug use: we respect the right of individuals to make choices for their own health and well-being.
summary: Rice SSDP seeks to engage Rice University students in understanding the lasting negative impact of the war on drugs and advocates for sensible policy and programming to support people who use drugs (PWUD) and those suffering with substance use disorders.

name: Pre-Physician Assistant Student Association
description: The purpose of the Pre-Physician Assistant Club will be to unite Physician Assistant students in order to foster professional, educational and social interests.
summary: The purpose of the Pre-Physician Assistant Club will be to unite Physician Assistant students in order to foster professional, educational and social interests.

name: Sleepy Cyborg Gallery
description: The gallery mission is three-fold: To provide a flexible, alternative space for young or new artists; to provide motivated individuals with a unique opportunity to direct, curate, and manage a working exhibition space; to engage the arts at Rice with the greater Houston community.
summary: The gallery mission is: To provide a flexible, alternative space for young or new artists; to provide motivated individuals with a unique opportunity to direct, curate, and manage a working exhibition space; to engage the arts at Rice with Houston.

name: Architecture Graduate Student Association
description:
The mission of the AGSA is as follows:

  1. To provide a collective voice and support network for Architecture graduate students, and to facilitate open and thoughtful communication between RSA’s graduate students and the administration of RSA, including but not limited to faculty, staff, and the Dean of Architecture.
  2. To foster a culture of inclusivity within Rice Architecture, and to work in tandem with Society to ensure that all students, regardless of their degree level, have a voice within the school.
  3. To communicate with the university through collaboration with the university-wide GSA and to open up avenues for engagement and collaboration with departments outside of the school of architecture. 
  4. To function as a resource for architecture graduate students regarding professional and personal opportunities. 
  5. To build and maintain relationships among students, professionals, alumni, and community members.
summary: The mission of the Architecture Graduate Student Association is to represent the graduate students of Rice Architecture and advocate for their interests and wellbeing on both the schoolwide and University level.

name: Political Science Graduate Student Association
description: The PS-GSA will serve as the department graduate student association for graduate students within the political science department. The PS-GSA has three main purposes. First, the PS-GSA will foster better professional and personal relationships among graduate students and between graduate students and faculty. Second, the PS-GSA will provide a forum for concerns, both professional and personal, about graduate student life. Third, the PS-GSA will foster professional development among graduate students.
summary: The PS-GSA serves political science graduate students by fostering better professional and personal relationships between students and faculty members and providing a forum for concerns about graduate student life in the department.

name: Human Resources
description: Human Resources
summary: Human Resources

name: Ratio Christi at Rice University
description: Ratio Christi equips university students and faculty to give historical, philosophical, and scientific reasons for following Jesus Christ. The purpose of this Ratio Christi Chapter is to cultivate the intellectual voice of Christ and the Christian worldview at the university, and to do this by encouraging stimulating dialogue and intellectual investigation which aid in answering life’s pressing questions, especially where those questions find their answers supported by the academic disciplines of study including but not limited to: culture, history, science, philosophy, and theology.
summary: Ratio Christi Rice equips university students and faculty to give historical, philosophical, and scientific reasons for following Jesus Christ.

name: Rice Turkish Student Association
description: The Rice Turkish Student Association (RTSA) welcomes all Rice students of Turkish-descent as well as students interested in the Turkish culture! We aim to create a collaborative community and promote Turkish culture with the greater Rice University.
summary: The Rice Turkish Student Association (RTSA) welcomes all Rice students of Turkish-descent as well as students interested in the Turkish culture! We aim to create a collaborative community and promote Turkish culture with the greater Rice University.

name: Student Organization Advisors
description:
Welcome to the Student Organization Advisors OwlNest page! Resources, events, and monthly articles will be added to this page to help you advise registered student organizations. 

 

Please click the blue Join button if you currently advise a student organization on campus!

2026-2027 Student Organization Advisor Form

Have questions? Email Student Activities at studentactivities@rice.edu.
summary: Group page for all current faculty and staff student organization advisors!

name: Cru
description: Cru is a caring community passionate about connecting students with Jesus and mobilizing students to love others here and around the world. We're eager to interact respectfully and intentionally above religion and differences with students on campus.
summary: Cru at Rice is a caring community passionate about connecting students with Jesus and mobilizing students to love others here and around the world.

name: Health Sciences at Rice (HEALAR)
description:
The goal of HEALAR is to build a community amongst Health Sciences majors at Rice and foster a supportive environment for majors’ professional and personal development. We are committed to using our channels of communication to encourage more inter-class year interactions, promoting member engagement through volunteering and experiential learning, and exposing students to diverse careers in health sciences. Non-majors, although not eligible for officer positions, are welcome to join our listserv + GroupMe and attend events.

HEALAR does not charge membership fees and at least 75% of our events each year will be free to attend (usually 100%). In addition, the club will aim to ensure all in-person events are held in ADA compliant buildings.

In addition to OwlNest, please join our listserv and groupme. Links here: https://kinesiology.rice.edu/majors-clubs
summary: Welcome to HEALAR! We are the major-specific club for Health Sciences; if you're a major, be sure to join us for fun community events, professional development, experiential learning opportunities, and more!

name: Psychological Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: The Psychological Sciences Graduate Student Association is dedicated to providing community and support for all graduate students of Rice University's Department of Psychological Sciences.
summary: The Psychological Sciences Graduate Student Association is dedicated to providing community and support for all graduate students of Rice University's Department of Psychological Sciences.

name: The SAFE Office: Interpersonal Misconduct Prevention and Support
description: Rice University’s The SAFE Office: Interpersonal Misconduct Prevention and Support offers care management and navigation to students who are reporting an incident of interpersonal violence perpetrated against them and to students who have been accused of perpetrating interpersonal violence. The Office also provides prevention education to the Rice community on sexual and domestic violence, sexual harassment, stalking and Title IX and Clery Act requirements. The SAFE Office offers assistance with: Emotional Support Education on healthy relationships, consent, and interpersonal violence dynamics Safety planning Information on reporting options Accompaniments to appointments Assistance with Supportive Measures Referrals to on and off campus resources Navigation support for reporting and responding students in Title IX related SJP cases All services are provided at no cost to the student.
summary: The SAFE Office offers care management and navigation to students who are reporting an incident of interpersonal violence perpetrated against them and to students who have been accused of perpetrating interpersonal violence.

name: Rice University Barbara and David Gibbs Recreation & Wellness Center
description: The mission of the Recreation Center is to encourage a lifetime of health and wellness for the students, faculty, and staff of Rice University by promoting the physical, social, and emotional benefits of physical activity. To that end, a wide array of recreational programs and services have been created and are offered in an effort to increase knowledge about and provide opportunities for the development of healthy behavior patterns.
summary: Fitness and Wellness Programs with the Recreation Center. The purpose of this page is to invite rice students, faculty, staff and community members to join us in a Spring 30 day wellness transformation program.

name: Transnational Asian Studies
description:
In the Department of Transnational Asian Studies, we research and study Asia not as a collection of nation-states or discrete regional communities but as a series of global transformations, influences, demographic movements and migrations, trade and political formations. We work across disciplines in the humanities and social sciences to better understand the peoples, ideas, practices, and cultures of Asia, including immigrant communities across the globe.  Our faculty both in the department and our affiliates across campus, offer an extraordinary breadth of classes and research opportunities on topics of gender, migration, race and identity, medicine and technology, literature and history, religion and the arts, of globalism, consumerism and economics.

 

The Bachelor of Arts degree in Asian Studies is designed to offer a comprehensive overview of Asian Studies. Our degree emphasizes research strength, introducing students to research projects and culminating in a research capstone, based on students' own design and data collection. Students who earn a B.A. in Asian Studies receive language and cultural proficiency. This degree offers opportunities in the foreign services and diplomacy, international NGOs, international trade or law, among other fields including advanced academic study.

 

The Department of Transnational Asian Studies is a place of enormous opportunity for undergraduates at Rice. Asian studies majors engage in faculty-supervised research, internship programs, critical language acquisition, study and work experience abroad. Students interested in Asian Studies may choose to join the Rice Asian Studies Organization, developed to enhance student life on campus, promoting and planning Asian Studies programming and organizing student-led events. One of RASO’s major activities is the publication of the Rice Asian Studies Review, a well-respected student-run scholarly journal, which offers Rice students an opportunity to publish their research and to develop editing/media skills.

 

The Department of Transnational Asian Studies remains in close collaboration with the Chao Center for Asian Studies-- the premier research hub for affiliated Rice faculty, students and post-doctoral fellows. The Department of Transnational Asian Studies and the Chao Center for Asian Studies are now housed on the third floor of Lovett Hall.
summary: The Department of Transnational Asian Studies explores the peoples, ideas, practices, and cultures of Asia and global Asian communities. Opportunities include interdisciplinary research, internships, critical language acquisition, study and work abroad.

name: OwlSpark
description: Established in 2012, OwlSpark was founded to ignite a passion for entrepreneurship within the Rice community. OwlSpark supports teams of entrepreneurs through an experiential program designed to launch technology companies. We build entrepreneurial capacity and accelerate the launch of emerging, tech-based startups through experiential education, mentorship, and access to foundational resources.
summary: OwlSpark is Rice University’s startup accelerator.

name: Materials Science and NanoEngineering
description: Materials science is a broad field involving engineers and scientists from many disciplines: chemical engineering, chemistry, civil engineering, bioengineering, physics, computer engineering, and more. New materials are in demand for medicine, manufacturing, space exploration, defense and many more areas, rendering it one of the most in-demand fields of study today. The Department of Materials Science & NanoEngineering offers two undergraduate degrees. A Bachelor of Arts is a more flexible degree for students looking to pursue areas of interest outside of engineering. The Bachelor of Science is an accredited program that prepares students for the professional practice of engineering or a career in academia.Our PhD program emphasizes applied as well as theoretical aspects of materials science and nanoengineering and offer students a collaborative atmosphere and an environment that values independence, innovation, and scholarship. The professional Master of Materials Science and Nanoengineering (MMSNE) is a non-thesis program designed for students who have shown academic excellence, as well as engineering professionals who wish to delve more deeply into engineering subject matter, increase opportunities for industry advancement and enhance their technical skills.
summary: The Department of Materials Science and NanoEngineering at Rice, established in 2013, brings together faculty engaged in research and teaching in the fields of materials science and nanotechnology.

name: Diversity Facilitators
description: Diversity Facilitators continue promoting campus unity and respect for diversity throughout the year, in part, via the “Dialogues on Diversity” series. These open, campus-wide discussions are held on Fridays at 12:00 PM in Miner Lounge (unless noted otherwise) and cover variety of cultural and social issues. Facilitators may program in their college and are available to address campus concerns as needed.
summary: Diversity Facilitators continue promoting campus unity and respect for diversity throughout the year, in part, via the “Dialogues on Diversity” series.

name: Rice Center for Engineering Leadership
description: The Rice Center for Engineering Leadership (RCEL) was established in 2009 with a gift from John ’73, ’74, and Ann ’75 Doerr. The official Engineering Leadership Certificate was approved in 2014. RCEL’s mission is to inspire, educate, and develop, ethical leaders in technology who will excel in research, industry, non-engineering career paths, or entrepreneurship. RCEL’s programing enhances a traditional engineering education by providing skills not typically covered in the Rice engineering curriculum. Through a series of curricular and co-curricular learning experiences, RCEL students learn to create and communicate a vision, build a high-performing team, form and execute collaborative plans, and create innovations that endure.
summary: RCEL’s mission is to inspire, educate, and develop, ethical leaders in technology who will excel in research, industry, non-engineering career paths, or entrepreneurship.

name: Women in Finance and Investing
description: WIFI@Rice is created to educate undergraduate students on finance and investments, with a particular focus on educating undergraduate women. Through educational seminars, mentoring initiatives, and exposure to successful professionals and businesses, WiFi provides the guidance and resources upon which women can build greater knowledge of the financial industry. Open to all who support this mission.
summary: WIFI@Rice educates undergraduates on finance and investments, focusing on women, through market education, buy-side resources, exposure to industry professionals, and real financial experience. Open to all who share this mission.

name: Digital Media Commons
description: The Digital Media Commons provides students, faculty, and staff with support for all manner of digital media projects. We have a wide variety of media production equipment for checkout free of charge including camcorders, still cameras, microphones, audio recorders, tripods, and more. We also have digital media editing workstations equipped with the Adobe Creative Cloud suite, iMovie, Garageband, Audacity, Finalcut Pro X, and other specific media editing software. Along with the workstations we have a number of useful peripherals including high resolution film or photo scanners, large format flatbed scanners, document scanners, bluray players, and a VHS digitization setup. We also teach a wide variety of short courses every semester covering a wide variety of digital media applications. For professors interested in incorporating media projects into their courses, we provide in class seminars on media production and editing tailored towards the end goal of specific projects as well. If you have any questions or projects in mind, feel free to visit us in the basement of Fondren library. At the DMC, your projects are our passion!
summary: The Digital Media Commons supports the creation and use of multimedia in education, scholarship, and creative expression.

name: The Department of Naval Science
description: Rice Naval Reserve Officer Training Corp
summary: Rice Naval Reserve Officer Training Corp

name: Oshman Engineering Design Kitchen
description: The Oshman Engineering Design Kitchen (OEDK) provides a space for undergraduate students majoring in bioengineering, chemical and biomolecular engineering, civil and environmental engineering, computational and applied mathematics, computer science, electrical and computer engineering, materials science and nanoengineering, mechanical engineering and statistics to design, prototype and deploy solutions to real-world engineering challenges. By working in close proximity to engineers in all disciplines, our students develop multiple perspectives on how to pursue a project. The OEDK is a 20,000-square-foot space, which includes not only the central work area with over 75 work tables, but also conference rooms, a classroom, a wet lab, rapid prototyping equipment, large format printers, designated woodworking area, a machine shop and access to a welding shop. The presence of large engineering firms, such as National Instruments and Texas Instruments allows engineers to become experienced with the products they will be using once they enter the workforce.
summary: The Oshman Engineering Design Kitchen (OEDK) provides a world-class venue for undergraduate engineering design projects, with cross-discipline and cross-technology training for students at Rice University.

name: Earth, Environmental & Planetary Sciences
description:
The goal of Rice University’s Department of Earth, Environmental and Planetary Sciences is to bring together the creative power of Houston’s energy, environmental and space industries in the context of whole earth systems to make sustainable progress on the landscape of environment and energy.

 

The mission of the Earth, Environmental & Planetary Sciences Department at Rice is to pursue excellence in research, education and outreach in the fields of Earth, environmental, energy and planetary sciences.
summary: The goal of Rice University’s Department of Earth, Environmental and Planetary Sciences is to bring together the creative power of Houston’s energy, environmental and space industries in the context of whole earth systems to make sustainable progress.

name: Weeks of Welcome
description:
Weeks of Welcome combines academic, educational, developmental, and social programming to provide a solid foundation for new and returning students as they transition to Rice University.

 

This initiative spreads across the first two weeks of the fall and spring semesters.

 

  • Goals/Objectives
    • Foster relationships between students and the broader Rice community.
    • Promote belonging and inclusivity for new and returning students.
    • Provide engaging events for students as they adjust to college life at Rice University.
    • To connect new and returning Rice students with campus resources and student life.

 

To have an event occurring between Saturday, August 23rd and Saturday, September 6th reviewed for potential addition to the WOW calendar, please fill out a WOW tag form by Friday, August 15th!
summary: Weeks of Welcome combines academic, educational, developmental, and social programming to provide a solid foundation for new and returning students as they transition to Rice University.

name: Student Success Initiatives
description: The Office of Student Success Initiatives (SSI) engages and supports Rice undergraduate students employing proactive advising and other high engagement strategies to facilitate students’ successful transition, retention and graduation from the university. Created in 2014, SSI works with students to uncover the various factors that might be impacting your success at Rice. Our team of dedicated advisors, assists students with a variety of issues such as time management, cultivating Rice-level study skills, dealing with failure, and making the social, emotional, and navigational transition to college. Our proactive advising allows for collaboration with campus partners as needed to best support students: COLLEGE MAGISTERS OFFICE OF ACADEMIC ADVISING STUDENT WELLBEING, COUNSELING CENTER TITLE IX OFFICE CENTER FOR CAREER DEVELOPMENT CENTER FOR CIVIC LEADERSHIP Visit our website to make an appointment with an advisor, or walk into our offices located in the Cloisters of the Rice Student Center.
summary: The Office of Student Success Initiatives(SSI) engages and supports Rice undergraduate students employing proactive advising and other high engagement strategies to facilitate students’ successful transition, retention and graduation from the university.

name: Wiess School of Natural Sciences
description: The Wiess School of Natural Sciences sees the universe as its laboratory. Intense curiosity drives each of us to explore the unknown. From hunting the smallest subatomic particles to investigating the unfathomable distances of our universe — and scientific inquiries at every scale in between — the goal at the heart of the School of Natural Sciences is discovery. As a student in the School of Natural Sciences, you join a uniquely collaborative community of scientists who aim to advance our fundamental understanding of the natural world through innovative research.
summary: Rice Natural Sciences empowers the next generation of discoverers and leaders through unsurpassed education and pioneering research that advances our fundamental understanding of the natural world.

name: Campus/Special Events
summary: Campus Events

name: The Women's Network - Rice
description: The Women's Network - Rice is a chapter of The Women's Network (TWN), a nationwide collegiate organization. TWN's mission is to create a community of ambitious women that desire to achieve while giving them the tools to succeed in the professional world. TWN is committed to changing the way women network. In creating a supportive, non-competitive community, we lift women up, creating a more proactive way of communicating and networking. We are the largest, most engaging collegiate women’s network nationwide – connecting women to professionals and preparing future leaders. The chapter is open to all who support its mission.
summary: The Women’s Network (TWN), a nationwide collegiate organization at Rice.  TWN’s mission is to  build a community of ambitious women and provide tools for success in the professional world. Our chapter is open to all who share this mission.

name: Kids Are Scientists Too
description:
Kids Are Scientists Too (KAST) is a volunteer non-profit organization dedicated to inspiring a love of science in underserved elementary students in HISD through fun and interactive STEM lessons!

KASTies develop lessons and travel to schools in HISD twice a month to teach interactive and FUN experiments to 3rd-5th graders.

KASTies have multiple opportunities for volunteering and leadership with different levels of time commitment. These include our Volunteer Krew, who travel off-campus to teach students 1hr-long lessons in-person, and our Curriculum Krew where we develop and test lesson plans.

We are always looking for more volunteers and leaders because HISD schools love ways to engage their students in free STEM opportunities!

 

Join! :)

p.s. check out our instagram page! put your email here to join the listserv, and here's our linktree
summary: Kids Are Scientists Too (KAST) is a small but growing organization dedicated to inspiring a love of science in underserved elementary students in HISD through fun and interactive lessons!

name: Rice Wind Energy
description: Want to design and build a performant wind turbine and immerse yourselves in real-world engineering challenges? Want to organize a wind farm and learn how to make this crucial cleaner form of energy viable? Want to strengthen your communication skills and let the local and global community know about the importance of shifting our energy sources to something cleaner? We’ve got all that in addition to a tight-knit and welcoming team culture. And at the end of each year, we travel to the Collegiate Wind Competition to showcase our work!
summary: A group of undergraduate students innovating in the renewable energy field to compete in the U.S. Department of Energy’s Collegiate Wind Competition

name: Rice Middle Eastern and North African Student Association
description: The mission of the Middle Eastern and North African Student Association (MENA SA) at Rice University is to provide a community for students interested in Middle Eastern and North African cultures and issues, and to provide cultural enrichment to the Rice and Houston community at large.
summary: The Middle Eastern and North African Student Association (MENA SA) provides community for all students interested in Middle Eastern and North African cultures and issues, promotes cultural enrichment and hosts events for Rice and the Houston community.

name: Rice Stroke
description: Rice Stroke is a student organization at Rice University focused on improving stroke education and patient care. Our members work directly with stroke patients in hospital settings, providing education on their condition, treatments, and follow-up care to promote better recovery and continuity of care. We also have the unique opportunity to shadow neurologists on rounds, gaining firsthand clinical exposure to stroke management and patient interactions. Rice Stroke is ideal for students interested in medicine, neurology, and patient advocacy, offering both meaningful patient engagement and professional development.
summary: Rice Stroke is a student organization that works directly with stroke patients to improve their understanding of stroke, treatment options, and follow-up care. Members also shadow neurologists on rounds, gaining clinical experience while supporting patie

name: Rice Neuro Transmitter
description: We have four major goals: (1) to promote interest in neuroscience within Rice and Houston by featuring student-written articles; (2) to highlight Rice students’ accomplishments in neuroscience research, (3) to serve as a real-time resource for information about everything in the neuroscience major at Rice, and (4) to encourage exploration of neuroscience career paths by interviews of professionals and/or Rice alumni. We also host competitions aimed to encourage high school students, both locally and globally, to explore neuroscience research. We plan on hosting events to make neuroscience research and scientific writing accessible to all! See our website for more information.
summary: Welcome to Rice’s Undergraduate Neuroscience Journal, Rice Neuro Transmitter!  The mission of this journal is to promote interest in neuroscience research, events, careers, outreach, and the major within Rice University and the local Houston area.

name: Latinx Graduate Students at Rice

We welcome all students, but we are particularly interested in sharing the experiences of students who are of Latinx descent or have spent a greater part of their lives in the states. We serve the needs of Latinx graduate students, which can include professional development and opportunities, social networking, and displaying school spirit by attending university events.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics - Rice University Chapter
description: The American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics has for many years promoted aerospace education, development, and competition. Now Rice’s chapter is looking to further promote this vastly expanding industry by hosting competition teams, speakers, and events for the Rice campus. AIAA is a professional society like ASME or IEEE, but for Aerospace Engineering, meaning that students and professionals can be members for the duration of their careers in the field. We also compete in the SAE Aero Advanced competition each year as Rice Flight!
summary: The American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics has for many years promoted aerospace education, development, and competition. Rice’s chapter is looking to further promote this with chapter events and our RC aircraft competition team, Rice Flight

description: As Rice students concerned about the sixth mass extinction and dedicated to ecological conservation, we aim to rehabilitate the natural environment through community education, research, networking and outreach, and hands-on restoration projects.

name: Students for Justice in Palestine
description: Based upon the guidelines provided by National SJP, Students for Justice in Palestine (SJP) is a student movement at Rice University that draws from values of freedom, solidarity, equality, safety and historical justice. We aim to elevate student Palestine solidarity work to a deeper level of political engagement. Through creating a platform for collaboration, providing organizational and educational support, disseminating accessible resources for student organizers, and connecting students in solidarity, we strive to be connected, disciplined, and equipped with the tools necessary to build grassroots momentum for Palestinian liberation in the ultimate goal of self-determination for the Palestinian people. All are welcome who support this mission.
summary: Students for Justice in Palestine is a student advocacy group rooted in networking and organization towards Palestinian liberation. All are welcome who support this mission.

name: Political Science
description: The Department of Political Science specializes in American politics (U.S. government, elections, and citizens’ behavior), comparative politics (governments in other countries), and international relations (conflict and cooperation in the world).
summary: The Department of Political Science specializes in American politics (U.S. government, elections, and citizens’ behavior), comparative politics (governments in other countries), and international relations (conflict and cooperation in the world).

name: School of Humanities
description:
At Rice, a liberal arts education begins in the humanities, where students from all academic backgrounds and career interests engage with our prize-winning faculty to interpret, critique and debate the crucial issues facing the world today. In many of our small seminar-style courses, students develop critical thinking and research skills that are enhanced by internships and research opportunities.

You’ll quickly discover that the humanities and the arts are integrated and connected on campus. The distinctive qualities of this “connective humanities” at Rice include:

  • Our deep engagement with the sciences, as reflected in our growing research expertise and innovative curricula in Medical Humanities; Environmental Studies; and Science and Technology Studies; and our emergent examination of Ethics of Technology;

  • Our global expertise in world histories, cultures and languages, including co-curricular study abroad programs and our advancement of African and African American Studies, Latin American and Latinx Studies, and Transnational Asian Studies as fields of research and curricular innovation;

  • Our pursuit of research and experiential learning that connects and reflects Houston’s diversity and standing as a global city; our region’s experience of climate vulnerability and crisis; its landscape of museums, galleries and communities of artists and writers; and the location of the world’s largest medical center at our doorstep.
summary: Students of all academic backgrounds and career interests engage with our prize-winning faculty to explore the biggest questions of our time, developing critical thinking and research skills that are enhanced by internships and experiential learning.

name: George R Brown Forensics Society
description: The George R Brown Forensics Society is Rice University's competitive intercollegiate speech and debate team. Founded in 1984, the society replaced the original Rice Forensic Society, which existed from 1954 to 1964. The team represents Rice by competing in speech and debate tournaments throughout the United States. The George R. Brown Forensics Society has a strong tradition of success. The society has brought home numerous national championships.

name: Rice Re-Generation
description:
As members of the Rice community, we believe that we can fulfill our overall objective of raising awareness of the value of stem cell research. By collaborating on a database website that compiles the most recent information on stem cells, partnering with and shadowing laboratories in the Texas Medical Center, educating high school students on the potential of stem cell research, creating publications, and conducting our own research, we strongly believe that we can advance the pursuit of scientific knowledge in the Rice and Houston community.  

On Campus

  1. Interact with the Rice student body through scientific issues such as stem cell research, regenerative medicine, and surgery strategies.
  2. Fundraise in partnership with other on-campus clubs.
  3. Conduct our own research to be published through the "Frontiers for Young Minds."

Off Campus

  1. Engage with the general Houston high school/middle school student population and increase awareness of the value of stem cell research through workshops and annual competitions.
  2. Encourage undergraduate students to participate in research by collaborating with the Benham-Pyle Lab.
summary: Rice Re-Generation focuses on collective stem cell research. Our goal is to raise awareness of the value of stem cell research by educating local high schools, partnering with labs in the Texas Medical Center, and generating our own publications/research

name: Rice Biokind Analytics
description:
About Us: Biokind Analytics unites undergraduate statisticians and data scientists to support healthcare nonprofit organizations through data-driven projects. By applying data science and analytics to real-world challenges, we transform classroom learning into meaningful societal impact.

Open to all Rice University undergraduate students, Biokind seeks to bridge the gap between Rice students' education in engineering and computing with social good. Our members come from diverse academic backgrounds, including Computer Science, Statistics, Bioengineering, Computational and Applied Mathematics, Electrical and Computer Engineering, Biosciences and more. Together, we collaborate with under-resourced healthcare nonprofit organizations to help them better understand their data, make informed decisions, and advance their missions of improving community health and well-being. Each semester, student teams work on analytics projects that directly support our nonprofit partners, ranging from community health assessments to donation analysis and impact forecasting, strengthening our partners’ missions while giving students hands-on experience applying data science for public good.  

2025 Goals:

  1. Expand healthcare nonprofit partnerships and increase client diversity.

  2. Create more networking opportunities for members pursuing careers in data science and related fields.

  3. Boost engagement and community-building through club activities and events.

Get Involved!

You can support Biokind’s mission by:

  • Providing donations or sponsorships to fund projects and events.

  • Offering networking opportunities in medicine, data science, and related industries.

  • Serving as mentors to guide current members’ professional and academic growth.

For more information, visit biokind.org or contact us at rice@biokind.org.
summary: Today, even small businesses use data analysis and statistical modeling to boost profits and efficiency. Our mission is to provide the same opportunity for healthcare non-profit organizations to boost their impact.

name: RiceApps
description:
RiceApps is the home of experiential learning. Our mission is to foster teams of Rice students to build real world software projects.
Every year, 50 students work together to build project from conception to deployment for the Rice community or a non-profit organization. Previous projects include:
  • Rice Public Art for the Moody Center for the Arts
  • Externship matching algorithm for the Rice Center for Career Development
  • Matching software for Hives for Heroes, a Rice founded non-profit supporting veterans.
  • iOS App for Second Servings to help distribute surplus food.
  • Hatch, which helps students plan out their academic semester
  • Text chat bot for the Patient Discharge Initiative.
  • Carpool (in development), a web app to help students find people to split a ride with. 
  • Recipie sharing platform for Houston Food Bank (in development).
In the process students learn software engineering best-practices, such as agile development, git, React, Express, and more. 
Additionally, our summer program Open Source Accelerator (OSA) takes on 40 Rice students with limited software development experience, and pairs them with upperclassmen mentors to help them learn how to build web applications. Here are the outcomes from summer 2021:
  • Before the start of the program, only 33.3% of participants indicated they were“confident in their ability to design and build their own project.” After the program, 90.5% felt confident.
  • Before, only 38.1% of participants indicated they understood the basics of web development. After, 90.5% understood.
  • Before, only 4.8% of participants indicated they did not know how to use React — an industry-standard web development technology. After, 95.2% indicated they knew React.
Ultimately, we provide educational benefit to the Rice CS community.
summary: Building software for social good

name: Rice Cinema Club
description: Rice Cinema Club hopes to connect Rice Owls through the art of cinema, film watching, and film discussion! We aim to serve as a place for those interested in film as an art form, careers in the film and media industry, and film as entertainment to find resources and connect. For those interested in film as an art, we show films weekly, chosen specifically to expose the Rice community to perspectives, styles, and artistry that they would most likely be unfamiliar with. For those interested in career exploration in the film and media industry, we provide opportunities to connect with those in the industry, including Houston-area filmmakers, industry professionals, film critics, etc. We are committed to partnering with the vibrant Houston arts community as well in order to accomplish the previously-stated goals.
summary: We are a film appreciation club, organizing film screenings and other movie-focused events throughout the year.

name: Neurotech@Rice
description:
NeuroTech@Rice aims to serve as a resource for Rice students to learn more about careers in neurotechnology, research in neuroengineering & Rice’s Neuroengineering Initiative (NEI), and other related neurotechnology resources and to make partnerships with various organizations in the Neurotechnology community to further expand opportunities for students to become involved. There are no membership fees or annual dues. We welcome anyone interested in neurotechnology and neuroengineering, regardless of major/field ranging from Electrical engineering, Bioengineering, Statistics, Computer Science, Neuroscience, Biology, Mechanical Engineering, Physics, Business, Psychology, Mathematics, Statistics, and Data Science.
