name: Rice University Boxing Club
description:
The Rice University Boxing Club aims to provide an environment for members of the Rice community interested in practicing and learning the sport of boxing and/or improving their physical condition. 

 

Members will participate in trainings and spars led by officers, sponsors, or hired coaches. 

 

Full members will be able to compete in competitions.
summary: Rice University Boxing Club is a sports club that aims to provide an environment for members of the Rice community interested in practicing and learning the sport of boxing and/or improving their physical condition.

name: Women in CyberSecurity - Rice University Student Chapter
description: WiCyS at Rice is a Student Chapter of the international Women in CyberSecurity 501 (c) (3) nonprofit dedicated to building a strong gender-diverse cybersecurity workforce by facilitating recruitment, retention and advancement for women in the field. Our mission is to decrease the gender disparity in cybersecurity; to provide education, mentorship, and networking support to students through the WiCyS community; and to promote and recruit women in the cybersecurity workforce through Rice University. Open to all who support this mission.
summary: WiCyS at Rice, part of the global Women in CyberSecurity nonprofit, dedicated to building a gender-diverse cybersecurity workforce by facilitating recruitment, retention, and advancement for women. Open to all who share this mission.

name: Rice Pickleball Club
description: Join us for a paddle-smashing, ball-spiking, pickle-tastic time on campus! Whether you're a ranked pro-pickler or an irresistibly curious novice, our club welcomes all skill levels. Together we'll rally, dink, and donk (WHILE STAYIN THE **** OUT OF THE KITCHEN!!!) through friendly open-play, mixers, and tournaments. It's the perfect way to de-stress, make new friends, and stay active. No fancy equipment needed - just bring your enthusiasm and we'll bring the rest. Come see why pickleball has been called the fastest-growing sport at Rice! So what are you waiting for? We’ll see you out on the courts, cause believe us this ain’t your grandma’s pickle.
summary: Rice Pickleball Club is where fun meets finesse on the court! Join our energetic community of picklers serving up exciting matches, friendly competition, and lots of laughs. Come pickle with us!

name: Rice University Zen Buddhism Club
description: The Rice University Zen Buddhism Club aims to cultivate wisdom, encourage spiritual and intellectual growth and walk a path of non-dualism. We aim to create a warm and family style social environment that creates activities tailored for all Rice students.
summary: The Rice University Zen Buddhism Club aims to cultivate wisdom, encourage spiritual and intellectual growth and walk a path of non-dualism. We aim to create a warm and family style social environment that creates activities tailored for all students.

name: Rice Rap Club
description: Rice Rap Club is an undergraduate social organization that provides a community for rap fans and celebrates Houston's hip hop history. Expect album listening parties, events, guest speakers, and competitions. No application, no commitment, all vibes.
summary: Rice Rap Club is an undergraduate social organization that provides a community for rap fans and celebrates Houston's hip hop history. Expect album listening parties, events, guest speakers, and competitions. No application, no commitment, all vibes.

name: Rice Alliance for Technology and Entrepreneurship
description: The Rice Alliance for Technology and Entrepreneurship (Rice Alliance) is Rice University’s nationally-recognized initiative devoted to the support of technology commercialization, entrepreneurship education and the launch of startups and tech companies. Our mission enables the creation and success of startups and the commercialization of new technologies in the Houston community and beyond. For more than 20 years, the Rice Alliance has served as a hub for entrepreneurial efforts at the university. We champion the adoption and growth of new programs by weaving an entrepreneurial mindset across campus—from nurturing the formation of new ideas to encouraging engineers, scientists and researchers to turn their work into commercial success to connecting founders with mentors and investors. With backing from former Rice University President, Dr. Malcolm Gillis, the Rice Alliance was founded in 2000 as a catalyst for entrepreneurship at Rice through a strategic alliance of three schools: the George R. Brown School of Engineering, the Wiess School of Natural Sciences and the Jesse H. Jones Graduate School of Business in collaboration with the Vice Provost and the Office of Research. We continue to have close relationships with all three schools and aim to work with groups across campus to elevate Rice’s entrepreneurial ecosystem. The desire to forge a foundational connection to the growth of Houston's startup ecosystem is at our core. Our long-time partnerships bridge Houston’s entrepreneurial ecosystem with Rice's campus. We’re proud to be located in the heart of the most diverse city in the country, assisting founders and supporters, while also bringing some of the top emerging startups to the bayou city for networking and investment.
summary: The Rice Alliance for Technology and Entrepreneurship (Rice Alliance) is Rice University’s nationally-recognized initiative devoted to the support of technology commercialization, entrepreneurship education, and the launch of technology companies. Our mi

name: Omega House @ Rice University
description: As of 2019, about 1.2 million people in the United States are living with HIV. However, despite the development of treatments that made HIV a "chronic" disease, there are many barriers to treatment adherence especially among socially disadvantaged and the aging population. Omega House aims to support these people with HIV by providing compassionate end-of-life care and acting as a safe environment for those with HIV. Because Omega House heavily depends on volunteer caregivers, Omega House @ Rice aims to work with the Omega House to train new Rice caregivers, plan resident engagement events, and work to better engage the Rice and Houston community on HIV/AIDS.
summary: Omega House @ Rice is commited to recruiting, training, and coordinating volunteer caregivers for the Omega House, a hospice dedicated to HIV/AIDS patients in Houston. We strive to provide compassionate care and to engage the Rice community on HIV/AIDS.

name: Rice University Adaptive Skating
description:
Rice University Adaptive Skating hosts Adaptive Skating HTX, a non-profit program that teaches children (ages 3-18) with disabilities how to ice skate! Held at Ice at the Galleria, classes meet once a week for 30 minutes, where students receive specialized instruction from our volunteers.

Currently, there are two types of volunteers: coaches and teaching assistants.

  • Coaches are experienced skaters that are are comfortable in teaching skills to others
  • Teaching assistants have some skating experience but perhaps cannot skate backwards; they will assist coaches during class

Even if you don't have much skating experience, don't worry! We will hold a training session for teaching assistants in the fall to strengthen skating skills. Please consider joining our program - especially if you have prior experience working with children with disabilities, or have special skills like sign language and EMS. We'd love to have you!

Join our GroupMe here: https://groupme.com/join_group/91245442/h78uHNWP
summary: We host Adaptive Skating HTX, a non-profit program striving to empower children with disabilities through specialized ice skating instruction. We welcome Rice students and community members to volunteer with us as coaches for our weekly classes!

name: Master of Engineering Management and Leadership Student Association
description:
The Master of Engineering Management and Leadership Student Association (MEMLSA) at Rice University is tasked with representing all MEML students at Rice University.

Developing extra-curricular activities and events to complement the MEML curriculum and education. Our goal is to advance the welfare of every MEML student to help them prepare for their future career.

Our purpose is

  1. Organize activities that foster engagement and collaboration among engineering management and leadership students from different backgrounds to build a community.
  2. Connect students with industry professionals through guest speaker events, networking opportunities, and other events to support the transition from academic to professional life.
  3. Enhance leadership skills, provide opportunities for professional development and encourage personal growth through activities to help students succeed in the engineering management field.
summary: Represent the MEML students at Rice University where we develop extra-curricular activities to complement education; connect students with industry professionals; and enhance leadership skills.

name: American Society for Engineering Management Rice University Chapter
description:
About ASEM @ Rice University

The ASEM Student Chapter at Rice University exists to empower the next generation of engineering and technology leaders by bridging technical excellence with strategic management skills.

Aligned with the ASEM national mission—to promote the development and practice of the engineering management profession—our chapter focuses on preparing students to lead in complex, technology-driven organizations.

Our Mission

We aim to develop well-rounded engineering managers by combining theory, practice, and real-world exposure. Through professional development, competition, and community, we help our members turn technical expertise into leadership impact.

What We Do

  • Advance engineering management in theory and practice through applied learning and real-world case work

  • Uphold high professional standards among our members

  • Connect students with industry leaders and professionals through networking events, workshops, and speaker series

  • Support career growth by equipping members with practical management, strategy, and communication skills

  • Compete globally, representing Rice University in ASEM national and international case competitions

Our Community

ASEM @ Rice serves as a platform where students—particularly those in engineering and the MEML program—can collaborate, challenge themselves, and grow into confident leaders. We foster an inclusive, high-impact community focused on continuous learning, mentorship, and professional excellence.

Our goal is simple but ambitious: to be one of the top ASEM chapters in the nation, recognized for leadership, competitive excellence, and the success of our members!
summary: We will equip our members with the skills and knowledge necessary for success in this field through professional development opportunities, including networking events, workshops, industry events, and participation in ASEM conferences and competitions. W

name: Los Búhos del Norte
description: As part of the mission to increase cultural enrichment at the Rice University campus and in the Houston community, we will provide a service through our musical abilities to enhance Mexican and Latino culture. By participating in different University sponsored and Houston community events, we can further educate people about an important aspect of Hispanic/Latin culture. Through versatile music, we hope to accomplish another level of educational performances for Rice University and the City of Houston.
summary: The group focuses on playing Norteña, Cumbia, Huapango-style, and classic spanish rock music with the aim of increasing awareness of  Mexico’s, Tejano, and Latin America rich musical heritage.

name: Rice Mixed Martial Arts Club
description:
Rice Mixed Martial Arts is a club dedicated to teaching the basics and fundamentals of various martial arts including Boxing, Brazilian Jiu Jitsu, and Wrestling to all Rice students, as well as hosting watch parties for various combat sports events.

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/106489842/sPGEB0Y4
summary: Rice Mixed Martial Arts is a club dedicated to teaching the basics and fundamentals of various martial arts including Boxing, Brazilian Jiu Jitsu, and Wrestling to all Rice students, as well as hosting watch parties for various combat sports events.

name: Rice Global Surgery Student Alliance
description:
Vision: Sustainable and accessible surgical, obstetric, and anesthesia care worldwide.

​

Mission: To educate, inspire, and unite students through engagement and mentorship in global surgery, including anesthesia & OB/Gyn.

​

Goals:

  • To create awareness about and to advocate for global surgery

  • To educate students about global surgery and sustainable interventions

  • To advance research in global surgery through student involvement

  • To connect students who are interested in global surgery across the country and world

  • To facilitate communication and mentorship between students and current global surgeons, OB/Gyns, and anesthesiologists.

  • To foster the development of the next generation of global surgeons, OB/Gyns, and anesthesiologists
summary: We are the Rice chapter of GSSA, the national student-run global surgery working group for the U.S., affiliated with InciSioN (the International Student Surgical Network). Our goal is to educate, inspire, and unite students through global surgery.

name: Rice University Figure Skating Club
description: The Rice University Figure Skating Club (RUFSC) is a university-sponsored sports club that participates in the Intercollegiate Program, which is overseen by United States Figure Skating (USFS). Our team members skate at all levels and are united by a common passion for figure skating.
summary: Rice University's US Figure Skating Association affiliated figure skating club.

name: Rice Ethiopian and Eritrean Student Association
description: Rice Ethiopian & Eritrean Student Association strives to create a space where Ethiopian and Eritrean students can celebrate their many similarities and nuanced differences, while also sharing their rich culture with the broader Rice community. The organization welcomes all students who are interested in learning about and engaging with Ethiopian and Eritrean cultures.
summary: Rice Ethiopian & Eritrean Student Association creates space for Ethiopian and Eritrean students to celebrate shared and unique traditions while sharing their rich culture with the Rice community. Open to all students interested in learning and engaging.

name: Link Nonprofit
description: Link Nonprofit is an organization which strives to connect underserved communities with healthcare resources and discounted care. We will achieve this by hosting booths at health fairs where we can hand out resources regarding preventative healthcare and conduct basic healthcare checkups. Additionally, we will direct patients to our website, where they can also be connected with resources.
summary: The Rice chapter of Link Nonprofit supports the national organization's mission by committing to promoting education and advocacy about preventative healthcare. We strive to do so by organizing campaigns targeting the Rice students and the Houston.

name: Peak
description: Peak - An invitation-based retreat for campus leaders to connect, learn, and grow.
summary: Peak - A place for campus leaders to connect, learn, and grow.

name: Rice Women's Club Lacrosse
description: Rice Women's Club Lacrosse is a competitive team that aims to foster a more inclusive and diverse lacrosse community. Open to players of all experience levels, we strive to help individuals grow and thrive as athletes in a collaborative environment.
summary: Rice Women's Club Lacrosse is a competitive team that aims to foster a more inclusive and diverse lacrosse community. Open to players of all experience levels, we strive to help individuals grow and thrive as athletes in a collaborative environment.

name: Rice Black Men's Association
description: RBMA fosters community, empowerment, and leadership development for those interested in the experiences of Black men at Rice. We support growth and build connections through mentorship, dialogue, and programs. Open to all students who share this mission.
summary: RBMA fosters community, empowerment, and leadership development for those interested in the experiences of Black men at Rice. We support growth and build connections through mentorship, dialogue, and programs. Open to all students who share this mission.

name: Rice Cubing Club
description: • The Rice Cubing Club is a dynamic and inclusive student organization that centers around the exploration, mastery, and enjoyment of twisty puzzles, with a particular focus on the iconic Rubik's Cube. Our club is driven by the shared passion for intellectual challenges, critical thinking, and fostering connections among students from diverse backgrounds and disciplines. At the core of our club's activities is the pursuit of knowledge and skill development. We provide a space where individuals of all skill levels can engage in puzzle-solving, learn new techniques, and refine their problem-solving abilities. Our members not only discover the intricacies of twisty puzzles but also cultivate essential life skills such as patience, persistence, and adaptability.
summary: The Rice Cubing Club is a dynamic and inclusive student organization that centers around the exploration, mastery, and enjoyment of twisty puzzles, with a particular focus on the iconic Rubik's Cube.

name: Office of Sustainability
description: The Office of Sustainability leads and provides support for a broad range of campus sustainability initiatives spanning the entire university enterprise. A sampling of this work includes planning for sustainability, green building, energy and water conservation, climate action, composting, recycling, environmental education, public lectures and events, student environmental projects, research partnerships, fundraising, and classroom engagement. The Office’s initiatives and actions are guided by the UN’s Sustainable Development Goals, providing a holistic view of sustainability aligned with solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges
summary: The Office of Sustainability leads and supports a broad range of sustainability initiatives spanning the entire university enterprise. The Office is guided by the UN’s Sustainable Development Goals, providing a holistic view of sustainability.

name: Civil and Environmental Engineering
description: Our curriculum is centered around four areas of specialization 1) Environmental engineering 2) Hydrology and water resources 3) Structural engineering and mechanics 4) Urban infrastructure and management We offer the following degrees: PHD MASTER OF SCIENCE PROFESSIONAL MASTER'S PROGRAM BS IN CIVIL ENGINEERING (ABET-ACCREDITED PROGRAM) BA WITH A MAJOR IN CIVIL OR ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING
summary: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering in the George R. Brown School of Engineering

name: ACEing Autism
summary: ACEing Autism helps children with autism to grow, develop and benefit from social connections and fitness through affordable tennis programming. Every week, we teach kids with autism in the local Houston area basic tennis skills.

name: Rice University Chapter of the Latin American Medical Association of Houston
description: Through mentorship/mentee opportunities, as well as educational opportunities, this organization aims to bring together undergraduate, medical students, and experienced professionals from all over the Houston area to discuss the fundamentals of Medicine and help students prepare for their futures. All are welcome who support this mission.
summary: This organization connects undergraduates, medical students, and professionals to discuss medical fundamentals and prepare Rice’s pre-health community, with a focus on supporting Latine and Hispanic students. Open to all who support this mission.

name: Rice African Graduate Student Association
description:
The Rice African Graduate Student Association is a group of graduate students and postdocs interested in the culture, history, and community of countries in the African continent. RAGSA supports members in their academic pursuits and social acclimation to the various environments on Rice university campus.

Beyond academic support, RAGSA is deeply committed to fostering social awareness and advocacy. The organization actively works to amplify the voices and lived experiences of African graduate students, creating platforms for dialogue, cultural expression, and community engagement. Through events, collaborations, and outreach, RAGSA plays a vital role in enhancing the visibility and impact of African perspectives within the broader Rice community.
summary: The Rice African Graduate Student Association is a group of graduate students & postdocs interested in the culture, history, & community of countries in the African continent. RAGSA supports members in their academic pursuits & social acclimation to Rice

name: InThread
description: InThread is a pioneering organization at Rice University dedicated to promoting sustainable design principles. Through a series of immersive hands-on events, we empower our members with the knowledge and skills needed to create garments using eco-friendly methods, all in preparation for our annual spring fashion show. Collaborating closely with the local thrift and arts communities, our mission is to cultivate a profound appreciation for fashion as an art form, enabling individuals to unleash their creative potential while actively contributing to the evolving landscape of the fashion industry with a strong environmental consciousness. As the first organization to bring a runway fashion show to Rice University, we embark on this ambitious endeavor to provide a platform for students who might otherwise go unnoticed, giving them the opportunity to showcase their unique expressions and talents.
summary: InThread empowers members through hands-on workshops to create eco-friendly garments for our annual spring fashion show. We cultivate a deep appreciation for sustainable fashion, providing a platform for students to showcase unique talents.

name: Rice Automotive Club for Enthusiasts
description: Rice Automotive Club for Engineers (RACE) is a student-run organization that helps students explore automotive engineering through hands-on projects and design challenges. We provide opportunities for everyone to develop practical engineering skills.
summary: Rice Automotive Club for Enthusiasts (RACE) is a student-run organization for anyone that wants to learn more about cars. We provide opportunities for everyone to develop practical skills with hands-on projects and engage in the local community as well.

name: Augmented Reality/Virtual Reality Club
description:
The Virtual Reality/Augmented Reality Club seeks to connect students with opportunities to collaborate and learn about immersive technologies.

Join our GroupMe!
summary: The Rice AR/VR club seeks to connect students with opportunities to collaborate and learn about immersive technologies.

name: Rice Student Venture Fund
description:
This fund will give students hands-on experience with the venture process, including sourcing deals, due diligence and financial modeling, and making investment decisions. The fund will be advised by a team of VC professionals and professors at Rice, including Kyle Judah, our faculty sponsor and director of Rice’s Lilie Program. 

Investment Thesis: Rice is a world class research institution on the cutting edge of technological development, featuring a diverse and highly motivated student body. Our deal flow revolves around those students looking to make an impact on the world through big ideas. We are looking to firstly invest in viable homegrown Rice startups from undergraduates, graduate students, professors, and technologies developed at Rice. Secondly, we plan to leverage the diverse interests and experiences of our student body and are open to deal flow from anywhere our team members’ passions take them. Lastly, and most importantly, we are looking to offer a syndicate for our Alumni, so they can co-invest in Rice Venture Fund’s opportunities.

The creation of this fund will enable a new era of Rice entrepreneurship, allowing student founders to build their visions and student investors to gain invaluable experience, and allow startups connected to the Rice Network to have a formal way to receive their early stage capital.
summary: We hope to provide an outlet for students who are passionate about VC in the form of a student led venture fund. This fund will give students hands-on experience with the venture process, including sourcing deals, due diligence and investment decisions.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances
description: The VAW Global Health Alliances (VAW Global) Rice Chapter is committed to addressing global health disparities. Through close collaboration with VAW Global Health Alliances, we work hand-in-hand with local initiatives and leaders to provide rural communities in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) with improved access to medical treatment, medication, nutritional security, clean water, and health education. We fulfill this goal by organizing Medical Outreach Trips to several host countries, including Panama, Peru, Vietnam, Uzbekistan, Mexico, Guatemala, and Dominican Republic, where we manage medical clinics, experience a hands-on clinical work environment, shadow local physicians, and learn about disease diagnosis, treatment courses, clinic operations, and basic pharmacology. The clinics are maintained year-round by VAW Global Chapters at universities throughout the world. More information is available at the VAW Global website.
summary: We address health disparities by partnering with communities abroad. Under direct medical supervision, our interns are immersed in a clinical environment, shadow local physicians, and learn about disease diagnosis, treatment courses, and pharmacology.

name: Hindu YUVA at Rice
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to bring together both Hindu and non-Hindu students to raise awareness about what Hinduism is. As one of the largest and oldest religions in the world, we believe it is necessary that there be an organization on campus to represent the interests of the Hindu community present here. We hope to create a comfortable atmosphere where students can come and celebrate their culture. We hope to achieve these goals by having team-building games, yoga and meditation, and exciting debates and presentations at our weekly meetings. All in all, we want to build a small Hindu community within Rice so that students can come and discover a home away from home. Please visit www.hinduyuva.org to learn more
summary: Hindu YUVA (Youth for Unity, Virtues, and Action) is a socio-cultural organization that promotes awareness of Hindu culture and way of life, with a specific focus on social life, volunteerism, and cultural/educational programs.

name: Rice Hellenic Student Association
description: As Rice University community members, we believe that celebrating and sharing cultural heritage promotes the multicultural ethos of the university. We hope to bring together those who identify with the Hellenic identity or culture while engaging with the entire Rice community. We will achieve our goals through building a community for Greek and Cypriot-identifying Rice students and faculty members and by organizing events that promote Hellenic cultural awareness on campus.
summary: An organization dedicated to bringing together Greek- and Cypriot-identifying students who will in turn promote Hellenic culture on campus.

name: Rice Student Birding Club
description:
Welcome to Rice Student Birding Club!

Join GroupMe --> https://www.groupme.com/join_group/98498056/ryyFWGSM

Join ListServ --> https://mailman.rice.edu/mailman/listinfo/ricebirding

Follow Instagram --> www.instagram.com/ricebirding

 

Weekly birdwalks on Fridays! Special events and off-campus trips! We here at RSBC do everything related to birds and birding. Everyone is welcome. No experience required. We have binoculars and other materials if you need to borrow them. No barriers to join the fun!

 

The mission of Rice Student Birding Club is to foster a community of students who share a passion for birds and nature. We aim to promote and provide opportunities for birdwatching, education, conservation, and research among our members and the public.

 
The Rice Student Birding Club exists to foster curiosity and appreciation for the diversity and beauty of birds and their habitats. We seek to inspire our members to become lifelong learners and advocates for birds and the environment and empower them to participate by improving the accessibility of birding.
summary: The RSBC is an organization dedicated to exposing students to campus bird biodiversity and making birdwatching an accessible hobby.

name: RISE LATAM
description:
RISE LATAM has the goal of providing a space for the community of international students who grew up in Latin America. The club is open to everyone in the Rice undergraduate community interested in learning about Latin American culture and being an international student in the United States.

 

RISE LATAM will focus on highlighting some of the struggles international students from Latin America face as Rice undergraduate students. This goal will dictate several, if not all, of the events that RISE LATAM will organize and carry out throughout the academic year. Furthermore, RISE LATAM will also focus on providing resources to its members, including academic, professional, and personal resources. 

 

We are committed to creating an environment where our members can freely express themselves in Spanish and Portuguese, their native languages, and engage in meaningful discussions, cultural exchanges, and social activities. Furthermore, RISE LATAM will also provide a space for its members to have discussions about dealing with homesickness and not being able to go back home for extended periods of time, since this is another major problem faced by several international students. With these ideals and goals in mind, RISE LATAM will create an integrated space that allows for the celebration of the intersecting identities of being Latin American and being an international student at Rice.
summary: RISE LATAM is a student organization focused on the experiences of international students from Latin America, providing networking, resources, and events for students who have lived a big portion of their life in Latin America.

name: Filipino Student Association
description: Rice Filipino Student Association is open to all students interested in celebratging Filipino culture. Abbreviated as Rice FSA, the organization centers on (1) tight-knit, local community engagement; (2) cultural enrichment; and (3) cross-cultural appreciation to provide a space for students to celebrate one another.
summary: Rice Filipino Student Association (Rice FSA) is open to all students interested in Filipino culture. We focus on community engagement, cultural enrichment, and cross-cultural appreciation to create a space to celebrate and learn from one another.

name: Rice German Club
description: We're a cultural club bringing German events to Rice every year! All are welcome, no matter their skill level in German. Be sure to follow us on Instagram at @ricegermanclub, and join our Groupme for updates and announcements!
summary: We aim to bring opportunities to learn about and engage in German language and culture to Rice campus, providing access to learning and cultural experiences otherwise only accessible within German-speaking countries.

name: Rice University Science Bowl
description: Rice University Science Bowl (RUSB) is a student-run club that is dedicated to promoting a passion for STEM education in high school students via the National Science Bowl competition. Science Bowl is an intensive and fast-paced team buzzer competition that tests high school students in 6 different fields, including biology, chemistry, physics, math, energy, and earth and space science. The difficulty of these questions ranges from advanced high school curricula to college-level concepts and sometimes even beyond at the highest levels of the competition. RUSB aims to promote an interest in STEM through this exciting and interactive competition via 2 primary initiatives: (1) Hosting Rice's first ever invitational Science Bowl competition for teams across the nation, and (2) Reaching out to and supporting STEM interest at local underprivileged high schools.
summary: Rice University Science Bowl (RUSB) is dedicated to promoting a passion for STEM education in high schoolers via 2 primary initiatives: (1) Hosting Rice's first Science Bowl invitational, and (2) Supporting STEM interest in underprivileged high schools.

name: Humanities Research Center
description: The Humanities Research Center (HRC) is an internationally recognized hub for fostering innovative intellectual inquiry in the humanities and the arts at Rice. In its current iteration, the HRC has three main goals: 1. to shape and support research and engagement practices that are locally rooted and globally linked; 2. to advance undergraduate and graduate education beyond disciplinary bounds through seminars, workshops, and research collaborations; and 3. to raise the visibility and impact of our scholarship and public engagement in key areas of humanistic and artistic inquiry. The HRC invites engagement from all School of Humanities faculty and students and seeks to include and support both disciplinary and interdisciplinary research endeavors and collaborations.
summary: The Humanities Research Center (HRC) is an internationally recognized hub for fostering innovative intellectual inquiry in the humanities and the arts at Rice.

name: Rice Taekwondo Club
description:
Rice Taekwondo Club welcomes any student, faculty, or staff of Rice University interested in practicing Taekwondo. We will offer group training sessions for everyone regardless of previous experience. You're welcome to join the practice sessions to start or continue practicing Taekwondo (both WT and ITF), learn the art of Taekwondo through forms, get martial arts or self-defense skills, or simply engage in regular physical exercise through commitment to the weekly practice sessions.

We work through everything from basic kicks and poomsae to advanced moves like tornado kicks and sparring, even experimenting with gymnastics. This year, we even performed a demonstration for Korean Culture Night at Rice! We’re always open to trying new things and helping each other improve, so if you have any specific areas you’d like to practice—whether it's TKD, martial arts, or strength training—feel free to let us know. You’re welcome to join at any level, anytime!
summary: We aim to provide training opportunities to anyone in the Rice community interested in practicing Taekwondo regardless of experience. Attend group sessions to gain self-defense skills, learn the art of Taekwondo and the principles of Black Belt.

name: Bangladeshi Student Association of Rice
summary: We want to create a community where students from Bangladesh can celebrate their traditions, festivals & culture with fellow countrymen and the rest of Rice University.

name: Rice Kasama
description: Rice Kasama is a culturally diverse hip-hop dance group based at Rice University, proudly representing the vibrant Southeast Asian dance scene on campus. With a mission to fuse the dynamic energy of hip-hop with the rich cultural tapestry of Southeast Asia, this talented and passionate group of dancers comes together to create performances that are both visually captivating and emotionally resonant.
summary: Rice Kasama is a culturally diverse hip-hop dance group based at Rice University, proudly representing the vibrant Southeast Asian dance scene on campus fusing cultural diversity with high-energy performances, community engagement, and inclusivity.

name: Electrical and Computer Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: Follow us on Instagram as we celebrate student wins, spotlights and be informed on our upcoming events: https://www.instagram.com/rice.ece.gsa?igsh=MXNuMmZpMWg3ZzI4MA==
summary: The ECE GSA exists to augment the organizational, educational, professional, and social aspects of the ECE graduate student experience. It serves as a connection to Rice’s overall GSA to voice larger concerns and gain supplementary support.

name: Graduate Muslim Student Association
description:
Salaam (peace be upon you)!

GMSA was created with graduate students' needs in mind. One of our goals is to foster community among graduate students who are Muslim, who identify with Muslim-majority countries, and who are friends of the community. Additionally, we will advocate to meet the needs of our students. We are developing under Graduate and Postdoctoral Studies as we develop our goals as a community. As a subset of the larger Muslim Student Association, we hope to host social, cultural, and educational events.
summary: A community space for Muslim graduate students and friends

name: Rice New Energy Fund
description: The Rice New Energy Fund (RNEF) is the world's first student-managed investment fund focused on the energy transition. RNEF was launched in August 2021 to give students a platform to partake in active investment management while engaging with the complexities of the energy transition. This novel student initiative complements Rice’s introduction of the business major for undergrads. Given the inflection point in finance talent at Rice and the energy transition in Houston, RNEF strives to nurture the next generation of climate investors.
summary: The Rice New Energy Fund (RNEF) is the world's first student-managed investment fund focused on the energy transition.

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society of Rice University
description: The Alexander Hamilton Society of Rice University is dedicated to the following principles: an appreciation that the world remains a dangerous place in which our power must be exercised with prudence, and where the primary threats to our security come from states that deny freedom to their own people and from non-state actors who embrace hatred and violence; a conviction, rooted in the history of the last century, that the world is a better, safer, and more prosperous place when the United States is willing and able to lead; and a commitment to maintaining the moral authority and material strength on which that leadership rests; a measured pride in the success of the American experiment; an understanding that America’s greatness is the result of its commitment to individual liberty, limited government, economic freedom, the rule of law, human dignity, and democracy; and a belief that the fundamental aim of every aspect of our policy, foreign and domestic, must be to defend these principles at home and ultimately to encourage their spread abroad; a clear recognition that, in such a world, our true friends and reliable partners are other democratic nations with whom we share an enduring commonality of values, and not simply a temporary convergence of interests; a firm belief that, time and again, in peace and in war, the ability of the American political system to profit from vigorous public discussion has proved its worth; and that, at this moment in our history, our public discussion of foreign, economic, and national security policy stands very much in need of renewal.
summary: The Rice University chapter a non-partisan, not-for-profit, national organization that seeks to identify, educate, and launch young men and women into foreign policy and national security careers.

name: Machine Learning at Rice
description:
Machine Learning @ Rice is a student organization that aims to provide a space for Rice students interested in machine learning to come together to gain practical knowledge and work on exciting projects. If you’re interested in getting involved in research, learning how to implement common industry tools, build real projects, or learning more about Machine Learning in general this is the right club for you!

ML@Rice engages with the Rice community in a variety of ways:

Opportunities and Resources

We act as the central hub for gathering and providing access to a wide range of artificial intelligence opportunities and resources, encompassing internships, research positions, events, conferences, and more, to support and enrich the Rice community's growth and engagement with artificial intelligence.

Lectures, Seminars, and Conferences

We enlighten the Rice community about the multifaceted world of artificial intelligence, covering its potential and limitations, ethical considerations, recent breakthroughs, and more to foster a well-informed and ethically aware ecosystem by holding lectures, seminars, and conferences around campus featuring faculty speakers and leading experts in AI.

Hands-on Experiences

We enable members to acquire practical AI development skills through hosting a variety of hands-on activities, including projects, competitions, and workshops, fostering an environment of active learning and innovation.

 

Questions? Contact us at mlclub.rice@gmail.com

Join our Slack here!
summary: Advocating and building community for undergraduate students with all levels of experience and interest in machine learning.

name: Rice Medical Bridges
description:
Medical Bridges, a Houston-based 501(c)(3) non-profit organization, is committed to building a foundation for a more equitable and healthy future globally by redistributing medical donations and supplies to underserved communities abroad. This mission is advanced through strategic partnerships with the medical sector, donors, and supporters. To extend its impact and engage more volunteers, Medical Bridges is launching a collegiate chapter at Rice University, called Rice Medical Bridges. 

Addressing the challenge of an abundant supply of medical donations but a lack of volunteer support for sorting and shipping, Rice Medical Bridges seeks to enhance volunteer participation. By offering unique incentives, we aim to make volunteering with Medical Bridges a preferred choice among Rice students, thereby growing their network of volunteers dedicated to healthcare equity. This initiative not only supports global health efforts but also enriches the educational and community service experience of Rice University students.
summary: Rice Medical Bridges, in partnership with the Houston-based non-profit Medical Bridges, is dedicated to advancing healthcare equity through a significant increase in volunteer engagement by mobilizing a substantial number of Rice student volunteers.

name: Biochemistry and Cell Biology Graduate Student Association
description: The BCB GSA represents the interests of all graduate students in the Biochemistry and Cell Biology Graduate Program. Any graduate student is welcome to discuss matters of concern, introduce new ideas to modify the graduate program, or suggest community building or career development activities. BCB GSA officers act as liaisons between the graduate students and graduate program administrators. The BCB GSA also organizes events where graduate students can mingle socially and academically. All graduate students are encouraged to participate in GSA-sponsored activities. As the program welcomes new first-year graduate students, the BCB GSA will aid in the new students' orientation to the department.
summary: The Graduate Student Association for students in the Biochemistry and Cell Biology Graduate Program. The BCB GSA represents the interests of all BCB graduate students and organizes social events.

name: Rice Zouk
description: Rice Zouk is a dance club. We focus on partner dancing, promoting a fun and safe space to explore movement. All are welcome!
summary: We are a dance club to practice Brazilian zouk, a social partner dance, in a fun and safe space.  All are welcome!

name: Rice Women’s Rugby Football Club
summary: We are a women’s rugby club open to all with an interest in learning and trying this exciting sport. We aim to be a competitive club team, coming from many different backgrounds to compete, have fun and share this sport.

name: Third World Students' Alliance
description: The Third World Students' Alliance is a graduate student organization dedicated to fostering a supportive network among Rice graduate students who are from or interested in Third World backgrounds and providing political education, social engagement, and mutual aid.
summary: The Third World Students' Alliance is an organization dedicated to fostering a supportive network open to all Rice graduate students from or interested in Third World backgrounds and providing political education, social engagement, and mutual aid.

name: Out & Allied
description: We are a student lead organization at the Jones business school. We are the LGBTQ+ affinity group for business students. We believe in fostering community outreach, and professional networking for LGBTQ+ professionals in the workplace. We also represent the needs of the LGBTQ+ population at rice business school.
summary: We are a student lead organization at the Jones business school. We are the LGBTQ+ affinity group for business students. We believe in fostering community outreach, and professional networking for LGBTQ+ professionals in the workplace.

name: Fulbright@Rice
description:
Welcome to Fulbright@Rice!

We are a vibrant, diverse community of Fulbright graduate students, passionate about education and research. We love learning about each other and welcome the opportunity to get to know you. If you are a graduate student or a postdoc who currently holds a Fulbright, or has held in the past a graduate Fulbright, we welcome you to reach out!
summary: Welcome to Fulbright@Rice! If you are a graduate student or a postdoc who currently holds a Fulbright, or has held in the past a graduate Fulbright, we welcome you to reach out!

name: Rice Education
description:
About Rice Education

At Rice Education, we believe that teaching is more than a career—it’s a catalyst for change. Whether you dream of leading a classroom, influencing education policy, or simply want to better understand how people learn, our programs open doors to skills and perspectives that will last a lifetime.

 

Your Pathways at Rice

  • MAT 4+1 Degree – Earn your bachelor’s and a Master of Arts in Teaching in just one extra year. You’ll graduate with Texas teacher certification, real classroom experience, and a competitive edge in the job market.

  • Minor in Teaching – Explore the art and science of education while keeping your career options open. The minor pairs well with any major and gives you a strong foundation in leadership, communication, and problem-solving.

  • Distribution II Course – Typically offered in the spring, EDUC 301 Philosophical, Historical, and Sociological Foundations of Education is analysis of events and ideas that have shaped the philosophy and practice of American schools today. Appropriate for all students interested in the influences and stresses that have created a unique educational system in our culturally diverse country. 

Why Take an Education Class?

  • Develop essential skills in communication, empathy, and critical thinking.

  • Learn how to design engaging, inclusive learning experiences—valuable in any field.

  • Gain hands-on experience working with schools and communities in Houston.

  • Join a community of passionate, driven peers and faculty mentors.

Why Become a Teacher?

  • Shape the next generation and make a measurable impact every day.

  • Step into a profession that values creativity, lifelong learning, and leadership.

  • Open doors to careers in education, nonprofits, government, and beyond.

  • Leave Rice with the confidence, credentials, and network to thrive.

Whether you’re curious about education or committed to making it your calling, Rice Education is here to help you explore, prepare, and lead.
summary: At Rice Education, we prepare changemakers. Whether you take EDUC 301 (Distribution Group II), explore our Minor in Teaching, or complete the MAT 4+1 to become a certified teacher in five years, you’ll gain the skills and experience to shape the future!

name: Theatre Program
description: The Theatre minor is a program that takes a broad-based approach to understanding the facets of theatre-making through classes in stagecraft (costume construction or theatre technology), acting or design, history of theatre, and a required production practicum. Through coursework focused on inquiry-based learning, coupled with production experience where students utilize practical experience, theatre students deeply engage in both theory and practice. Theatre courses are open to all students who want to gain a greater appreciation for the art of theatre.
summary: The Rice Theatre Program is a minor with classes in stagecraft (costume construction or theatre technology), acting or design, history of theatre, and a required production practicum. Open to all students!

name: The Moody Experience
description:
The Moody Experience = Rice Beyond the Classroom

 

Thanks to the $50 million Gift for Student Opportunity from the Moody Foundation, Rice students will have even greater opportunity to participate in enriching “beyond the classroom” experiences for many years to come. 

 

What will your Moody X = ?

 

Expanding your worldview on an international trip as a Moody Global Fellow?

 

Unleashing your curiosity by doing research as a Moody Research Fellow?

 

Finding your path to a meaningful career through the Moody Gateway Program?

 

Engaging your community through service in the Moody Civic Immersion program?

 

Pursuing your passion with support from the Moody Opportunity Fund?

 

Learn more about the many opportunities unlocked by the Moody Experience!
summary:
The Moody Experience = Rice Beyond the Classroom 
Thanks to the Moody Foundation, current and future Owls have unprecedented support and opportunity for a wide variety of experiences including international travel, civic immersion, research, and more!

name: Department of Biosciences
description: BioSciences at Rice is home to undergraduate and doctoral degree programs in Biochemistry & Cell Biology and Ecology & Evolutionary Biology. We also participate in multidisciplinary degree programs in Biological Science, Environmental Science, and Neuroscience, as well as professional science master’s programs in Bioscience & Health Policy and Environmental Analysis. We are investigating fundamental properties of living organisms and impacts on the future quality of life on earth. Our research spans animal behavior, biochemistry, biophysics, cancer biology, cell biology, computational biology, conservation biology, developmental biology, ecology, evolution, genetics, microbiology, neurobiology, plant biology, signal transduction, structural biology, synthetic biology, and systems biology. Our faculty members play prominent positions in diverse scientific organizations and have received national and international recognition for their research and mentoring accomplishments.
summary: Biochemistry & Cell Biology and Ecology & Evolutionary Biology.

name: Phi Beta Kappa: Rice Chapter
description:
Since our founding in 1776, Phi Beta Kappa has celebrated excellence in the liberal arts and sciences and championed freedom of thought. As America’s most prestigious academic honor society, we are uniquely equipped to advocate for the value and benefits of liberal arts and sciences education.

The Rice University chapter of Phi Beta Kappa, Beta of Texas, was founded in 1929. Each year, our chapter inducts a new group of initiates, recognizes the outstanding teaching performance of a professor, and awards the outstanding academic achievements of one of the student inductees.

While other honor societies recognize academic excellence in pre-professional fields such as engineering or architecture, the purpose of Phi Beta Kappa is to honor outstanding achievement in the liberal arts and sciences, in courses that reflect a breadth of intellectual interest and a love of learning for its own sake.

Find out more about the Phi Beta Kappa Society on the national website: https://www.pbk.org

Learn more about the Rice chapter and the criteria here: https://pbk.rice.edu
summary: As America’s most prestigious academic honor society, the purpose of Phi Beta Kappa is to honor outstanding achievement in the liberal arts and sciences, in courses that reflect a breadth of intellectual interest and a love of learning for its own sake.

name: Rice Association For African Development
description:
RAADE - Rice Association for African Development

RAADE is a student-led organization dedicated to driving transformative change across the African continent through innovation and collaborative problem-solving. We connect Rice students with real-world development challenges by partnering directly with African organizations, businesses, and NGOs.

Our Mission: To empower Rice students to create scalable, human-centered solutions for Africa's most pressing challenges while building the next generation of global leaders committed to sustainable development.

https://youtu.be/Ax2EFMEwMxE?si=jR6ZEHEfQP1HOCTl
summary: We are a student club that plans to host innovation labs where members of the Rice community can provide their unique backgrounds in solving some of the challenges faced by African Businesses and NGOs

name: Hearts for the Homeless Rice
description: The purpose of Hearts for the Homeless Rice is to provide free and informative heart health education events to the homeless community near Rice and in the Houston area. This includes providing free blood pressure screenings, information on healthcare resources around Houston, and donations of basic healthcare supplies to the homeless community. H4H Rice also hopes to educate its members on hidden difficulties within the homeless community surrounding access to healthcare and other resources.
summary: H4H Rice aims to provide free and informative heart health events to the homeless community in Houston. These events will consist of blood pressure screenings, information on healthcare resources, and donations of basic supplies.

name: Medical Arts Collaborative
description:
1. MAC hopes to expand upon Rice University’s interdisciplinary studies by exploring and advocating for the intersections between art and medicine. We hope to raise awareness of the challenges faced in the healthcare industry through various mediums of art along with advocating for the diverse ways in which art can play a role in medicine.
  2. Here are our current goals as MAC:
  3. 1. Plan and host an annual gala displaying works of medical artists to engage Rice, Houston, national, and international communities.
  4. 2. Connect with local medical artists at Rice, TMC, Houston, and more to promote collaboration and conversation.
  5. 3. Hold campaigns, workshops, and panels to promote the integration of arts in healthcare policy, education, and practice.
summary: MAC explores the intersection between art and medicine at Rice. We hope to raise awareness of the challenges faced in the healthcare industry through various mediums of art and explore diverse ways in which art can play a role in medicine.

name: Rice Rangilas
description: Rice Rangilas aims to spread awareness of Indian culture through Garba, a unique style of traditional Indian folk dance. Our team offers a convenient option for students with busier schedules, requiring only up to 2 hours of practice per week, compared to other desi dance teams at Rice that practice ~6 hours per week. Rangilas performs once in the fall at Rice SAS's annual showcase, Dhamaka, and once in the spring, at our own hosted showcase. We aspire to foster meaningful, lifelong friendships through our shared passion for dance.
summary: Rice Rangilas is an Indian folk dance team that teaches Garba. Open to all, we offer students with busier schedules the opportunity to dance for 2 hrs weekly, compared to other teams that practice up to 6 hrs weekly. We perform twice annually.

name: Rice Blue Sole Steppers
description: The Blue Sole Steppers is a new step team that welcomes dancers of all experience levels to learn and participate in the world of step! Throughout the year, we will participate in flash mobs, Soul Night, and fun rehearsals and team bonding experiences! Step is a great way to exercise, make friends, and have fun! All while entertaining the masses.
summary: The Blue Sole Steppers is a new step team that welcomes dancers of all experience levels to learn and participate in the world of step! Throughout the year, we will participate in flash mobs, Soul Night, and fun rehearsals and team bonding experiences!

name: NEOS Jazz Band
description: We play jazz.
summary: We play jazz.

name: Rice Hong Kong Student Association
description: As members of the Rice community, we believe that all cultures should be represented and promoted across campus, and that HKSA can provide students with a space and opportunity to promote awareness of Hong Kong culture and the Cantonese language through a variety of activities. We also hope to strengthen this community for those who speak Cantonese, identify with Hong Kong, or want to learn more about the culture and language, while spreading cultural awareness across campus and collaborating with other organizations that share this goal. All are welcome who support this goal.
summary: As members of the Rice community, we believe that HKSA provides students with a space and opportunity to promote awareness of Hong Kong culture and the Cantonese language.

name: Handcrafted Hope
description: Handcrafted Hope is a non-profit philanthropic and volunteer organization. The president/s organize crocheting and knitting events to engage the Rice community to use their skills in making clothing. Our main annual event will be a clothing drive in the winter where we collect donated or knitted clothes to give back to the community. We also wish to raise awareness on the state of underserved communities and funds as well to donate. Our goal is to help alleviate the need for clothing and money in the healthcare and homeless setting. This organization serves as a bridge between Rice and the underserved members and hospitalized patients of our community.
summary: Handcrafted Hope is a non-profit philanthropic and volunteer crocheting and knitting club. We donate our handcrafted items to hospitals and homeless centers. We want to reduce the need for clothing and blankets in the healthcare and homeless setting.

name: Mulan Dance Crew
description: As members of a diverse Rice student body, we see values in creating a Chinese dance club that brings together students with different cultural backgrounds through one form of art. Open to all, we aim to bridge the gap between Chinese and American culture through dance movements as a mutual language, while incorporating Chinese history and human rights movements (especially contemporary Chinese feminism) as a background to our workshops and training. Through our yearly production and annual showcase, we hope also to bring authentic Chinese culture to a broader audience within and beyond the extended Rice community. We believe in the therapeutic power of dance which helps us heal through self-expression and the connection of mind and body in a room, by implementing special workshops facing the entire Rice community and using dance as therapy following the Chinese Nüshū model, we hope to create caring club culture that echoes Rice University’s core values.
summary: We are a Chinese dance club that builds an inclusive community through the art and therapeutic power of dance. Open to all, we explore Chinese dance and its history, including its diverse forms and connections to Chinese feminism.

name: Rice Undergraduate Marketing Association
description:
• What do we do 
    • Educate students on marketing careers 
    • Prepare students to achieve the jobs they’re looking for
    • Provide experiential learning (cases, conferences, etc)
  • Why do we do it 
    • Exposing students to careers they otherwise wouldn’t have known about
    • Connect students interested in marketing to build a community
  • How do we do it
    • We provide resources (career, knowledge) and alumni
    • Host events with professionals and educators
    • Create opportunities for real-world implementation of marketing strategies
summary: RUMA connects students passionate about marketing through career exposure, education, and experiential opportunities.

name: First-Generation, Limited-Income Legal Collective @ Rice
description: The First-Generation, Limited-Income Legal Collective at Rice is intended to be a supportive space for pre-law students, with a special focus on first-generation, limited-income, and working-class students at Rice University. Its purpose is to provide a space for students of these backgrounds to share and affirm experiences, advocate for our communities, provide resources to underrepresented students, advance accessibility within the legal profession, and support career aspirations. All are welcome who support this mission.
summary: The First-Generation, Limited-Income Legal Collective at Rice is intended to be a supportive space for pre-law students, with a special focus on first-generation, limited-income, and working-class students at Rice University.

name: Association for Women Geoscientists Rice University Student Chapter
description: The Association for Women Geoscientists Rice University Student Chapter (hereinafter the “Association”) is an international organization devoted to enhancing the quality and level of participation of women in geosciences and to encouraging girls and young women (including cis-gender, transgender, gender fluid and non-binary persons) to explore geoscience careers.
summary: The AWG Rice University Student Chapter is an international organization devoted to enhancing the quality and level of participation of women (including cis-gender, transgender, gender fluid and non-binary persons) in geoscience!

name: Rice Justice Project
description: At Rice Justice Project, we engage with the community to raise awareness for criminal justice issues, advocate for reform, and work to facilitate change. In addition to our advocacy and community engagement, we connect students with families with incarcerated loved ones preparing for their parole interviews. Our project is rooted in the philosophy that all humans deserve to be treated with respect and true justice is rehabilitative rather than punitive. With the help of Texas Incarcerated Families Association, we provide students with training on how to draft parole letters, construct advocacy packets, and provide emotional support to best serve their incarcerated parole applicants. In doing so, we hope to challenge certain stigmas surrounding incarcerated persons, while also providing students with an opportunity to tangibly aid some of the most underserved members of our community.
summary: We advocate for criminal justice reform and help justice-impacted families create parole packets. Our project is rooted in the philosophy that all humans deserve to be treated with respect and true justice is rehabilitative rather than punitive.

name: Team ENOUGH Rice
description: As members of the Rice community, we believe that by working together we can fulfill our overall objective of addressing issues having to do with gun violence prevention by raising awareness and being a direct voice in bringing about change in the plight of gun violence in the Houston area and around the country. To tackle these issues, we must pass common sense gun laws and address how guns have oversaturated our communities, creating easy access to firearms. But we must also address how systemic racism has facilitated environments where police brutality, white supremacy, poverty, unemployment, lack of affordable housing, poor education, homophobia, lack of accessible healthcare, and a criminal punishment system thrive. We must address all these issues if bloodshed is to stop running rampant in our communities. All are welcome who support this mission.
summary: Team ENOUGH, Brady's youth-led initiative, works to educate young people about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action to prevent it. We are student- advocates, who are fighting to end gun violence at Rice, Houston, and beyond.

name: Rice Women on Bikes
summary: Rice WOB fosters community with a particular focus on forming a supportive community among female and non-binary cyclists, offering rides, social events, and resource sharing. Cyclists of all skill levels are welcome who support this mission.

name: Rice Coalition on Substance Use
description: The mission of the RCSU is to reframe how the campus approaches substance use, by supporting those in need and working to implement policies that align with our values of well-being and mutual support. This includes hosting educational events, workshops, and initiatives that highlight alternative strategies for care, as well as creating spaces for open discussion about substance use challenges. Through our programs and partnerships, we strive to ensure that all members of the Rice community have access to the resources, education, and support they need to make informed decisions and seek help when necessary.
summary: The Rice Coalition on Substance Use (RCSU) is dedicated to promoting awareness, education, and support regarding substance use on our campus to foster a sense of community more inclusive to the diverse student body at Rice.

name: Rice Hawai'i Club
description: The Rice Hawai’i Club invites all students to learn about and immerse themselves in the local culture of Hawai’i. Activities include eating/cooking local cuisine, learning about Hawaiian history, etc. Along with spreading the traditions of the islands, RHC will also give members from Hawai’i a sense of community at Rice where they can truly feel at home and share their sense of pride for Hawai’i with one another.
summary: The Rice Hawai’i Club invites all students to learn about and immerse themselves in the local culture of Hawai’i. Along with spreading the traditions of the islands, RHC will also give members from Hawai’i a second home.

name: Rice Energy Trading Group
description:
Rice Energy Trading Group, the energy and commodity trading club at Rice University, is established to provide a platform for students to explore, understand, and engage with the dynamic world of energy and commodity markets. Our purpose is to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and real-world trading practices, fostering a community of future leaders in the energy and commodity sectors.

The purpose of this organization shall be to:

1) Educate members on the intricacies of energy and commodity trading, including market dynamics, risk management, and emerging trends in sustainable resource allocation;

2) Develop practical skills through simulations, case studies, and hands-on trading experiences that prepare students for careers in energy finance and commodity trading;

3) Connect students with industry professionals, facilitating networking opportunities and exposure to diverse career paths within the energy and commodity trading sectors;

4) Promote interdisciplinary collaboration, encouraging members to apply their diverse academic backgrounds to address complex challenges in global resource markets;

5) Cultivate awareness of the critical role that energy and commodity trading plays in global economics, geopolitics, and sustainable development; and

6) Provide hands-on experience in commodity trading through the management of donor-funded commodity trading pods.
summary: Rice Energy Trading Group, the energy trading club at Rice University, provides a platform for students to explore, learn, and engage with global energy and commodity markets.

name: Long Distance Squad
description: We are an open-to-all run club that seeks to promote a fun and inclusive enviornment where anyone can engage in fitness. We aim to host fun events, collaborate with businesses outside of Rice, etc.
summary: We are an open-to-all run club that seeks to promote a fun and inclusive environment where anyone can engage in fitness. We aim to host fun events, collaborate with businesses outside of Rice, etc.

name: Rice Undergraduate Real Estate Society
description: Rice Undergraduate Real Estate Society is committed to immersing students in the dynamic real estate industry, offering a comprehensive environment for learning, networking, and professional growth. Our mission is to provide opportunities for students to gain practical work experience, develop industry connections, and build a solid foundation for successful careers in real estate.
summary: RURES is committed to immersing students in the dynamic real estate industry, offering a comprehensive environment for learning, networking, and professional growth.

name: SKY Campus at Rice
description:
The purpose of SKY Campus at Rice is to create a mindful campus community where students, faculty, and staff thrive with clarity, resilience, and purpose. We offer stress management tools and techniques for personal and professional success through evidence-based breathwork, meditation, yoga, and personal awareness seminars, while also providing opportunities for community service and leadership development.

Our approach is built on four pillars:
1. Evidence-Based Practices: We teach breathwork and meditation for well-being and resilience.
2. Inclusion and Social Connection: We provide a safe space for meaningful connections, dialogue, and a sense of belonging.
3. Service and Leadership: We foster leadership skills through teamwork and purpose-driven service projects.
4. Emotional Intelligence Training: We explore mental frameworks for living a happy and successful life on and off campus.
summary: The purpose of SKY Campus at Rice is to teach students, faculty, and staff evidence-based stress management techniques and to promote personal and professional success through breathwork, meditation, yoga, and personal development seminars.

name: Rice Sport Management Society
description: To better prepare Rice Sport Management students to work in the professional field. We will strive to open up networking, experiential, and professional opportunities for our members. We will expand Rice's connections within the sports industry, providing students access to professionals in their desired fields
summary: To better prepare Rice Sport Management students to work in the professional field.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America
description: The purpose of YDSA is to establish student involvement and activism surrounding equality, radical justice, democracy, & socialism at Rice; this purpose also serves to deepen student understanding of principles of solidarity, freedom & dedication to political & social involvement.
summary: The purpose of YDSA is to establish student involvement and activism surrounding equality, radical justice, democracy, & socialism at Rice.

name: Senior Health Compass
description: Students will be paired with seniors to provide services that range from: reminders and follow-ups with seniors about their medications and doctor appointments; creating a compiled list of current medications and medical history. Education events for seniors about navigating online medical platforms and insurance/Medicare plans will be held, in addition to informative sessions for students about common geriatric medical problems. Students will receive the opportunity to advocate our services to local seniors through health fairs.
summary: Our mission is to serve seniors in their healthcare journey and assist them in becoming self-sufficient in their navigation of the modern field of geriatric medicine, while educating the Rice student body about senior healthcare.

name: TAMID at Rice
description: TAMID provides students with experiential business opportunities. Members can become engaged with our education curriculum, and then join either our consulting branch or investment fund, working on projects with real Israeli start-ups.
summary: TAMID provides students with experiential business opportunities. Members can become engaged with our education curriculum, followed joining either our consulting branch or investment fund, working on projects with real Israeli start-ups.

name: Rice Global
description: Rice Global drives Rice University's global strategy, fostering international research partnerships worldwide. With a focus on key research areas, we've established academic collaborations across continents, including our Paris Center, serving as a European hub, as well as expanding Rice's international presence with the launch of Rice Global India to underscore the university’s commitment to fostering global collaboration and advancing its contributions to India’s rapidly growing education and technology sectors.
summary: Fostering innovative international collaborations to amplify the impact of Rice research, accelerate solutions to global challenges, and extend the global reach and reputation of Rice University.

name: Rice Swim Club
summary: Rice Swim Club welcomes swimmers of all ability levels, and offers opportunities to practice and compete against other college swim clubs in a fun environment! We have several meets each semester, including regional and national championships.

name: Rice SAAVETX EF
description:
The SAAVETX Education Fund is a non-profit organization dedicated to promoting civic engagement and education related to the South Asian community in Texas. We believe in empowering individuals with the knowledge and resources needed to participate actively in the democratic process and make informed decisions. All who support this mission are welcome.

Our organization provides educational programs, resources, and outreach initiatives to increase voter registration, voter turnout, and political awareness among South Asian Texans. We work to bridge the gap between communities and elected officials, advocating for issues that are important to our community and fostering a greater understanding of the democratic system.

Through collaborative efforts, community partnerships, and grassroots organizing, the SAAVETX Education Fund strives to create a more inclusive, informed, and engaged South Asian community that actively participates in shaping the future of Texas. We are committed to promoting social justice, equality, and civic empowerment.
summary: We're an organization dedicated to promoting civic engagement and education related to the South Asian community in Texas through programs, resources, and outreach that boost voter turnout and empowerment. Open to all who support this mission.

name: Sunrise Rice
description:
The Sunrise mission statement: 

We believe everyone should have access to clear air and water, full pantries of food, affordable housing, free public transportation, good and meaningful jobs, vibrant community centers, and lives filled with joy. We’re building a movement to make that happen. 

We’re organizing thousands of young people to win a Green New Deal that will: stop the climate crisis, create good union jobs for all, invest in racial and economic justice, and reimagine our government to be by and for the people.
summary: We are a Rice University chapter of the national student-run climate activism movement, Sunrise.

name: Medic Outpost
description: Medic Outpost’s purpose is to allow undergraduate students to enhance traditional healthcare in these remote areas by training community members to be first responders, boosting health workers' skills and knowledge, and supplying life-saving equipment.
summary: Medic Outpost’s purpose is to allow undergraduate students to enhance traditional healthcare in these remote areas by training community members to be first responders, boosting health workers' skills and knowledge, and supplying life-saving equipment.

name: Rice IGNITE
description: Rice IGNITE is part of a national organization dedicated to empowering women and nonbinary people in the political sphere. Reports show that one in five girls have personally been discouraged from engaging with or participating in politics, and there exists a massive gender gap in the field of policy. We want to invest in these communities to create changemakers who will lead us into the future.
summary: A club dedicated to empowering women and nonbinary people in the political sphere, engaging them in on and off-campus opportunities to get involved.

name: Universities Allied for Essential Medicines at Rice University
description:
Medicines and medical innovations should be affordable and accessible for all. We're connecting locals in Houston to resources like medication assistance programs, advocating for policy change, and promoting awareness. Do you think everyone should have equal access to healthcare & medical therapies? Click the link below to get involved ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️

http://linktr.ee/uaematrice
summary: Medicines and medical innovations should be affordable and accessible for all. We're connecting locals in Houston to resources like medication assistance programs, advocating for policy change, and promoting awareness.

name: Rice Securities Investment Group
summary: Rice Securities Investment Group (RSIG) is a club for Rice students that are passionate about value investing. We will create teams of 2-4 students to compete at stock pitch, investment, and case competitions around the country.

name: Global Partners in Hope
description: The Global Partners in Hope (GPiH) Rice Chapter works with Global Partners in Hope to help build sustainable healthcare in Togo and educate the Rice community about African healthcare systems. Closely collaborating with GPiH, we work to provide Togolese women and children with improved medical treatment. We achieve this goal by sending interns on two-week long Outreach Trips to West African countries, where they will assist physicians at the clinic and engage in humanitarian efforts.
summary: The Global Partners in Hope Chapter builds sustainable healthcare in Togo and learns about African healthcare systems through two-week Outreach Trip Internships to Togo. Interns shadow doctors at the Togolese Clinic and participate in cultural immersion.

name: Rice Animal Ethics Society
description: Rice Animal Ethics Society exists to promote and facilitate the study of animal ethics at Rice University. Our organization seeks to bring together those interested in animal ethics across disciplines. We will create new opportunities for engagement with this underappreciated field of study and provide a forum for rigorous engagement with animal ethics. Our members will help to organize animal ethics conferences, workshops, reading groups, and more.
summary: Rice Animal Ethics Society exists to promote and facilitate the study of animal ethics at Rice University.

name: Gulf Students Organization
description:
1. GSO is dedicated to fostering a strong sense of community among students from the Gulf region individuals from Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, Bahrain, and Oman while promoting academic excellence, cultural exchange, and intellectual growth.
