2. A key goal of the GSO is to host distinguished off-campus guests, including experts, scholars, and industry leaders, to facilitate economic and scientific panels. These events are designed to broaden students’ perspectives, ignite their curiosity, and inspire innovative thinking.

 

3. By creating opportunities for meaningful dialogue and interaction with leading professionals, the GSO seeks to enrich the academic and professional journeys of its members and the broader student community.
summary:
GSO is dedicated to fostering a strong sense of community among students from the
Gulf region individuals from Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, Bahrain, and
Oman while promoting academic excellence, cultural exchange, and intellectual
growth.

name: Girls into VC @ Rice
description:
Girls Into VC is the first organization dedicated to bringing girls into venture capital. We’re committed to bridging the gender gap by creating a robust pipeline of skilled, passionate young women equipped and ready to revolutionize the industry. Through targeted education, resources, and mentorship, we empower the next generation of women leaders in venture capital.

Historically, venture capital is a male-dominated field, lacking diversity on the investment team. This not only negatively affects women interested in the field, but also companies and entrepreneurs. Many women are not taught about venture capital, think joining the field is an unattainable goal, and are discouraged from joining clubs or startups because of their gender. As a result, women are not being invested in because women are not making these decisions.
summary: Girls Into VC is the first organization dedicated to bringing girls into venture capital. We’re committed to bridging the gender gap by creating a robust pipeline of skilled, passionate young women equipped and ready to revolutionize the industry.

name: Rice Agua Vida
description:
We’re THE water club at Rice. Follow us at https://www.instagram.com/rice.aguavida/
Affiliated with @isla_urbana & @islaurbanausa, a nonprofit that addresses water scarcity by harvesting rainwater.
summary: We are focused on addressing water scarcity by harvesting rainwater.

name: Tech for All Private Foundation
description:
1. By calling up the power of rice alumni and utilizing a personal network, we launch the proposition of TAPF( Tech for All Private Foundation) by unifying 50+ tech start-up owners in Houston and several independent high-level executives from Silicon Valley (personal connection), with a capital pool of 5 million dollars.

 

  1. In the process of reaching out to them, we have crafted thorough business plan and statistics model for the operation of TAPF (introduced below) and its core aim,  which is to utilize minimal personal profits benefited by the surging tech industry these years, and to combine them into a stronger flow of monetary power that could better repay society with donations more in scales(instead of fragmented personal donations). 

 

  1. By holding potential virtual or in-person event, TAPF is also an excellent platform for tech startups to network and foster stronger partnerships, both for future career as well as further development and sustainability of TAPF.

 

  1. Of the pool of money, 50 percent is donated directly to Rice-related nonprofit organizations such as 180 Degrees Consulting - Rice University Branch, Rice University Community Bridges Program, Rice University Boniuk Institute for the Study and Advancement of Religious Tolerance, Teach For America - Houston, The Kinder Institute for Urban Research; as well as some Houston local non-profit organizations such as Houston Food Bank, The Greater Houston Community Foundation, Star of Hope Mission, etc. The savings would be well-designed by our members with deep experiences in investment banking consulting internship (we partner with 180 dc of Rice branch)as well as specialists in investment (through rice alumni connections) to invest in funds or national debts with very stable returns（an annual return of 8% approximately). This prepares for the future growth and sustainability of TAPF.
summary: adsfsdff

name: Rice Quantitative Fund
description:
The Rice Quantitative Fund, founded in September 2024, is a student-led organization focusing on all aspects of quantitative finance, including but not limited to trading strategies across all frequencies, macroeconomic data analysis, quantitative risk management, trading strategy research, and academic theory verification. The organization is centered around its fund, dedicated to testing the research and optimizing trading strategies.

Historically, Rice's quantitative finance community has been overshadowed by the large student population in fundamental analysis and investment banking. By forming the organization, we seek to gather interested individuals at Rice and study quantitative finance in an academically rigorous manner.

We seek to include talents passionate about systematic research in quantitative finance. The fund is organized to have experienced “project leaders” lead independent projects, in which interested individuals can choose a project to participate in. Depending on the practicality of the research topics, the outcome could be incorporated into the fund's trading strategy, published in an online database, or applied in other ways.
summary: Our club focuses on all aspects of quantitative finance. It is centered around its fund dedicated to testing research and optimizing trading strategies. We seek to include talents who are passionate about systematic research in quantitative finance.

name: American Cancer Society
description: The American Cancer Society (ACS) is a nationwide, nonprofit organization committed to eliminating cancer and improving the lives of those affected by it. Established in 1913, ACS is a leader in cancer research, patient support, advocacy, and education. The organization funds innovative research to find cures, provides free resources such as transportation and lodging for patients, and promotes prevention and early detection to reduce cancer risks. ACS also advocates for public policies that increase access to care and address health disparities. With the support of millions of volunteers and donors, ACS is creating a world where cancer is no longer a threat to lives.
summary: The American Cancer Society (ACS) is a nationwide, nonprofit organization committed to eliminating cancer and improving the lives of those affected by it. Established in 1913, ACS is a leader in cancer research, patient support, advocacy, and education.

name: Buddhist Undergraduate Student Association
description: The Buddhist Undergraduate Student Association at Rice seeks to create a supportive and inclusive space for undergraduate students from diverse Buddhist backgrounds or interested in Buddhism to connect, share experiences, and grow in practice together.
summary: The Buddhist Undergraduate Student Association at Rice seeks to create a supportive and inclusive space for undergraduate students from diverse Buddhist backgrounds or interested in Buddhism to connect, share experiences, and grow in practice together.

name: Program in Environmental Studies
description: The Environmental Studies program offers an undergraduate minor in Environmental Studies and several interdisciplinary courses for students interested in broadening their understanding of environmental issues. The program is administered by School of Humanities. Environmental Studies is an interdisciplinary field that explores the interconnection between humans and the natural environment. Modern environmental issues reflect the complex interactions of natural and social systems at global and local scales, and the resulting impacts on the Earth have led many to ask whether humankind has entered into a new epoch in the planet’s history, one in which humans are now a key driver in the change of Earth systems. The Environmental Studies program fosters the critical, integrative thinking required to better understand the complexities of this human-nature relationship and the resultant scales of impact, and to assess and develop solutions that meet intergenerational human needs without compromising the natural systems upon which humans depend.
summary: The Environmental Studies program offers an undergraduate minor in Environmental Studies and several interdisciplinary courses for students interested in broadening their understanding of environmental issues.

name: Office of Research Security and Export Controls
description: Office of Research Security and Export Controls serves as a trusted resource to Rice’s leadership, faculty, and community of scholars in dealing with Research Security and Export control regulations. We recognize the importance of striking a balance between research security while maintaining an open and collaborative partnership with the international community.
summary: Office of Research Security and Export Controls serves as a trusted resource to Rice’s leadership, faculty, and community of scholars in dealing with Research Security and Export control regulations.

name: Center for Engineering Excellence Through Equity
description:
The Center for Engineering Excellence Through Equity aims to leverage the strengths of the Rice Engineering and Computing community to build an inclusive school culture that welcomes people from diverse backgrounds and actively seeks to implement equitable practices and institutional structures that ensure all students, faculty and staff feel valued and, most importantly, thrive at Rice University.

 

The Center for Engineering Excellence Through Equity focuses on promoting equitable practices and institutional structures through various efforts and initiatives including: - Research and Assessment. The center will collaborate with the Office of Institutional Effectiveness, the Office of Faculty Development, and Human Resources to collect data on the social climate status for all stakeholders in the Rice engineering community. This research will inform the development of strategic programming from the Dean’s Office to ensure that the proposed programming supports engineering excellence. Initiatives will be continuously assessed to ensure goals are achieved. - Community Development. The center will develop programming that facilitates relationships between stakeholders in engineering and that addresses community needs to support an inclusive culture.
summary: The Center for Engineering Excellence Through Equity aims to leverage the strengths of the Rice Engineering and Computing community to build an inclusive school culture that welcomes people from diverse backgrounds.

name: Office of Academic Support for Undergraduate Students
description: Looking to achieve your preferred academic path? Through our programming and networking with other partners in the Rice community, the Office of Academic Support for Undergraduate Students is here to help you get there. Check out our website for the most up to date information!
summary: Through Drop-In study, Peer Assisted Learning, the Peer Educator Training Program as well as Workshops and the Testing Center, we aim to ensure that every Rice undergraduate is able to pursue, progress through, and achieve their preferred academic path.

name: Department of Modern and Classical Languages, Literatures and Cultures
description: We encourage responsible and inclusive citizenship in our students as we prepare them for local, international, or global careers that require an adventurous, adaptable, and creative mindset. Students learn transformative skills that enable them to better effect change in the world and create their own unconventional paths. Our programs foster an intellectually lively and culturally diverse community where teaching and research explore critical questions around aesthetics, ethics and politics.
summary: MCLLC is an intellectually vibrant, student-friendly, and research-centered department that is home to six programs: Classical Studies, European Studies, French Studies, German Studies, Latin American Studies, and Spanish and Portuguese.

name: Information Security Office
description:
The Information Security Office protects the electronic assets of Rice University. ISO staff maintains information confidentiality, integrity, and availability of electronic data, resources, and communications. Our office defends against issues that impact the security of technology infrastructure using virus and spam prevention, system intrusion detection, and forensics. Security staff also offer guidance and specific protection measures to the Rice community.

We help with Security Assessments of Vendors, Security Investigations, Vulnerability Management, Security Awareness on Campus, Phishing, Securing Data, and Cybersecurity Compliance.
summary: The Information Security Office protects the electronic assets of Rice University. ISO staff maintains information confidentiality, integrity, and availability of electronic data, resources, and communications.

name: Electrical and Computer Engineering
description: Electrical and Computer Engineering (ECE) is at the crossroads of hardware and software – the integration of these tools to create better, faster, safer technologies for things like cars, aircraft, computers, smartphones, or surgical robots. We create, innovate, and design technologies in computing, communications, electronics, and automation. The Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Rice University provides high-quality undergraduate and graduate education. We emphasize fundamental principles that respond to and create technological change. Our programs are designed to challenge the exceptional ability of Rice students, ensuring that our graduates are prepared for leadership roles. Our research efforts involve students from all levels and concentrate on developing our fundamental understanding of scientific, mathematical, and engineering principles. We turn results into algorithms, analysis techniques, and prototypes that extend technology's frontier.
summary: Electrical and Computer Engineering (ECE) is at the crossroads of hardware and software – to create better, faster, safer technologies and to innovate and design technologies in computing, communications, electronics, and automation.

name: Bridge the Gap
description: Our organization aims to connect Rice students with local volunteer shelters and agencies in the Houston community, including those serving survivors of domestic violence, women, and unhoused individuals. Through these partnerships, we strive to provide meaningful support to agencies while fostering strong bonds between Rice undergraduates and the local community. Our volunteers will engage in a variety of initiatives, including legal and medical navigational assistance, and family-oriented support services, to help address the diverse needs of those seeking refuge and stability.
summary: Our organization aims to connect Rice students with local volunteer shelters and agencies in the Houston community, including those serving survivors of domestic violence, women, and unhoused individuals.

name: Rice Hockey Club
description: Our mission is to bring awareness to the sport of hockey in the Rice community, and allow the opportunity for anyone at any skill level to play this amazing game. We hope to build a sense of community at Rice, where members can develop their skills, both on and off the ice, that will create lasting memories.
summary: Hockey Team @Rice

name: Rice Alternative Investments Club
description: RAIC aims to expand knowledge and opportunities in the alternative investments sector for undergraduate students by increasing awareness of career paths and connecting club members with internships and networking opportunities.
summary: RAIC aims to expand knowledge and opportunities in the alternative investments sector for undergraduate students by increasing awareness of career paths and connecting club members with internships and networking opportunities.

name: Rice Madrigals
description: Rice Madrigals is a small, audition-based, choir dedicated to the performance of madrigal and classical choir pieces. Our group focuses on the harmonies, expressive storytelling, and historical performance practices of this unique vocal style. We perform at two principal concerts per year to spread the beauty of choir and music. Auditions are open to all in the fall semester. Join us in exploring this wonderful tradition!
summary: Rice Madrigals is a small, audition-based, choir dedicated to the performance of madrigal and classical choir pieces. Auditions are open to all in the fall semester.

name: Visual Artists at Rice
description: VAAR (Visual Artists at Rice) is a creative community dedicated to connecting artistic minds across all disciplines at Rice University. Open to everyone regardless of experience, VAAR offers a welcoming space for anyone who wants to create. Whether working on an art homework piece, chatting, or simply doodling, you are encouraged to join. We foster artistic expression in all forms through weekly studio hours in the new art studios at Sarofim Hall, museum visits, painting lessons, and other creative activities. To further fulfill our mission, we organize student exhibitions that showcase artwork created by members of the Rice community, providing opportunities for students to share their work and network with fellow creatives at Rice and in the Houston area. At VAAR, we believe art is present in every field (including but not limited to science, mathematics, and business), and everyone has some artistic spark just waiting to be ignited.
summary: VAAR (Visual Artists at Rice) is a creative community open to all students, offering weekly studio hours in Sarofim Hall. VAAR fosters a relaxed, welcoming space for anyone interested in art. Paint, draw, hang out, and create in whatever way feels right.

name: Open Hearts
description: We work with local Houston schools to provide accessible arts and crafts activities for students of all ages with developmental delays! Come join us if you are passionate about the arts, service, teaching, or community service :)
summary: We work with local Houston schools to provide accessible arts and crafts activities for students of all ages with developmental delays! Come join us if you are passionate about the arts, service, teaching, or community service :)

name: Accessibility Buddies
description: Accessibility Buddies aims to create a more accessible and inclusive community for individuals with disabilities through peer support groups, advocacy initiatives, and collaboration with campus resources. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging and empowerment for students who engage with the Student Disability Resource Center (SDRC) and beyond.
summary: Accessibility Buddies aims to create a more accessible and inclusive community for individuals with disabilities through support groups and advocacy.

name: Rice SYNergy
summary: Rice SYNergy is a student-led organization that intends to build a supportive community for Rice undergraduate researchers and serve as a forum and foundation for undergraduate research opportunities, ideas, resources and competition teams at Rice.

name: Rice Critical Humanities Collective
shortName: Rice CHC, CHC, Rice Critical Humanities Club
description: The Rice Critical Humanities Collective was founded by Rice undergrads and for Rice undergrads. Our mission is to provide an accessible space for any and all undergraduates to analyze, question, and discuss the social, cultural, and political issues shaping this current moment. Through lunch meetings roughly every few weeks, we hope to think together about how culture, power, and technology influence our world.  We want to foster a critical and humanistic approach towards these topics in a way that challenges conventional understandings of them, but our primary goal is to maintain a cross-disciplinary and accessible culture. This is to offer students from all backgrounds the opportunity to think critically about current issues.
summary: A space for Rice undergrads to come together and reflect on contemporary culture, power, and social life through a critical lens, challenging conventional understandings.

name: Rice Undergraduate Law Review
description: The Rice Undergraduate Law Review strives to be the hub of undergraduate legal scholarship and publication for Rice University, the legal community, and the broader public. We aim to engage global audiences in discourse about law and public policy.
summary: The Rice Undergraduate Law Review strives to be the hub of undergraduate legal scholarship and publication for Rice University, the legal community, and the broader public.

name: Stock the Nest
description: As members of Stock the Nest/Swipe Out Hunger, we believe in addressing food insecurity both on the Rice University campus and in the greater Houston community in order to promote health and equity universally. We aim to support local campus initiatives such as the Rice Food Pantry while simultaneously raising awareness and support for community food access through organizational partnerships. As a partner of Swipe Out Hunger, we intend to relate our campus mission to the overarching mission of the parent organization.
summary: With Stock the Nest, we aim to address food insecurity at Rice University and in the greater Houston community. We support the Rice Food Pantry and raise awareness for community food access through organizational partnerships, such as Houston Food Bank.

name: Rice Arm Wrestling Club
description:
The Rice Arm Wrestling Club (RAW Club) is a welcoming, technique-first community for all sizes, strengths, and experience levels. We teach safe, skill-based arm wrestling, build functional strength through weekly practices, and foster camaraderie through supportive coaching and friendly matches. “RAW” reflects our values—Respect, Accessibility, and Wellness—as well as the raw power and discipline the sport develops.

No prior experience required and no dues. Expect structured warm-ups, fundamentals (hand control, posting, pronation/supination), injury-aware training, and optional mock brackets. We host socials, intro clinics, and occasional joint practices with nearby universities. At least one officer is present at every practice to ensure a safe, inclusive environment. Join us to learn proper technique, grow stronger, and be part of a fun, encouraging community—whether you’re curious to try your first pull or eager to train more seriously.
summary: Rice Arm Wrestling Club (RAW Club): inclusive, technique-focused community for all sizes/levels. We teach safe, skill-based armwrestling, build strength with weekly practices, and host matches plus occasional joint events with nearby universities.

name: Rice Women in Physics
description: Rice Women in Physics includes members at all academic levels in physics and related departments, from undergraduates to faculty, and hosts monthly social and networking events. The organization is open to all who support its mission, with a special focus on fostering community and opportunities for women in physics.
summary: Rice Women in Physics hosts members at all levels in physics and related fields, from undergraduates to faculty at monthly social and networking events. Open to all who support its mission, fostering community and opportunities for women in physics.

name: AKOMA
description: Akoma is Rice University's one and only Afro-dance team. Akoma is a Ghanaian Adinkra symbol that translates to "the heart" it symbolizes love, endurance, unity, goodwill, and patience. Our mission is to bring joy to Rice and the greater Houston community through the vibrant art of African dance. We are committed to building a supportive team of confident, inspired dancers who share a love for music and movement. Embracing the meaning of the Adinkra symbol Akoma, we unite as one heart. Our team performs at events like SOAR, VISION, and Africayé.
summary: Akoma is Rice University's one and only Afro-dance team! Our mission is to bring joy to Rice and the greater Houston community through the vibrant art of African dance. We are committed to building a team of confident, inspired dancers.

name: Russian Speakers Association
description: RSA’s mission is to create a space where Russian-speaking students can come together to practice their language and culture, while also welcoming all members of the Rice community who are interested in learning about and engaging with Russian language and culture.
summary: RSA’s mission is to create a space for Russian-speaking students to practice language and culture while welcoming all Rice community members interested in learning and engaging with Russian language and culture.

name: Rice Furries
description: The furry fandom is a subculture interested in anthropomorphic animals. Its origins date back to at least the 1980s in science fiction and comic conventions. Due to the diversity within the fandom, as well as fictional portrayals of the fandom based heavily on stereotypes, a certain amount of stigma and controversy is unfortunately associated with the fandom. Rice Furries hopes to serve and portray the furry population on campus positively, as well as foster community collaboration between furries at Rice with other interest-based or academic groups. This organization is meant to provide a safe space for students who are furries or are interested in becoming furries, as well as a creative space focused on furry art and cosplay within the community.
summary: Rice Furries aims to serve the interests of fans of anthropomorphic animals through education on topics related to and community building centered around the furry fandom.

name: Serenade Seraphim
description: A worship music club dedicated to glorifying God through song and creative performances. We strive to provide a welcoming, low-pressure, and safe space where students of all backgrounds and skill levels can come together to sing contemporary worship music songs, Marian hymns, and faith-inspired musical medleys. Through practices, performances, and community events, we hope to deepen faith, strengthen fellowship, and bring peace and joy to our campus community.
summary: A worship music club bringing faith to life through song! We sing contemporary worship, Marian hymns, and creative medleys, with performances and community events that glorify God, deepen faith, and spread peace on campus.

name: Rice Corporate Law Society
description:
Unsure about your future path but interested in law, business, or policy? The Rice Corporate Law Society helps students at every stage explore opportunities, gain experience, and build connections.

 

The Rice Corporate Law Society (RCLS) cultivates a supportive community for students interested in business, law, and public policy. We provide a multifaceted program that includes resources, workshops, speaker events, networking mixers, case competitions, and hands-on simulations to build practical skills and career readiness. RCLS connects members to alumni, practicing attorneys, and faculty mentors, supports both law-school preparation and business-career exploration, and welcomes students at every stage — from those who are simply curious to those pursuing legal careers. Our goal is to bridge classroom learning and professional practice while fostering ethical leadership and meaningful professional relationships.
summary: The Rice Corporate Law Society provides resources, interactive events, competitions, workshops, and networking opportunities for students interested in corporate law, business law, and related careers at the intersection of law, business, and policy.

name: Science and Technology Studies Minor
description: Science and Technology Studies is a joint program between Rice’s School of Humanities and School of Social Sciences that examines science and technology within their social, political, cultural and historical contexts. We use the interdisciplinary methodologies of the humanities and social sciences to study how science and technology come to be and impact the world around us.
summary: Science and Technology Studies is a joint program between Rice’s School of Humanities and School of Social Sciences that examines science and technology within their social, political, cultural and historical contexts.

name: Thai Student Association
description: The Thai Student Association at Rice University fosters cultural exchange and community through events, food, and traditions. We welcome all students to explore Thai heritage, build friendships, and celebrate diversity on campus.
summary: The Thai Student Association at Rice University fosters cultural exchange and community through events, food, and traditions. We welcome all students to explore Thai heritage, build friendships, and celebrate diversity on campus.

name: Sikh Students Association
summary: The Sikh Student Association is a community for students to celebrate and share Sikh culture, faith, and values on campus. We host events that promote friendship, service (seva), and learning, while creating space for interfaith dialogue.

name: Rice Zine Fest
description: We aim to create a platform for creatives, with the hope of encouraging dialogue and community engagement, supporting DIY culture and highlighting marginalized voices. RZF’s organizers believe in the power of art to reach people in different walks of life, and building a more connected, compassionate and dynamic community. Our main event includes a Zine Fest that showcases student-made zines and brings together creatives at Rice to celebrate the community, zine-making, and student expression.
summary: We aim to create a platform for creatives, with the hope of encouraging dialogue and community engagement, supporting DIY culture and highlighting marginalized voices. Come to Zine Fest!

name: Rice Powerlifting Team
description: Rice Powerlifting is a student-run collegiate club dedicated to the sport of powerlifting. We aim to foster a welcoming and empowering environment that promotes physical strength, mental discipline, and camaraderie among members. Open to athletes of all skill levels, from beginners to national-level competitors. Rice Powerlifting emphasizes proper lifting techniques, safety, and consistent development. As a recognized chapter of USA Powerlifting, the club provides coaching, mentorship, and resources for students interested in recreational training or competing against other universities. We are committed to exemplifying sportsmanship, integrity, and personal growth both on and off the platform.
summary: Rice Powerlifting provides students the opportunity to engage in the sport of powerlifting in the USAPL. Open to lifters of all experience levels, the club fosters a supportive and empowering community, promoting strength, discipline, and camaraderie.

name: Blacksmithing at Rice
summary: Rice's premier and only blacksmithing organization is committed to introducing the Rice community to blacksmithing through workshops and demonstrations!

name: Rice Composers Collective
description:
The Rice Composers Collective is a campus-wide community for composers or anyone interested in the compositional process. We welcome students from all backgrounds, styles, and levels of experience—whether you’re writing your first piece, experimenting with electronics, or developing a personal compositional voice.

Our mission is to foster curiosity and excitement around contemporary music at Rice and throughout the Houston community. We create opportunities for students to share original work, collaborate with performers and artists across disciplines, and hear their music brought to life through high-quality performances and recordings. Through partnerships on and off campus, we aim to build an inclusive, supportive space where new ideas can grow and creative risks are encouraged.
summary: An organization that hosts concerts and events for undergraduate and graduate students, both inside and outside of the Shepherd School of Music.

name: Rice Alternative Dispute Resolution
description:
Who are we:

A student organization focused on exploring and practicing alternative dispute resolution (ADR) methods such as mediation, negotiation, and arbitration, with opportunities to compete in the INADR Undergraduate Mediation Tournament as well as engage in broader educational and practical activities.

Area:
Intersection of law and business, diplomacy, and psychology; conflict and dispute resolution; communication and persuasion.

Activities:

  • Introductory workshops on mediation principles, negotiation tactics, and conflict resolution theory in a legal context
  • Case study discussions analyzing successful and unsuccessful dispute resolution efforts in corporate, legal, and diplomatic contexts
  • Collaborations to explore mediation techniques in business consulting and negotiation settings
  • Guest speakers from mediation professionals, legal practitioners, and HR experts
  • Simulation exercises with rotating roles (mediator, advocate, client) to practice skills in a low-pressure environment

Competitive element:

  • Preparation and participation in the INADR Undergraduate Mediation Tournament for interested members

Major Goal:
To cultivate practical ADR skills and understanding across Rice’s student body, culminating in a well-prepared team representing Rice in the INADR mediation competition and promoting conflict resolution as a valuable professional tool.
summary: A student organization focused on exploring and practicing alternative dispute resolution (ADR) such as mediation, negotiation, and arbitration, with opportunities to compete in undergraduate mediation tournaments.

name: Rice PsychOWLogy Club
description: Rice PsychOWLogy Club is a student-run organization dedicated to fostering curiosity, scholarship, and community among students interested in psychology. We provide a welcoming space for majors as well as any Rice students interested in the study of mind and behavior to connect through academic and social support. Regular programming includes faculty/professional panels, peer-led study sessions, and social events that allow students to meet one another as well as faculty members. The Club partners with the Psychology department to advertise opportunities made available to the department and help members explore graduate and career pathways.
summary: Rice PsychOWLogy Club brings together Rice students to explore psychological science through academic, professional, and social support in an inclusive, student-led environment that connects members with faculty and local partners.

name: Skin Science and Cancer Awareness at Rice
summary: SSCAR aims to increase awareness of skin cancer and dermatological conditions through education, advocacy and outreach. We will empower people to make informed choices about skin care and sun safety while advocating for accessible dermatologic resources.

name: Neuroengineering Student Association
summary: The NESA is established to primarily represent and support the interests of the students from all Rice Neuroengineering Initiative affiliate Labs, and secondarily of all Rice University students of the Neuroengineering disciplines.

name: Rice Economic Forum
description: The Rice Economic Forum (REF) is a student organization focused on fostering interest and engagement in economics through a wide range of academic and professional activities. The club creates opportunities for students to explore economic topics by organizing events, facilitating discussions, and supporting research initiatives. It serves as a platform for collaboration between students and faculty, encouraging dialogue on economic issues that span local, national, and international contexts. Members are invited to participate in workshops, speaker series, and networking events that connect them with professionals, alumni, and peers who share similar interests. The Forum also promotes undergraduate and graduate research in economics, commerce, and trade, and works to build partnerships with organizations both within and beyond the university to support student development. Through these efforts, the club aims to cultivate a vibrant community where students can deepen their understanding of economics and build lasting connections.
summary: An institution dedicated to the advancement of the study of Economics at Rice University.

name: Rice Formula SAE
summary: Rice Formula SAE is a student-run engineering team that designs, builds, and competes with a Formula-style race car in the international Formula SAE Series. The club fosters hands-on design, manufacturing, teamwork, and professional development.

name: Rice Undergraduate Archaeological Society
description: RUAS provides a space for archaeology and anthropology majors and individuals interested in the field to explore their interest, and find community. Furthermore RUAS intends to positively represent the field of archaeology to the Rice community and the public through community archaeology, outreach, and events, and to provide a mentorship space for underclassmen to familiarize themselves with the major and the field.
summary: RUAS provides a space for archaeology majors and those interested in the field to learn, connect with similarly interested individuals, and engage with the Houston community through fun archaeology activities.

name: Rice Satellite Club
description:
The Rice Satellite Club’s mission is to promote a collaborative, hands-on learning environment for the development of satellite-related technologies. The club will focus on everything from software, hardware, and communications design to more theoretical pursuits. Members will meet in sub-teams, each led by a different sub-team lead, and work with supervisors to determine club goals.

If you are interested, please join our discord: https://discord.gg/gPvbakj2
summary: A club focused on learning about and working with satellite-related technologies

name: Voices for Health
description:
We are an organization dedicated to mobilizing college students who speak Mandarin, Spanish, or both to make a meaningful impact in local healthcare. Members partner with under-resourced hospitals to serve as language interpreters, helping bridge communication gaps between patients and medical staff.

To achieve this, our club provides training in medical terminology, cultural sensitivity, and effective communication strategies to ensure that volunteers are prepared to support patients in a professional and compassionate manner. Members are paired with hospital staff to assist in patient intake, translation during consultations, and guidance on navigating health insurance and other healthcare resources. We also organize workshops, peer mentorship, and reflection sessions to help students share experiences, learn from challenges, and continually improve their skills.

By volunteering with us, students gain valuable real-world experience in healthcare, develop cross-cultural communication skills, and make a tangible difference in the lives of patients, helping improve access to care and overall health outcomes in our community.
summary: We are an organization that mobilizes college students who speak Mandarin, Spanish, or both to serve at local under-resourced hospitals as language interpreters, helping patients communicate effectively and access quality care.

name: Rice Athletes in Finance
description: The purpose of Rice Athletes in Finance (RAIF) is to recruit, educate, and empower Rice student-athletes in pursuing careers within the finance industry. The organization aims to bridge the gap between athletics and careers in finance by aiding their journey with education, mentorship, and network facilitation.
summary: Recruiting, educating, and empowering Rice student-athletes in pursuing careers within the finance industry. We aim to bridge the gap between members and careers in finance by aiding their journey with education, mentorship, and network facilitation.

name: Rice Medical Funding Initiative
description:
The Rice Medical Funding Initiative (RMFI) is a student-led organization dedicated to supporting underfunded health and emergency service nonprofits by connecting them with vital grant funding. 
Many community-based organizations, such as volunteer fire departments and rural health clinics, face significant financial barriers to sustaining operations and investing in life-saving equipment. Traditional grant writing services are prohibitively expensive, often costing up to 40% of a grant's value, and many organizations lack the administrative capacity to pursue competitive funding opportunities. RMFI addresses this gap by mobilizing Rice students to provide accessible, high-quality grant writing and funding support at no cost to our partners.

Through community partnerships, financial analysis, and tailored grant applications, RMFI helps organizations unlock essential resources, while offering student hands-on experience in leadership, civic engagement, and professional development. Members engage in training workshops on grant writing, nonprofit finance, and community outreach while working in small student-led teams alongside partner organizations.

Last year, our pilot project raised $1.8K in funding for our community partner and helped them fund critical needs.

RMFI equips students to make a tangible impact beyond the hedges of Rice, while strengthening the resilience of Houston-area health and emergency service providers. Our mission is to alleviate financial burdens for nonprofits at the intersection of healthcare and community.
summary: The Rice Medical Funding Initiative empowers students to support local health and emergency service nonprofits by providing affordable, high-quality grant writing, unlocking vital funding while developing leadership and professional skills.

name: Rice Psychology Student Journal
description: Rice Psychology Student Journal aims to highlight and showcase student work in the field of psychology. Students dedicate countless hours to research, learning, and innovation. RPSJ serves as an opportunity for all Rice University students to collaborate, develop, and publish their work. RPSJ inspires and uplifts student research, ideas, and findings.
summary: RPSJ is an undergraduate publication dedicated to psychology.

name: Children at Risk at Rice
description:
Children at Risk (C@R) is a non-profit organization that has been dedicated to improving the lives of children and families throughout Texas through comprehensive research, data-driven advocacy, and evidence-based policy recommendations. The Rice chapter will focus on critical issues affecting young people, including early education, safety and opportunity, and health. Through rigorous analysis and collaborative partnerships, C&R works to create systemic change that ensures all children have the opportunity to thrive.

We hope to create an environment where students can engage in meaningful research on issues affecting children and families in the Houston area and throughout Texas, develop practical advocacy skills through hands-on experience with policy analysis and community outreach, and raise awareness about critical issues facing vulnerable youth in our communities.
summary: Children at Risk at Rice is a student-led chapter of the Texas nonprofit focused on child advocacy. We conduct health policy research, create policy briefs, and engage in community outreach to improve the well-being of children across Texas.

name: Central Asian Students Association
description: The Central Asian Students Association (CASA) is dedicated to uniting students who share an interest in the diverse cultures, traditions, and languages of Central Asia. Through cultural events, educational discussions, and collaborations with the wider Rice community, CASA seeks to celebrate heritage, promote cross-cultural understanding, and empower students to represent Central Asia’s vibrant identity on campus.
summary: CASA unites students passionate about Central Asian culture through community, celebration, and dialogue, fostering cultural exchange and global understanding at Rice University.

name: The (Real) Stall Talks
description:
The (Real) Stall Talks

 

The (Real) Stall Talks is a supportive, stigma-free community where Rice students can talk openly about the less glamorous sides of college life. This ranges from bathroom anxiety to hygiene, self-care, and body image. We believe humor and honesty are powerful tools for building community and promoting wellness. Our mission is to destigmatize health and hygiene conversations, provide practical peer-to-peer advice, and foster a culture of compassion, confidence, and self-sufficiency among students.

 

Starting college is overwhelming. Students often face questions and insecurities that nobody prepared them for: where’s the best bathroom on campus (It’s too awkward to poop while my suitemates are here!)? How do you shave without razor bumps (Online tutorials don’t really work!)? What actually works for acne or stress sweat (I’m too broke for a dermatologist!)? How do you bring up awkward topics with suitemates?

 

The (Real) Stall Talks fills a unique gap at Rice. While there is the Student Health Center and RHS, there isn’t yet a peer community where students can have honest, specific conversations about everyday body and life struggles. There are questions that online forums and YouTube videos can’t answer completely. That’s where we come in.

 

Our group creates a supportive space where students can:

  • Destigmatize the awkward but real parts of self-care, from bathroom anxiety to body image.
  • Share practical, real-life advice that is not generic online tutorials, but tailored peer-to-peer experiences of what actually works.
  • Build confidence and community through humor, honesty, and compassion.

Activities We’ll Run

  • Rice Bathroom & Self-Care Survival Guide: a crowdsourced handbook with student tips.
  • “Where’s the Best Bathroom on Campus?” Map: fun, useful, and community-built.
  • Discussion Circles: safe, judgment-free spaces to talk about taboo or awkward topics.
  • Workshops: guest speakers like campus wellness staff or dermatologists.
  • Resource Swaps: students sharing what products and routines actually work for them.
  • “Stuff Nobody Taught Us” Sessions: how to unclog a drain, laundry hacks, affordable self-care basics.

By blending humor and honesty, The (Real) Stall Talks makes space for conversations that students need but rarely get to have. We’ll help normalize self-care struggles, promote both physical and mental wellness, and create a strong peer-support network that enhances life at Rice.
summary: The (Real) Stall Talks is a supportive, stigma-free community where Rice students can talk openly about the less glamorous sides of college life. This ranges from bathroom anxiety to hygiene, self-care, and body image.

name: Rice for Leukemia & Lymphoma Society
description: Through fundraising campaigns, awareness events, and community engagement, we aim to mobilize the student body in the fight against blood cancers. Whether through hosting charity runs, organizing speaker panels with survivors and researchers, or partnering with local businesses for donation drives, our goal is to empower students to become advocates for change. Together, we strive to raise critical funds for research and patient support while educating our campus on the impact of blood cancers and how we can all make a difference.
summary: A club supporting the Leukemia & Lymphoma Society by raising funds, spreading awareness, and engaging our campus in the fight against blood cancers through events, outreach, and advocacy to improve the quality of life for patients and their families.

name: SOLIDWORKS User Group
description:
The Rice University SOLIDWORKS User Group (SWUG) is a student-led organization dedicated to equipping Rice engineers with the technical and professional skills needed to thrive in industry. Our mission is to ensure that every Rice engineering student has access to high-quality training in SOLIDWORKS, one of the most widely used 3D CAD platforms, while also fostering mentorship connections with practicing engineers in Houston and beyond.

SWUG hosts workshops, information sessions, and hands-on projects that teach students how to design, model, and apply SOLIDWORKS to real-world challenges. Members gain practical experience through community-focused design projects and opportunities to compete in national and international competitions such as the AAKRUTI Innovation Competition.

By bridging classroom learning with professional practice, SWUG provides a collaborative environment where students can grow as designers, innovators, and future engineers while contributing to the Rice community.
summary: The Rice University SOLIDWORKS User Group (SWUG) is a student-led organization dedicated to equipping Rice engineers with the technical and professional skills needed to thrive in industry. Our mission is to ensure that every Rice engineering student has

name: Society of Plastics Engineers @ Rice
description: Society of Plastic Engineers (SPE) is a polymers and plastics organization with dozens of student chapters across the globe. This SPE chapter offers engineering students the opportunity to learn from experts in the industry and academia, discover new opportunities in the area of plastics, and apply their knowledge and networking skills in real life settings.
summary: Society of Plastics Engineers at Rice connects students with industry and academic experts, offering opportunities to explore careers in plastics and polymers, gain hands-on experience, and build skills through events, networking, and mentorship.

name: Butterfly Books
description:
Butterfly Books™ is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to providing literature to socioeconomically disadvantaged children and children with medical needs through read aloud events at schools, community centers, and hospitals; book bundle donations; and Little Library instillations. To date, we have reached more than 30,000 children and donated over 9,000 books.

 

More information can be found here: www.butterflybooks.org
summary: Butterfly Books™ is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to providing literature to socioeconomically disadvantaged children and children with medical needs through read aloud events and book bundle donations.

name: Rice Reformed University Fellowship
summary: Rice RUF is a student organization that seeks to share the light and life of Jesus with the larger campus community. This is a place for any student, regardless of background or beliefs to come and consider & discuss the claims of the Bible

name: Rice Pre-Veterinary Society
description: The Rice Pre-Vet Society (RPVS) provides resources and community for students interested in veterinary medicine by organizing opportunities and events to help members gain experience in the veterinary field. Such opportunities include group volunteering at shelters and/or animal care events, meeting with various veterinarians to learn about their perspectives on the field, and coordinating trips to open houses hosted by veterinary schools.
summary: The Rice Pre-Vet Society provides resources and community for students interested in veterinary medicine by organizing opportunities and events to help members gain experience in the veterinary field.

name: Rice Federalist Society
description: The Rice Federalist Society will be a student-led chapter of the Federalist Society, a group of libertarian and conservative lawyers and legal thinkers. At Rice, the chapter will put on debates each semester regarding interesting and timely legal topics, relevant to our students. We believe that in order to best formulate one's stance on legal and political matters, we must compare and contrast very different viewpoints, and we will thus try to choose speakers from different walks of life. Logistically speaking, the Rice chapter will receive funding from the National Organization, and for each debate we will thus be able to fly in one speaker to talk (likely a judge, legal scholar, or lawyer), and we will also invite a local speaker to participate too (possibly a Rice professor or Houston-based lawyer.).
summary: The Rice Federalist Society is a student-led chapter dedicated to promoting respectful, robust debate, regarding legal issues. We put on debates each semester about interesting current topics in the legal world and find important hearing all viewpoints.

name: Houston StuMo
description:
The purposes of this organization shall be: 

  1. To encourage students in spiritual growth through Bible study, prayer, and discipleship.
  2. To provide opportunities for Christian fellowship and community on campus.
  3. To develop leadership skills rooted in Christian character.
  4. To engage in campus outreach and service to the Rice community.
  5. To connect students with the broader mission of Student Mobilization (StuMo) and partner with local ministries for mutual encouragement and service.
summary: To empower and equip students to grow in Christian faith, character, and leadership.

name: NAACP at Rice University
description: The National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP) is dedicated to upholding the rights enshrined in the 13th, 14th, and 15th Amendments to the United States Constitution. These amendments ensure an end to slavery, equal protection under the law, and the right to vote, respectively. The NAACP's mission is to promote the political, educational, and equality rights of all citizens, with a special focus on the rights of minority citizens, and to eradicate racial prejudice through democratic means. Over the years, the NAACP has played a pivotal role in advancing equity with a special interest in the African American community in the United States. Reestablishing this NAACP chapter can further their mission and contribute significantly to promoting access here at Rice. Reviving the NAACP chapter at Rice not only aligns with the university's mission but also has the potential to play a crucial role in shaping an inclusive experience within the community. The organization is open to all who support its mission.
summary: The NAACP promotes political, educational, and equality rights for all citizens, with a focus on minority communities, the eradication of racial prejudice, access at Rice, and fostering inclusion on campus. Open to all who share this mission.

name: Streams of Joy Campus Fellowship at Rice University
summary: Streams of Joy Campus Fellowship is a Christian student group dedicated to promoting spiritual growth, Christian fellowship, academic excellence, and leadership development among students at Rice University

name: AWS Cloud Club at Rice University
description: The student-led Cloud Club introduces peers at Rice and in the Greater Houston Area to cloud computing technologies and its applications in security, AI, analytics, and business. Through hands-on projects, industry tech talks, members gain both technical and business expertise, building industry-relevant skills in cloud computing that are valuable across all majors.
summary: The mission of this student-led Cloud Club is to teach peers from the Rice community and the Greater Houston Area about the AWS Cloud and its various use cases, including those related to security, AI, business analytics, business transformation, etc.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha - Alpha Epsilon Pi
description:
Today, Pi Sigma Alpha hosts chapters on nearly 850 campuses and has inducted more than 300,000 members. Its rolls feature national, state, and local political leaders including one president, three Supreme Court justices, and dozens of members of Congress. Through these 100 years there have been many changes but neither the mission of the organization or the quality of the individuals inducted has changed. Each year those who have achieved academic excellence are inducted and display the same passion for politics and policy. Like those who first joined, today’s student strives to uphold the purpose of Pi Sigma Alpha first described in 1919: “to encourage the scientific and practical study of problems of government, to foster reforms in our governmental machinery, and to aid in the education of the electorate in problems of government."

Pi Sigma Alpha, the National Political Science Honor Society, was founded in 1920 at the University of Texas, Austin and is today headquartered in Washington, DC. Rice University chartered the Alpha Epsilon Pi chapter in 2008 and has initiated over 130 members since then.

Pi Sigma Alpha has several goals:

  • To recognize and promote excellence in academic achievement for political science, government, inter national and public affairs
  • To stimulate scholarship and interest in political science
  • To promote curricular and extracurricular activities related to political science
  • To promote civil dialogue

New members are inducted into Pi Sigma Alpha every spring (March-April). Membership in the Alpha Epsilon Pi chapter requires high academic achievement.

The chapter organizes activities related to political science scholarship and politics throughout the year that are open to members and non-members. Chapter members have the opportunity to participate in a national undergraduate conference and submit papers to the Pi Sigma Alpha undergraduate journal. Members are also eligible for internship scholarships, graduate school scholarships, and awards through the national organization. Deadlines are in May and June of each year.

For more information on the national Pi Sigma Alpha organization, go to www.pisigmaalpha.org
summary: Alpha Epsilon Pi is Rice's chapter of Pi Sigma Alpha, the national political science honor society. Pi Sigma Alpha aims recognize academic excellence by, and provide leadership opportunities for, undergraduate and graduate students in political science.

name: Project Sunshine Rice University
description:
To deliver the healing power of play to children with medical needs through passionate and dedicated volunteers.

We organize Here to Play, TelePlay, and Kits for Play programs, offer monthly volunteer opportunities to lead activities for inpatients, and raise funds to spread joy through art, games, and companionship. Founded by a former recipient of a Project Sunshine Play Kit during her own hospitalization at Children’s Colorado, our chapter brings that same spirit of comfort and creativity to pediatric patients on campus and beyond with partnerships at Texas Children's and Ronald McDonald House.
summary: To deliver the healing power of play to children with medical needs through passionate and dedicated volunteers.

name: American Society of Artificial Internal Organs at Rice
description:
Check out the national website here: https://asaio.org/ 

If you are interested, please reach out to anarkat@rice.edu, saahith.garg@rice.edu, or satvika.nadella@rice.edu
summary: ASAIO @ Rice connects students to medical device innovation through research, clinical exposure, and collaboration with the Texas Medical Center—advancing careers in artificial organ development and life-saving technology.

name: MEDLIFE
summary: MEDLIFE is a volunteer-led global health organization that empowers families and communities in South America, Africa, and other underserved regions to overcome poverty and improve their quality of life. We want to bring equality and education to life.

name: Rice Healthcare Consulting
description: Rice Healthcare Consulting (RHC) is a pre-professional student organization dedicated to bridging the worlds of healthcare and business. Our mission is to introduce Rice undergraduates to the dynamic field of healthcare consulting—where data-driven problem solving meets the pursuit of improved patient outcomes. Through partnerships with hospitals, nonprofits, government agencies, and consulting firms, RHC provides students with hands-on experiences that develop their analytical, strategic, and operational skills. Members learn to evaluate healthcare systems, identify inefficiencies, recommend actionable solutions, and support compliance and innovation efforts. By preparing students to think critically about healthcare delivery and policy, RHC empowers future leaders to make meaningful impacts across clinical, corporate, and global health settings. In a campus environment rich with pre-med students and growing interest in the healthcare-business intersection, RHC fulfills a unique and essential niche at Rice University.
summary: Rice Healthcare Consulting (RHC) is a pre-professional student organization dedicated to bridging the worlds of healthcare and business through hands-on experiences with hospitals, nonprofits, government agencies, and consulting firms.

name: MOVE Mountains
description: As members of the Rice community, we believe that by working together we can combat the systemic shortcomings surrounding physical and mental health care for the disabled, an underserved population which often intersects with these grossly overlooked public health needs. We are fulfilling this goal by going out into the Houston community and working directly with disability-serving organizations to support or initiate exercise and mental well-being programs, as well as educating the Rice community about these issues and mobilizing campus members as civic volunteers and leaders. This involves training Rice students as social and mental support partners before partnering them with disabled athletes during exercise sessions, pioneering a combined approach to physical, socioemotional, and mental health. Our events on campus are more awareness-oriented, and through them we aim to develop a more informed student body. We also hope to grow Rice students as leaders through the various shift leader and project ideator positions we will create, allowing them to not just be part of the volunteer team but the development team which will spearhead new initiatives and grow our club in new directions.
summary: We’re bringing physical and mental healthcare to Houston’s disabled, combatting public health disparity! Get mental health training, volunteer with us at adaptive athletics events (no ability needed!), and explore our community leadership opportunities!

name: Gift of Life at Rice
description: Gift of Life Marrow Registry is a nonprofit that cures blood cancer and inherited immune disorders through bone marrow and stem cell transplants. Since its founding, the registry has recruited over 381,000 potential donors and facilitated nearly 4,000 lifesaving transplants.
summary: Gift of Life Marrow Registry is a nonprofit that cures blood cancer and inherited immune disorders through bone marrow and stem cell transplants.

name: Cultural Nutrition Initiative
description: The Cultural Nutrition Initiative (CNI) at Rice promotes healthy eating patterns in underserved communities in Houston by providing culturally sensitive nutrition education to middle school students. Obesity and weight-related diseases rank as the leading causes of mortality in the United States, and one of the principal reasons behind this public health epidemic is poor nutrition. This issue disproportionately affects low-income communities and racial minorities due to less resources and unique familial and work demands. Despite this, there have been little efforts to effectively address this gap as socioeconomic and cultural differences are not usually factored into public health program design, and the skills taught don't translate well to actual behavioral change. The aim of this program is to contribute to resolving chronic disease disparities by providing tailored nutrition education to young students through our 4-day workshop series, where students learn the basics of nutrition science and how to cook cheap, low-ingredient, quick recipes influenced by Hispanic and Soul cuisines.
summary: To provide culturally sensitive and socioeconomically appropriate nutrition education to underserved middle school students by teaching them about the basics of nutrition science and how to cook low-ingredient, quick, and nutritious cultural recipes

name: Rice Classic Literature Club
description: Rice Classic Literature Club is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering community among students who enjoy engaging with classic literary works. The club meets biweekly to discuss selected texts from the literary canon, emphasizing close reading, open dialogue, and shared interpretation in a welcoming, low-pressure environment. No prior coursework or experience is required, and membership is open to all Rice students with an interest in literature.
summary: Rice Classic Literature Club is a student-led organization that meets biweekly to discuss classic literary works. The club fosters community among students who enjoy close reading, thoughtful discussion, and shared appreciation of classic texts.

name: Global Shapers - Rice Chapter
summary: A student-led chapter of the Global Shapers Houston Hub, empowering Rice students to lead community impact projects, foster leadership, and connect with a global network of changemakers.

name: Claude Builder Club @ Rice University
description: The Claude Builder Club is an inclusive community for all students interested in exploring AI development, developing technical skills, and learning about the benefits and risks associated with AI. CBC is a space for passionate students to build their own projects while connecting with peers who can elevate their ideas. CBC members are AI thought leaders on their campus and beyond.
summary: The Claude Builder Club (CBC) at Rice University is an inclusive student community for exploring AI, building projects, and learning together through workshops, hackathons, and events while fostering creativity, collaboration, and ethical innovation.

name: Humanitarian Environmental Relief Organization
summary: H.E.R.O. is a nonprofit organization with a mission to educate and fundraise for charitable organizations that are doing work on the ground in areas of the world facing humanitarian or environmental crises.

name: Czech and Slovak Society
summary: The Czech and Slovak Society aims to build a supportive, professional, and friendly network for Czech and Slovak undergraduate and graduate students.

name: CoreServe Foundation Rice
description:
Sparking hope in the next generation.

The CoreServe Foundation, a 501(c)(3) public charity, is a national organization that provides access to quality education for children in rural areas of Africa, specifically Gambia and Nigeria. The Rice University Chapter is the flagship chapter in the university/college division. The club helps raise awareness, fundraise, and collect material donations for CoreServe in addition to other advocacy and activities on campus.
summary: A 501(c)(3) charitable organization providing access to quality education for children in rural areas of Africa.

name: Pay It Forward
description: Pay It Forward is a student-led organization dedicated to helping high school students navigate the college application process, ensuring that every student—regardless of background—has an equal opportunity to pursue higher education. Our mission is to make college more accessible by connecting students with mentors who provide support in every area, including college essay writing, AP/SAT/ACT preparation, and financial aid guidance, as well as many other aspects of the journey. We believe the college journey shouldn’t be walked alone because success is shared, not kept. Through mentorship and community engagement, we aim to empower students across the Houston area and, ultimately, expand our reach throughout the United States. Together, we inspire students to reach for the stars and give back by helping the next generation do the same.
summary: Pay It Forward is a student-led organization dedicated to helping high school students navigate the college application process, ensuring that every student—regardless of background—has an equal opportunity to pursue higher education.

name: Rice ReUse
summary: Rice ReUse aims to inspire and empower Rice University students to engage in and promote sustainable move-in and move-out practices on campus, and to foster a campus-wide culture of environmental responsibility by rehoming surplus items after move-out.

name: Rice Cooking Club
description: We are a club focused on teaching Rice University students basic and professional cooking skills. From how to shop for food effectively to perfecting any dish you can imagine. We provide free weekly cooking lessons to all members of the club. We are an allergy friendly space, so people with dietary restrictions can still participate. This club is ran by Aaron Gold (insta: ChefAG305
summary: We are a club focused on teaching Rice University students basic and professional cooking skills. From how to shop for food effectively to perfecting any dish you can imagine. We provide free weekly cooking lessons to all members of the club.
